1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"strings"
13	
14		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15		"golang.org/x/term"
16	
17		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
18	
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
26	)
27	
28	var (
29		flagTo           string
30		flagFrom         string
31		flagModel        string
32		flagProvider     string
33		flagEngine       string
34		flagTier         string
35		flagPreset       string
36		flagInstructions string
37		flagPair         bool
38		flagPairWith     string
39		flagJSON         bool
40		flagNoHistory    bool
41	)
42	
43	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
44	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
45		root := &cobra.Command{
46			Use:   "translate [text...]",
47			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
48			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
49				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
50				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
51				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
52			Version:       buildVersion(),
53			SilenceUsage:  true,
54			SilenceErrors: true,
55			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
56			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
57			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
58			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
59			RunE: runRoot,
60		}
61		f := root.PersistentFlags()
62		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
63		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
64		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
65		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
66		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
67		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
68		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
69		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
70		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
72		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
74	
75		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
76		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
77		return root
78	}
79	
80	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
81	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
82		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
83	}
84	
85	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
86	func overrides() config.Overrides {
87		return config.Overrides{
88			Source:       flagFrom,
89			Target:       flagTo,
90			Engine:       flagEngine,
91			Provider:     flagProvider,
92			Model:        flagModel,
93			Tier:         flagTier,
94			Preset:       flagPreset,
95			Instructions: flagInstructions,
96			Pair:         flagPair,
97			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
98		}
99	}
100	
101	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
102	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
103	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
104	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
105		if len(args) > 0 {
106			return config.ModeCLI
107		}
108		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
109			return config.ModeCLI
110		}
111		return config.ModeTUI
112	}
113	
114	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
115		ctx := cmd.Context()
116	
117		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
118		if err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if created {
122			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
123		}
124	
125		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
126		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
127		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
128		pairWith := ""
129		if res.Pair {
130			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
131			pairWith = pwm.Code
132		}
133	
134		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
135			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
136		}
137		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
138		if err != nil {
139			return err
140		}
141	
142		st := openStore(cfg)
143		if st != nil {
144			defer st.Close()
145		}
146	
147		switch {
148		case len(args) > 0:
149			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
150			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
151			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions)
152			if err != nil {
153				return err
154			}
155			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
156			return nil
157	
158		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
159			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
160			if err != nil {
161				return err
162			}
163			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
164			if text == "" {
165				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
166			}
167			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
168			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions)
169			if err != nil {
170				return err
171			}
172			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
173			return nil
174	
175		default:
176			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
177		}
178	}
179	
180	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
181	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
182		if pair && away != "" {
183			return lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
184		}
185		return home
186	}
187	
188	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
189	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
190	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
191		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
192			return
193		}
194		stt, err := state.Load()
195		if err != nil || stt == nil {
196			return
197		}
198		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
199			res.Source = stt.Source
200		}
201		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
202			res.Target = stt.Target
203		}
204	}
205	
206	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
207	// suppressed with --no-history.
208	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
209		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
210			return nil
211		}
212		path := cfg.History.Path
213		if path == "" {
214			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
215		}
216		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
217		if err != nil {
218			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
219			return nil
220		}
221		return st
222	}
223	
224	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
225	//
226	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
227	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
228	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
229	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
230		if st != nil {
231			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
232		}
233		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
234			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
235		}
236	}
237	
238	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
239	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
240		src := source
241		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
242			src = res.DetectedSource
243		}
244		return store.Record{
245			SourceLang:   src,
246			TargetLang:   target,
247			Engine:       res.Engine,
248			Model:        res.Model,
249			Input:        input,
250			Output:       res.Translation,
251			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
252			Notes:        res.Notes,
253		}
254	}
255	
256	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
257	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
258		mode := "fixed"
259		if source == "auto" {
260			mode = "auto"
261		}
262		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
263			Source:     source,
264			Target:     target,
265			SourceMode: mode,
266			Engine:     engineName,
267		})
268	}
269	
270	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
271	// pair on exit.
272	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
273		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
274		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
275		modelProvider := ""
276		if res.Provider != nil {
277			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
278			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
279		}
280		p := tui.Params{
281			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
282			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
283			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
284			Store:         st,
285			Source:        source,
286			Target:        target,
287			Pair:          res.Pair,
288			PairWith:      pairWith,
289			Model:         res.Model,
290			Preset:        res.Preset,
291			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
292			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
293			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
294		}
295		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
296		if err != nil {
297			return err
298		}
299		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
300			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
301				s, t := fm.Pair()
302				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
303			}
304		}
305		return nil
306	}
307	
308	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
309	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
310	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
311		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
312		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
313		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
314			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
315		}
316		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
317			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
318		}
319		return sm.Code, tm.Code
320	}
321	
322	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
323	//
324	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
325	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
326	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
327	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
328		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
329		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
330	
331		req := engine.Request{
332			Text:   text,
333			Source: source,
334			Target: target,
335			Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
336			Stream: stream,
337			Preset: preset,
338			Extra:  instructions,
339		}
340		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
341		if err != nil {
342			return nil, err
343		}
344	
345		var onTok func(string)
346		if stream {
347			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
348		}
349		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
350		if err != nil {
351			if stream {
352				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
353			}
354			return nil, err
355		}
356	
357		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
358		// failed and why, and how to switch.
359		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
360			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
361		}
362		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
363			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
364		}
365	
366		switch {
367		case flagJSON:
368			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
369			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
370			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
371				return nil, err
372			}
373		case stream:
374			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
375		default:
376			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
377		}
378		return res, nil
379	}
380
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	}
37	
38	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
39	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
40	type TranslateResult struct {
41		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
42		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
43		Target         string   `json:"target"`
44		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
45		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
46		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
47	
48		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
49		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
50		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
51	
52		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
53		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
54		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
55		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
56		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
57	
58		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
59		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
60		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
63		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
64		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
65		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
66		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
67		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
68	
69		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
70		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
71		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
72		// Transient: not serialized.
73		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
74	}
75	
76	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
77	type DictEntry struct {
78		Word      string    `json:"word"`
79		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
80		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
81		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
82	}
83	
84	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
85	type Meaning struct {
86		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
87		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
88		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
89		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
90	}
91	
92	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
93	type Definition struct {
94		Text    string `json:"definition"`
95		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
96	}
97	
98	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
99	type ChunkKind int
100	
101	const (
102		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
103		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
104		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
105		ChunkDone
106		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
107		ChunkError
108	)
109	
110	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
111	type Chunk struct {
112		Kind   ChunkKind
113		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
114		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
115		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
116	}
117	
118	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
119	//
120	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
121	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
122	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
123	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
124	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
125	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
126	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
127	type Engine interface {
128		Name() string
129		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
130		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
131		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
132		Supports(m Mode) bool
133	}
134	
135	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
136	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
137	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
138		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
139		if err != nil {
140			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
141		} else {
142			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
143		}
144		close(ch)
145		return ch
146	}
147	
148	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
149	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
150	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
151	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
152		for c := range ch {
153			switch c.Kind {
154			case ChunkToken:
155				if onToken != nil {
156					onToken(c.Text)
157				}
158			case ChunkDone:
159				return c.Result, nil
160			case ChunkError:
161				return nil, c.Err
162			}
163		}
164		return nil, ErrNoResult
165	}
166
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		return nil
64	}
65	
66	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
67	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
68	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
69	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
70		cfg := res.Cfg
71		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
72			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
73		}
74		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
75			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
76		}
77		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
78	}
79	
80	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
81	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
82		d := res.Dictionary
83		if d == nil {
84			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
85				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
86			}
87			return res.Translation + "\n"
88		}
89		var b strings.Builder
90		head := d.Word
91		if d.Phonetic != "" {
92			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
93		}
94		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
95		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
96			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
97			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
98				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
99					break
100				}
101				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
102				if def.Example != "" {
103					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
104				}
105			}
106		}
107		return b.String()
108	}
109

1	package lang
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"unicode"
6	
7		"github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo"
8	)
9	
10	// Detect returns a best-effort ISO-639-1 source-language code for text, or "" if
11	// the guess is not confident. Used to fill DetectedSource on the LLM path (where
12	// the model returns only the translation) and as a fallback when APIs are down.
13	func Detect(text string) string {
14		info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
15		if info.Confidence < 0.5 {
16			return ""
17		}
18		return info.Lang.Iso6391()
19	}
20	
21	// IsChinese reports whether text contains any Han characters — used to route
22	// dictionary lookups (Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT).
23	func IsChinese(text string) bool {
24		for _, r := range text {
25			if unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) {
26				return true
27			}
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	// PairTarget implements bidirectional "pair" mode: if the input is written in
33	// the home language, translate it to away; otherwise translate it to home. This
34	// makes "foreign → my language, my language → foreign" work from one input box
35	// (e.g. home=zh-TW, away=en: Chinese → en, everything else → zh-TW).
36	func PairTarget(home, away, text string) string {
37		if away == "" || away == home {
38			return home
39		}
40		if inLang(text, home) {
41			return away
42		}
43		return home
44	}
45	
46	// inLang reports whether text is (best-effort) in the language of code. Chinese
47	// uses a reliable Han-script check; others fall back to offline detection.
48	func inLang(text, code string) bool {
49		base := baseCode(code)
50		switch base {
51		case "", "auto":
52			return false
53		case "zh":
54			return IsChinese(text)
55		default:
56			d := Detect(text)
57			return d != "" && d == base
58		}
59	}
60	
61	// baseCode strips a region suffix: "zh-TW" → "zh".
62	func baseCode(code string) string {
63		code = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(code))
64		if i := strings.IndexByte(code, '-'); i > 0 {
65			return code[:i]
66		}
67		return code
68	}
69
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20	}
21	
22	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
23	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
24	type Mode int
25	
26	const (
27		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
28		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
29	)
30	
31	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
32	type Resolved struct {
33		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
34		Target       string
35		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
36		Provider     *Provider
37		Model        string
38		Tier         string
39		Preset       string
40		Instructions string
41		Pair         bool
42		PairWith     string
43		Stream       bool
44		Color        string
45	
46		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
47		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
48		LiveTranslate bool
49		DebounceMs    int
50	
51		Cfg *Config
52	}
53	
54	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
55	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
56	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
57	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
58	
59	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
60	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
61		switch mode {
62		case ModeCLI:
63			return c.CLI
64		case ModeTUI:
65			return c.TUI
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
71	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
72	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
73		if ov == nil {
74			return
75		}
76		if ov.Engine != nil {
77			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
78		}
79		if ov.Tier != nil {
80			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
81		}
82		if ov.Preset != nil {
83			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
84		}
85		if ov.Instructions != nil {
86			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
87		}
88		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
89			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
90		}
91		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
92			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
93		}
94		if ov.Pair != nil {
95			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
96		}
97		if ov.PairWith != nil {
98			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
99		}
100		if ov.Stream != nil {
101			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
102		}
103		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
104			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
105		}
106		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
107			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
112	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
113	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
114		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
115		// the on-disk config is untouched).
116		g := c.General
117		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
118		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
119	
120		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
121			if flag != "" {
122				return flag
123			}
124			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
125				return v
126			}
127			return cfgVal
128		}
129	
130		r := Resolved{
131			Cfg:           c,
132			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
133			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
134			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
135			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
136			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
137			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
138			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
139			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
140			Color:         g.Color,
141			Stream:        g.Stream,
142			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
143			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
144		}
145	
146		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
147		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
148		if provName == "" {
149			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
150				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
151			} else {
152				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
153			}
154		}
155		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
156	
157		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
158		// provider's tier model.
159		cfgModel := ""
160		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
161			cfgModel = *ov.Model
162		}
163		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
164		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
165			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
166		}
167		return r
168	}
169	
170	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
171	// else the first configured provider, else "".
172	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
173		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
174			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
175				return name
176			}
177		}
178		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
179			return c.Providers[0].Name
180		}
181		return ""
182	}
183
```

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"strings"
13	
14		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15		"golang.org/x/term"
16	
17		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
18	
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
26	)
27	
28	var (
29		flagTo           string
30		flagFrom         string
31		flagModel        string
32		flagProvider     string
33		flagEngine       string
34		flagTier         string
35		flagPreset       string
36		flagInstructions string
37		flagPair         bool
38		flagPairWith     string
39		flagJSON         bool
40		flagNoHistory    bool
41	)
42	
43	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
44	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
45		root := &cobra.Command{
46			Use:   "translate [text...]",
47			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
48			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
49				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
50				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
51				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
52			Version:       buildVersion(),
53			SilenceUsage:  true,
54			SilenceErrors: true,
55			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
56			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
57			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
58			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
59			RunE: runRoot,
60		}
61		f := root.PersistentFlags()
62		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
63		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
64		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
65		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
66		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
67		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
68		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
69		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
70		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
72		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
74	
75		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
76		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
77		return root
78	}
79	
80	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
81	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
82		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
83	}
84	
85	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
86	func overrides() config.Overrides {
87		return config.Overrides{
88			Source:       flagFrom,
89			Target:       flagTo,
90			Engine:       flagEngine,
91			Provider:     flagProvider,
92			Model:        flagModel,
93			Tier:         flagTier,
94			Preset:       flagPreset,
95			Instructions: flagInstructions,
96			Pair:         flagPair,
97			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
98		}
99	}
100	
101	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
102	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
103	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
104	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
105		if len(args) > 0 {
106			return config.ModeCLI
107		}
108		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
109			return config.ModeCLI
110		}
111		return config.ModeTUI
112	}
113	
114	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
115		ctx := cmd.Context()
116	
117		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
118		if err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if created {
122			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
123		}
124	
125		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
126		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
127		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
128		pairWith := ""
129		if res.Pair {
130			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
131			pairWith = pwm.Code
132		}
133	
134		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
135			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
136		}
137		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
138		if err != nil {
139			return err
140		}
141	
142		st := openStore(cfg)
143		if st != nil {
144			defer st.Close()
145		}
146	
147		switch {
148		case len(args) > 0:
149			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
150			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
151			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions)
152			if err != nil {
153				return err
154			}
155			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
156			return nil
157	
158		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
159			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
160			if err != nil {
161				return err
162			}
163			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
164			if text == "" {
165				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
166			}
167			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
168			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions)
169			if err != nil {
170				return err
171			}
172			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
173			return nil
174	
175		default:
176			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
177		}
178	}
179	
180	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
181	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
182		if pair && away != "" {
183			return lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
184		}
185		return home
186	}
187	
188	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
189	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
190	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
191		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
192			return
193		}
194		stt, err := state.Load()
195		if err != nil || stt == nil {
196			return
197		}
198		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
199			res.Source = stt.Source
200		}
201		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
202			res.Target = stt.Target
203		}
204	}
205	
206	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
207	// suppressed with --no-history.
208	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
209		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
210			return nil
211		}
212		path := cfg.History.Path
213		if path == "" {
214			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
215		}
216		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
217		if err != nil {
218			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
219			return nil
220		}
221		return st
222	}
223	
224	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
225	//
226	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
227	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
228	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
229	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
230		if st != nil {
231			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
232		}
233		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
234			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
235		}
236	}
237	
238	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
239	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
240		src := source
241		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
242			src = res.DetectedSource
243		}
244		return store.Record{
245			SourceLang:   src,
246			TargetLang:   target,
247			Engine:       res.Engine,
248			Model:        res.Model,
249			Input:        input,
250			Output:       res.Translation,
251			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
252			Notes:        res.Notes,
253		}
254	}
255	
256	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
257	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
258		mode := "fixed"
259		if source == "auto" {
260			mode = "auto"
261		}
262		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
263			Source:     source,
264			Target:     target,
265			SourceMode: mode,
266			Engine:     engineName,
267		})
268	}
269	
270	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
271	// pair on exit.
272	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
273		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
274		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
275		modelProvider := ""
276		if res.Provider != nil {
277			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
278			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
279		}
280		p := tui.Params{
281			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
282			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
283			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
284			Store:         st,
285			Source:        source,
286			Target:        target,
287			Pair:          res.Pair,
288			PairWith:      pairWith,
289			Model:         res.Model,
290			Preset:        res.Preset,
291			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
292			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
293			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
294		}
295		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
296		if err != nil {
297			return err
298		}
299		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
300			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
301				s, t := fm.Pair()
302				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
303			}
304		}
305		return nil
306	}
307	
308	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
309	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
310	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
311		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
312		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
313		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
314			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
315		}
316		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
317			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
318		}
319		return sm.Code, tm.Code
320	}
321	
322	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
323	//
324	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
325	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
326	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
327	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
328		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
329		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
330	
331		req := engine.Request{
332			Text:   text,
333			Source: source,
334			Target: target,
335			Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
336			Stream: stream,
337			Preset: preset,
338			Extra:  instructions,
339		}
340		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
341		if err != nil {
342			return nil, err
343		}
344	
345		var onTok func(string)
346		if stream {
347			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
348		}
349		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
350		if err != nil {
351			if stream {
352				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
353			}
354			return nil, err
355		}
356	
357		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
358		// failed and why, and how to switch.
359		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
360			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
361		}
362		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
363			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
364		}
365	
366		switch {
367		case flagJSON:
368			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
369			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
370			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
371				return nil, err
372			}
373		case stream:
374			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
375		default:
376			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
377		}
378		return res, nil
379	}
380

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
10	)
11	
12	// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
13	// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
14	// a named provider builds a single LLM engine. (google/dict engines join the
15	// chain in later slices.)
16	func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
17		cfg := res.Cfg
18	
19		if res.Engine != "auto" {
20			switch res.Engine {
21			case "google":
22				return googleFromConfig(cfg), nil
23			case "llm":
24				// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
25			}
26			if res.Provider != nil {
27				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
28			}
29			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
30		}
31	
32		var engines []engine.Engine
33		for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
34			switch {
35			case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
36				p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
37				engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
38			case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
39				engines = append(engines, googleFromConfig(cfg))
40			}
41			// "dict" is wired in a later slice.
42		}
43		if len(engines) == 0 {
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
45		}
46		if len(engines) == 1 {
47			return engines[0], nil
48		}
49		return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
50	}
51	
52	// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
53	func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
54		if model == "" {
55			model = p.Model
56		}
57		return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
58			Name:      p.Name,
59			BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
60			Model:     model,
61			APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
62		})
63	}
64	
65	// googleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
66	func googleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
67		return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
68			Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
69			ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
70			UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
71			Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
72		})
73	}
74	
75	// dictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
76	// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
77	func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
78		if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
79			return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
80				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
81				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
82				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
83				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
84			})
85		}
86		var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
87		if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
88			apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
89				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
90				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
91				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
92				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
93			})
94		}
95		return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
96			Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
97			CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
98			EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
99			AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
100			Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
101			APIFallback:  apiFb,
102		})
103	}
104	
105	// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
106	// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
107	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
108	func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
109		cfg := res.Cfg
110		return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
111			CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
112			Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
113		})
114	}
115	
116	// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
117	// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
118	// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
119	// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
120	func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
121		cfg := res.Cfg
122		primaryName := res.Engine
123		if primaryName == "" {
124			primaryName = "auto"
125		}
126		set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
127		if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
128			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: googleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
129		}
130		if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
131			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: dictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
132			if res.Provider != nil {
133				set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: smartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
134			}
135		}
136		return set
137	}
138
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/chain.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"sync"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	// Chain is the AUTO fallback router. It tries engines in configured order and
11	// fails over to the next one when the current engine is known-down or fails
12	// before producing any token. Once tokens have streamed, a mid-stream error is
13	// surfaced rather than restarting on another engine (which would garble output);
14	// the common "provider not running" case fails at connect time, pre-token, and
15	// switches cleanly.
16	type Chain struct {
17		engines []Engine
18		ttl     time.Duration
19	
20		mu    sync.Mutex
21		avail map[string]probeCache
22	}
23	
24	type probeCache struct {
25		ok bool
26		at time.Time
27	}
28	
29	// NewChain builds a chain from engines in priority order. ttl is how long a
30	// health verdict is cached (default 5s).
31	func NewChain(engines []Engine, ttl time.Duration) *Chain {
32		if ttl <= 0 {
33			ttl = 5 * time.Second
34		}
35		return &Chain{engines: engines, ttl: ttl, avail: map[string]probeCache{}}
36	}
37	
38	// Name reports the chain as the "auto" engine.
39	func (c *Chain) Name() string { return "auto" }
40	
41	// Supports reports true if any member supports the mode.
42	func (c *Chain) Supports(m Mode) bool {
43		for _, e := range c.engines {
44			if e.Supports(m) {
45				return true
46			}
47		}
48		return false
49	}
50	
51	// recentlyDown reports whether we have a fresh "down" verdict for e, so we can
52	// skip it without paying a probe on the hot path. Engines are otherwise tried
53	// optimistically (no pre-probe latency on the happy path).
54	func (c *Chain) recentlyDown(e Engine) bool {
55		c.mu.Lock()
56		defer c.mu.Unlock()
57		pc, ok := c.avail[e.Name()]
58		return ok && !pc.ok && time.Since(pc.at) < c.ttl
59	}
60	
61	func (c *Chain) mark(e Engine, ok bool) {
62		c.mu.Lock()
63		c.avail[e.Name()] = probeCache{ok: ok, at: time.Now()}
64		c.mu.Unlock()
65	}
66	
67	func (c *Chain) candidates(m Mode) []Engine {
68		var out []Engine
69		for _, e := range c.engines {
70			if e.Supports(m) {
71				out = append(out, e)
72			}
73		}
74		return out
75	}
76	
77	// Translate proxies the first working engine's stream. Failover happens only
78	// before the first token.
79	func (c *Chain) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
80		cands := c.candidates(req.Mode)
81		if len(cands) == 0 {
82			return nil, ErrNoEngineForMode
83		}
84		out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
85		go func() {
86			defer close(out)
87			var lastErr error
88			var warns []string // reasons earlier engines were skipped/failed
89			for _, e := range cands {
90				if ctx.Err() != nil { // request abandoned (e.g. user typed more)
91					out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
92					return
93				}
94				if c.recentlyDown(e) {
95					lastErr = fmt.Errorf("%s: recently unavailable", e.Name())
96					warns = append(warns, e.Name()+" skipped (recently unavailable)")
97					continue
98				}
99				sub, err := e.Translate(ctx, req)
100				if err != nil {
101					lastErr = err
102					warns = append(warns, err.Error()) // engine errors are already name-prefixed
103					c.mark(e, false)
104					continue
105				}
106	
107				streamed, failed := false, false
108				for ch := range sub {
109					switch ch.Kind {
110					case ChunkToken:
111						streamed = true
112						out <- ch
113					case ChunkDone:
114						c.mark(e, true)
115						if ch.Result != nil && len(warns) > 0 {
116							ch.Result.Warnings = append(ch.Result.Warnings, warns...)
117						}
118						out <- ch
119						return
120					case ChunkError:
121						lastErr = ch.Err
122						failed = true
123						// Marking deferred until after the loop: a cancelled request
124						// (live-mode typing) must NOT be treated as an engine failure.
125					}
126				}
127	
128				if failed {
129					if ctx.Err() != nil { // cancelled: don't mark down, don't fail over
130						out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
131						return
132					}
133					c.mark(e, false)
134					warns = append(warns, lastErr.Error()) // already name-prefixed
135					if streamed {                          // already showed partial output: surface, don't restart
136						out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: lastErr}
137						return
138					}
139					continue // clean pre-token failover
140				}
141			}
142			if lastErr == nil {
143				lastErr = ErrAllEnginesFailed
144			}
145			out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%w: %v", ErrAllEnginesFailed, lastErr)}
146		}()
147		return out, nil
148	}
149	
150	// Detect returns the first available engine's detection, else "".
151	func (c *Chain) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
152		for _, e := range c.engines {
153			if c.recentlyDown(e) {
154				continue
155			}
156			if code, err := e.Detect(ctx, text); err == nil && code != "" {
157				return code, nil
158			}
159		}
160		return "", nil
161	}
162	
163	// Available reports whether any member is currently reachable.
164	func (c *Chain) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
165		for _, e := range c.engines {
166			if e.Available(ctx) {
167				c.mark(e, true)
168				return true
169			}
170			c.mark(e, false)
171		}
172		return false
173	}
174
```
</details></tool-use>

17		}

26			},

29				Short: "Print the effective configuration as TOML",
30				RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
31					cfg, _, err := config.Load()
32					if err != nil {
33						return err
34					}
35					b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
36					if err != nil {
37						return err
38					}
39					_, err = os.Stdout.Write(b)
40					return err
41				},
42			},
43		)

45	}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm getting a clearer picture of how the CLI works. The `PairTarget` in `internal/lang/detect.go` is central to understanding pair mode, where "home" and "away" represent the resolved target and the paired-with target respectively, configured through `Pair` and `PairWith` settings. Language detection happens through `lang.Detect` using `whatlanggo`, with `inLang` checking for Chinese specifically.

The logic in `PairTarget` is straightforward: it checks if the input text matches the home language using `inLang(text, home)`. If it does, it returns the away target; otherwise, it returns home. For a zh-TW⇄en pair, this works correctly — English text gets detected as non-Chinese and returns zh-TW, while Chinese text triggers the Han-character check and returns en.

But here's the issue: `PairTarget` only checks whether text is in the HOME language, not symmetrically checking the away language. This asymmetry could be where the detection breaks down.

The real problem might be which language is configured as home versus away. If the user set `default_target = en` and `pair_with = zh-TW`, then home is en and away is zh-TW. When they type English "test", `inLang("test", "en")` would call `Detect("test")` on a short word, which likely returns low confidence and fails — so it incorrectly returns home (en) instead of translating to zh-TW. The asymmetry means the pair doesn't work the same way in both directions depending on which language is configured as the default target. So when English is the home language, short English text like "test" doesn't get detected reliably by whatlanggo—it returns low confidence and defaults back to home, producing English instead of the expected Chinese translation. This asymmetry only happens when the home and away languages are swapped. I'm also noticing that the language detection confidence threshold is a real issue for short inputs, and there's something about a recent commit that addressed drift in the remember_last_pair logic for the CLI. The real issue is that when home is set to English with the default config, English input stays English instead of switching to Chinese. I need to trace through the actual configuration—checking the default settings, how the state file persists the pair, and how the TUI applies it—to see where the pair routing breaks down for this specific case.
</details></think>

1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"

13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"

15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
23		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
24		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
25		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
26		History   History    `toml:"history"`
27	}
28	
29	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
30	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
31	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
32	type Overlay struct {
33		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
34		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
35		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
36		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
37		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
38		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
39		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
40		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
41		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
42		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
43		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
44		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// General holds behavior settings.
48	type General struct {
49		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
50		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
51		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
52		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
53		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
54		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
55		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
56		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | llm | google | dict
57		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
58		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
59		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
60		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
61		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
62		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
63	}
64	
65	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
66	type Chain struct {
67		Order []string `toml:"order"`
68	}
69	
70	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
71	type Provider struct {
72		Name      string `toml:"name"`

74		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
75		Model     string `toml:"model"`

77		ModelMax  string `toml:"model_max,omitempty"`
78		APIKeyEnv string `toml:"api_key_env,omitempty"` // "" => no Authorization header
79	}

85		ExtraDT   []string `toml:"extra_dt,omitempty"`
86		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
87		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
88	}
89	
90	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.

92		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`

94		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
95		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
96		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`
97		Wordlist string `toml:"wordlist,omitempty"` // "" => /usr/share/dict/words if present
98	
99		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
100		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
101		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
102		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
103		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
104		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
105	}
106	
107	// History configures translation history storage.
108	type History struct {
109		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
110		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
111		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
112	}
113	
114	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
115	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
116	type SmartDict struct {
117		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
118		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
119		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
120		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
121		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
122		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
123	}
124

131		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
132	)

143				LiveTranslate:     false, // off by default to avoid spamming LLM/API while typing
144				DebounceMs:        700,   // when live is on, wait longer before firing
145				Engine:            "auto",
146				Tier:              "fast", // haiku by default — snappy for short, quick translations
147				Preset:            "contextual",
148				AlternativesCount: 3,
149				Stream:            true,
150				Color:             "auto",
151			},
152			Chain: Chain{Order: []string{"copilot", "ollama", "google", "dict"}},

182			},

196				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences

206	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
207	func Path() string {
208		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
209			return p
210		}
211		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
212	}
213	
214	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
215	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
216	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
217		p := Path()
218		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
219		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
220			cfg = Default()
221			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
222				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
223			}
224			return cfg, true, nil
225		}
226		if err != nil {
227			return nil, false, err
228		}
229		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
230		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
231			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
232		}
233		return cfg, false, nil
234	}
235	
236	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
237	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
238		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
239			return err
240		}
241		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
242		if err != nil {
243			return err
244		}
245		p := Path()
246		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
247		if err != nil {
248			return err
249		}
250		tmpName := tmp.Name()
251		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
252		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
253			tmp.Close()
254			return err
255		}
256		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
257			return err
258		}
259		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
260	}
261	
262	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
263	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
264		for i := range c.Providers {
265			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
266				return &c.Providers[i]
267			}
268		}
269		return nil
270	}
271	
272	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
273	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
274	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
275		switch tier {
276		case "fast":
277			if p.ModelFast != "" {
278				return p.ModelFast
279			}
280		case "max":
281			if p.ModelMax != "" {
282				return p.ModelMax
283			}
284		}
285		return p.Model
286	}
287

43	### Per-front-end defaults (`[cli]` / `[tui]`)
44	
45	The CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI share `[general]`, but
46	either can override any subset of it via an optional `[cli]` or `[tui]` table — e.g.
47	a snappy concise CLI and a richer, higher-tier TUI:
48	
49	```toml
50	[general]
51	preset = "contextual"
52	tier   = "fast"
53	
54	[cli]                 # one-shot / piped translation
55	preset = "concise"
56	
57	[tui]                 # interactive front-end
58	tier          = "max"
59	live_translate = true
60	```
61	
62	Only keys you list are overridden; the rest inherit `[general]`. Flags and env vars
63	still win over both.
64	
65	## TUI keys
66	
67	`↵` translate · live-debounce auto-translates ~700ms after you stop typing (off by default) ·
68	`^y` copy · `^l` toggle live · `^e` cycle engine · `^t` target language · `^p` model ·
69	`^o` style · `^g` toggle pair mode · `^r` history · `^u` clear · `⌥↵` newline · `^c`/`esc` quit.
70	
71	## Engines & fallback
72	
73	The `auto` engine tries `chain.order` in turn, failing over **before the first
74	token** (so a down provider switches cleanly; a mid-stream error surfaces rather
75	than restarting):
76	
77	- **copilot-proxy** — OpenAI-compatible at `http://localhost:4141/v1`, no API key.
78	  Default tier is **fast** (`claude-haiku-4-5`); `--tier default` → `claude-sonnet-5`,
79	  `--tier max` → `claude-opus-4-8`. Claude models are served only via the proxy's
80	  Anthropic Messages endpoint (`/v1/messages`) — the engine routes `claude-*` ids
81	  there automatically (they 400 on `/chat/completions`).
82	- **Ollama** — `http://localhost:11434/v1` (offline; `llama3.2:3b`).
83	- **Google** — free, keyless; also reports the detected source language.
84	- **openrouter** — configured but off the default chain; needs `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.
85	
86	> ⚠️ Using a GitHub Copilot subscription to back non-GitHub tools (copilot-proxy)
87	> may violate Copilot's Terms of Service. Reorder `chain.order` (drop `copilot`,
88	> lead with `ollama`/`google`) in the config if that matters to you.
89	
90	Config lives at `~/.config/translate/config.toml`, history at
91	`~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl`, last-pair state at
92	`~/.local/state/translate/state.json` (XDG-honored on macOS and Linux).
93	
94	## Dictionary mode
95	
96	`^e` cycles to the dictionary engine (or use `translate define <word>`). It is an
97	**offline bilingual** dictionary that routes by script — Chinese → CC-CEDICT
98	(zh→en), English → ECDICT (en→zh):
99	
100	```sh
101	translate dict update all      # one-time ~67 MB download/build into ~/.local/share/translate/dict
102	```
103	
104	Until then, English lookups fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (`[dict] api_fallback`),
105	and Chinese lookups prompt you to run the update. Misses show a ranked "did you
106	mean" list. Set `[dict] source = "api"` to use only dictionaryapi.dev.
107	
108	### Smart dictionary (`smart-dict`)
109	
110	A distinct engine that keeps the offline dictionary fast and deterministic, but on
111	a miss — no entry, or a fuzzy match too far off — falls back to the LLM for a gloss
112	plus example sentences (never silently: the result carries a `⚠ … defined via <provider> (LLM)`
113	warning). Close typos (edit distance ≤ `[smartdict] close_distance`, default 1) still
114	show "did you mean" without calling the LLM.
115	
116	```sh
117	translate define serendipity        # exact ECDICT entry
118	translate define zzzznotaword       # miss → LLM definition (⚠ warning on stderr)
119	translate define helllo             # distance-1 typo → "did you mean: hello, …"
120	translate define --plain <word>     # force the offline dictionary, no LLM fallback
121	```
122	
123	`translate define` uses smart-dict whenever an LLM provider is reachable
124	(`[smartdict] define_default`, `--smart`/`--plain` to force); in the TUI, `^e`
125	cycles to a `smart-dict` engine alongside the plain `dictionary`.
126	
127	```toml
128	[smartdict]
129	close_distance = 1            # en edit-distance ≤ this stays "did you mean"; beyond → LLM
130	preset         = "dictionary" # LLM output style for the fallback
131	define_default = true         # `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
132	```
133	
134	Data: **CC-CEDICT** (CC BY-SA 4.0, © Paul Andrew Denisowski / MDBG) and
135	**ECDICT** (MIT, © skywind3000).
```
</details></tool-use>

1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- If the source language equals the target language, return the text unchanged (lightly
31	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
32	
33	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
34	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
35	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
36	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
37	
38	Rules:
39	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
40	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
41	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
42	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
43	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
44	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
45	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
46	
47	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
48	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
49	that also gives brief usage examples.
50	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
51	
52	Rules:
53	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
54	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
55	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
56	- Format each example on two lines:
57	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
58	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
59	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
60	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
61	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
62	
63	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
64	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
65		switch preset {
66		case PresetContextual:
67			return translateSystemPromptContextual
68		case PresetDictionary:
69			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
70		default:
71			return translateSystemPromptConcise
72		}
73	}
74	
75	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
76	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
77	Target language: %s
78	
79	Text:
80	%s`
81	
82	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
83	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
84	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
85	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
86		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
87		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
88			src = "auto"
89		} else {
90			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
91		}
92		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
93		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
94		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
95			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
96		}
97		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
98	}
99
```
