```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	}
37	
38	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
39	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
40	type TranslateResult struct {
41		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
42		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
43		Target         string   `json:"target"`
44		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
45		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
46		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
47	
48		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
49		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
50		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
51	
52		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
53		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
54		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
55		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
56		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
57	
58		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
59		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
60		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
63		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
64		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
65		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
66		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
67		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
68	
69		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
70		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
71		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
72		// Transient: not serialized.
73		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
74	}
75	
76	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
77	type DictEntry struct {
78		Word      string    `json:"word"`
79		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
80		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
81		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
82	}
83	
84	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
85	type Meaning struct {
86		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
87		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
88		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
89		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
90	}
91	
92	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
93	type Definition struct {
94		Text    string `json:"definition"`
95		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
96	}
97	
98	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
99	type ChunkKind int
100	
101	const (
102		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
103		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
104		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
105		ChunkDone
106		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
107		ChunkError
108	)
109	
110	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
111	type Chunk struct {
112		Kind   ChunkKind
113		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
114		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
115		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
116	}
117	
118	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
119	//
120	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
121	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
122	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
123	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
124	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
125	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
126	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
127	type Engine interface {
128		Name() string
129		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
130		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
131		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
132		Supports(m Mode) bool
133	}
134	
135	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
136	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
137	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
138		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
139		if err != nil {
140			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
141		} else {
142			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
143		}
144		close(ch)
145		return ch
146	}
147	
148	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
149	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
150	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
151	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
152		for c := range ch {
153			switch c.Kind {
154			case ChunkToken:
155				if onToken != nil {
156					onToken(c.Text)
157				}
158			case ChunkDone:
159				return c.Result, nil
160			case ChunkError:
161				return nil, c.Err
162			}
163		}
164		return nil, ErrNoResult
165	}
166
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/state/state.go`

```text
1	// Package state persists small session state (the last language pair and source
2	// mode) so a bare `translate` resumes where the user left off.
3	package state
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
12	)
13	
14	// State is the persisted last-session snapshot.
15	type State struct {
16		Source     string    `json:"source"`                // "auto" or a code
17		Target     string    `json:"target"`                // code
18		SourceMode string    `json:"source_mode,omitempty"` // auto | fixed
19		Provider   string    `json:"provider,omitempty"`
20		Engine     string    `json:"engine,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt  time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	}
23	
24	// Load reads the state file, returning a zero State if it does not exist.
25	func Load() (*State, error) {
26		b, err := os.ReadFile(xdgpath.StateFile())
27		if err != nil {
28			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
29				return &State{}, nil
30			}
31			return nil, err
32		}
33		var s State
34		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err != nil {
35			return &State{}, nil // corrupt state is non-fatal
36		}
37		return &s, nil
38	}
39	
40	// Save writes the state atomically (temp file + rename).
41	func Save(s *State) error {
42		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
43			return err
44		}
45		s.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
46		b, err := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		p := xdgpath.StateFile()
51		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".state-*.json")
52		if err != nil {
53			return err
54		}
55		tmpName := tmp.Name()
56		defer os.Remove(tmpName)
57		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
58			tmp.Close()
59			return err
60		}
61		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
62			return err
63		}
64		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
65	}
66
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I need to trace through the TUI's pair handling logic and examine the llm.go engine to understand how the LLM call works and the prompt gets transmitted. I should also look at commit b183aa1 for engine changes around the smart-dict LLM fallback and per-frontend defaults, plus how the prompt's source="auto" parameter works—the model auto-detects on the LLM side while the TARGET remains fixed by PairTarget's heuristic. So far the key finding is that pair mode is set to home.

The routing logic in `PairTarget` is asymmetrical: it only checks if the text matches the HOME language using Han-check for Chinese or whatlanggo's `Detect` with a confidence threshold of 0.5, but it never tests the away language. This means any text that doesn't look like home or fails detection defaults back to home. The prompt passes source as "auto" or a fixed value, and the target is whatever `PairTarget` computed, so the LLM doesn't actually choose the direction—that's already decided locally by the heuristic, and the model just translates into the target it receives. If source equals target, the model returns the text unchanged.

The unreliability stems from this asymmetry: if home is set to English and the user types "test", whatlanggo might fail to detect it with sufficient confidence on such short input, causing it to fall back to home (English) instead of routing to the away language (Chinese). But if home is Chinese instead, that same English text would correctly route to Chinese. The problem arises when the default configuration sets `default_target` to English, which becomes the home language in pair mode.

There's also a drift issue with `remember_last_pair`: the system saves both source and target from the last translation, so if you translate Chinese to English, the next run might restore English as the home language. The CLI fix in commit 8a243b9 addressed this, and the TUI path was already correct, but `applyLastPair` still restores both source and target, which could cause source to drift from its typical "auto" setting.

The bigger reliability problem is that `whatlanggo` requires confidence ≥ 0.5 to detect a language, so short text often returns empty. This makes the pair heuristic unreliable except when home is Chinese (since the Han character check is solid) and away is non-Chinese. Going the other direction—home as English, Spanish, or French—depends on `whatlanggo`, which is flaky on short inputs. I should verify whether there's a scenario where both home and away could be incorrectly set, and check what the actual user config looks like.
</details></think>

commit b183aa18240fab6012d7a1f5a67970f72f632b19
Author: Da-Wei Lee <daviddwlee84@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jul 10 16:41:47 2026 +0800

    feat(config,engine): per-front-end [cli]/[tui] defaults + smart-dict LLM fallback
    
    Add optional [cli] and [tui] config overlays so the one-shot CLI and the
    interactive TUI can diverge from [general] (e.g. concise/fast CLI, contextual/max
    TUI). Resolve() now takes an invocation Mode and applies the overlay; precedence
    is flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default. LiveTranslate/DebounceMs move
    into Resolved so the TUI stops reading cfg.General directly.
    
    Add a distinct `smart-dict` engine: an offline dictionary lookup that falls back
    to the LLM (dictionary preset: gloss + examples) on a miss or a fuzzy match too
    far off, while keeping close typos as "did you mean". The previously-dropped
    Levenshtein distance is surfaced via TranslateResult.SuggestDistance so the
    threshold ([smartdict] close_distance) is enforceable. Reachable via the TUI ^e
    cycle and `translate define` (--plain/--smart, [smartdict] define_default). The
    fallback stamps a warning so the downgrade is never silent.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
+// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
+// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
+func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
+	cfg := res.Cfg
+	return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
+		CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
+		Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
+	})
+}

+// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
+// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
+// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
+func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
+	cfg := res.Cfg
+	if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
+		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
+	}
+	if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
+		return smartDictFromConfig(res)
+	}
+	return dictFromConfig(cfg)
+}

+type SmartDict struct {

280	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
281	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
282	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
283	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
284		label := "translating…"
285		if m.retrying {
286			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
287		}
288		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
289	}
290	
291	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
292	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
293	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
294	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
295	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
296		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
297		if text == "" {
298			m.status = statusIdle
299			return m, nil
300		}
301		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
302		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
303			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
304		}
305		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
306		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
307		if !force {
308			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
309				m.cancelInflight()
310				m.status = statusDone
311				m.err = nil
312				m.result = cached
313				if cached.Engine != "" {
314					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
315				}
316				if cached.Model != "" {
317					m.curModel = cached.Model
318				}
319				m.streamBuf = ""
320				m.lastInput = text
321				m.lastDoneKey = key
322				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
323				return m, nil
324			}
325		}
326		m.seq++
327		if m.cancel != nil {
328			m.cancel()
329		}
330		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
331		m.cancel = cancel
332		m.inflight = m.seq
333		m.status = statusTranslating
334		m.streamBuf = ""
335		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
336		m.err = nil
337		m.result = nil
338		m.lastInput = text
339		m.pendingKey = key
340		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
341	
342		seq := m.seq
343		ne := m.active()
344		target := m.target
345		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate {
346			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
347		}
348		req := engine.Request{
349			Text:          text,
350			Source:        m.source,
351			Target:        target,
352			Mode:          ne.Mode,
353			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
354			Model:         m.modelOverride,
355			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
356			Preset:        m.preset,
357			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
358		}
359		ch, err := ne.Engine.Translate(ctx, req)
```

19	// LLMConfig parameterizes an OpenAI-compatible backend (copilot-proxy, Ollama,
20	// OpenRouter, LiteLLM, or a generic base_url+key endpoint).
21	type LLMConfig struct {
22		Name      string        // "copilot", "ollama", "openrouter", ...
23		BaseURL   string        // e.g. "http://localhost:4141/v1"
24		Model     string        // e.g. "claude-sonnet-5"
25		APIKeyEnv string        // env var holding the key; "" => no Authorization header
26		Timeout   time.Duration // per-request timeout (0 => 60s)
27	}
28	
29	// LLMEngine talks to any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint.
30	type LLMEngine struct {
31		cfg  LLMConfig
32		key  string
33		http *http.Client
34	}
35	
36	// NewLLM builds an LLM engine, resolving the API key once from the environment.
37	func NewLLM(cfg LLMConfig) *LLMEngine {
38		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
39			cfg.Timeout = 60 * time.Second
40		}
41		key := ""
42		if cfg.APIKeyEnv != "" {
43			key = strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv))
44		}
45		return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
46	}
47	
48	// Name returns the provider name (e.g. "copilot").
49	func (e *LLMEngine) Name() string { return e.cfg.Name }
50	
51	// Supports reports that LLM engines translate. (Dictionary lookups route to the
52	// dedicated dictionary engine in v1, so the chain never sends dict mode here.)
53	func (e *LLMEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
54	
55	// auth adds the bearer token only when a key is configured. copilot-proxy needs
56	// no Authorization header, so an empty APIKeyEnv yields no header at all.
57	func (e *LLMEngine) auth(r *http.Request) {
58		if e.key != "" {
59			r.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+e.key)
60		}
61	}
62	
63	// endpoint joins the base URL with a path segment.
64	func (e *LLMEngine) endpoint(path string) string {
65		return strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.BaseURL, "/") + path
66	}
67	
68	// Available is a cheap health probe: GET {BaseURL}/models with a short timeout.
69	func (e *LLMEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
70		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 800*time.Millisecond)
71		defer cancel()
72		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
73		if err != nil {
74			return false
75		}
76		e.auth(req)
77		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
78		if err != nil {
79			return false
80		}
81		defer resp.Body.Close()
82		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
83		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
84	}

87	// with the free-API slice. Callers fall back to lang.Detect when this returns "".
88	func (e *LLMEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
89		return "", nil
90	}

92	// --- OpenAI chat wire types ---
93	
94	type chatMessage struct {
95		Role    string `json:"role"`
96		Content string `json:"content"`
97	}
98	
99	type chatRequest struct {
100		Model    string        `json:"model"`
101		Messages []chatMessage `json:"messages"`
102		Stream   bool          `json:"stream"`
103	}
104	
105	type chatResponse struct {
106		Choices []struct {
107			Message      chatMessage `json:"message"`
108			FinishReason string      `json:"finish_reason"`
109		} `json:"choices"`
110		Error *struct {
111			Message string `json:"message"`
112		} `json:"error"`
113	}
114	
115	// streamDelta is one OpenAI SSE chunk (stream:true). FinishReason is non-nil
116	// only on the terminal chunk ("stop" on a clean finish, "length" on a cap hit).
117	type streamDelta struct {
118		Choices []struct {
119			Delta struct {
120				Content string `json:"content"`
121			} `json:"delta"`
122			FinishReason *string `json:"finish_reason"`
123		} `json:"choices"`
124	}

127	
128	const anthropicMaxTokens = 4096
129	
130	type anthropicRequest struct {
131		Model     string             `json:"model"`
132		MaxTokens int                `json:"max_tokens"`
133		System    string             `json:"system,omitempty"`
134		Messages  []anthropicMessage `json:"messages"`
135		Stream    bool               `json:"stream"`
136	}
137	
138	type anthropicMessage struct {
139		Role    string `json:"role"`
140		Content string `json:"content"`
141	}

146			Text string `json:"text"`
147		} `json:"content"`
148		StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
149		Error      *struct {
150			Message string `json:"message"`
151		} `json:"error"`
152	}
153	
154	type anthropicStreamEvent struct {
155		Type  string `json:"type"`
156		Delta struct {
157			Type string `json:"type"`
158			Text string `json:"text"`
159			// StopReason is carried on the terminal "message_delta" event
160			// ("end_turn"/"stop_sequence" on a clean finish, "max_tokens" on a cap
161			// hit); empty on "text_delta" events.
162			StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
163		} `json:"delta"`
164		Error *struct {
165			Message string `json:"message"`
166		} `json:"error"`

168	
169	// isAnthropicModel reports whether a model id must use the Anthropic Messages API
170	// (/v1/messages) rather than /chat/completions. copilot-proxy serves Claude
171	// models ONLY via the messages endpoint — they return HTTP 400
172	// "model_not_supported" on /chat/completions despite being listed in /v1/models.
173	func isAnthropicModel(model string) bool {
174		return strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(model), "claude")
175	}
176	
177	// Translate performs a translation, streaming tokens when req.Stream is set.
178	// It always returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk, and
179	// dispatches to the Anthropic Messages API for Claude models.
180	func (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
181		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
182			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
183		}
184		model := NormalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)
185		// Apply a per-request model override only when it targets this provider, so a
186		// copilot model id never reaches an Ollama fallback (which would 404).
187		if req.Model != "" && (req.ModelProvider == "" || req.ModelProvider == e.cfg.Name) {
188			model = NormalizeModelID(req.Model)
189		}
190		if isAnthropicModel(model) {
191			return e.translateAnthropic(ctx, req, model)
192		}
193		return e.translateOpenAI(ctx, req, model)
194	}
195	
196	// ModelLister is implemented by engines that can enumerate their models.
197	type ModelLister interface {
198		Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)
199	}
200	
201	type modelsResponse struct {
202		Data []struct {
203			ID                 string   `json:"id"`
204			SupportedEndpoints []string `json:"supported_endpoints"`
205		} `json:"data"`
206	}
207	
208	// Models fetches the provider's model ids, keeping only those usable through the
209	// transports this engine speaks: OpenAI /chat/completions, or (for claude-*) the
210	// Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint. Ids needing only /responses are dropped.
211	func (e *LLMEngine) Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
212		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 4*time.Second)
213		defer cancel()
214		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
215		if err != nil {
216			return nil, err
217		}
218		e.auth(req)
219		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
220		if err != nil {
221			return nil, err
222		}
223		defer resp.Body.Close()
224		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
225			return nil, e.httpError(resp)
226		}
227		var mr modelsResponse
228		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&mr); err != nil {
229			return nil, err
230		}
231		var out []string
232		for _, m := range mr.Data {
233			if usableModel(m.ID, m.SupportedEndpoints) {
234				out = append(out, m.ID)
235			}
236		}
237		sort.Strings(out)
238		return out, nil
239	}
240	
241	// usableModel reports whether a model id can be driven by this engine.
242	func usableModel(id string, endpoints []string) bool {
243		if isAnthropicModel(id) {
244			return true // routed via /v1/messages
245		}
246		for _, ep := range endpoints {
247			if ep == "/chat/completions" {
248				return true
249			}
250		}
251		// No endpoint metadata (e.g. Ollama) => assume chat-usable.
252		return len(endpoints) == 0
253	}
254	
255	// finalize builds the terminal result from the accumulated translation text.
256	func (e *LLMEngine) finalize(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
257		res := &TranslateResult{
258			Translation: strings.TrimSpace(full),
259			Target:      req.Target,
260			Engine:      e.cfg.Name,
261			Model:       model,
262		}
263		// The plain-text prompt returns only the translation, so fill the detected
264		// source offline when the caller asked to auto-detect.
265		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
266			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
267		}
268		return res
269	}
270	
271	// markTruncated flags a result whose stream ended before the model finished, so
272	// the caller keeps the partial text but never treats it as a complete answer.
273	func markTruncated(res *TranslateResult) {
274		res.Truncated = true
275		res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings,
276			"output was cut off before completion (stream truncated) — press Enter to retry")
277	}
278	
279	// translateOpenAI uses the OpenAI /chat/completions endpoint.
280	func (e *LLMEngine) translateOpenAI(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
281		system, user := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
282		body := chatRequest{
283			Model: model,
284			Messages: []chatMessage{
285				{Role: "system", Content: system},
286				{Role: "user", Content: user},
287			},
288			Stream: req.Stream,
289		}
290		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
291		if err != nil {
292			return nil, err
293		}
294		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/chat/completions"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
295		if err != nil {
296			return nil, err
297		}
298		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
299		if req.Stream {
300			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
301		}
302		e.auth(httpReq)
303	
304		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32) // buffered so a fast stream doesn't block on renders
305		go func() {
306			defer close(ch)
307			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
308			if err != nil {
309				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
310				return
311			}
312			defer resp.Body.Close()
313			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
314				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
315				return
316			}
317	
318			var full strings.Builder
319			complete := true
320			if req.Stream {
321				var err error
322				complete, err = e.readSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
323				if err != nil {
324					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
325					return
326				}
327			} else {
328				var cr chatResponse
329				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&cr); err != nil {
330					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
331					return
332				}
333				if cr.Error != nil {
334					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, cr.Error.Message)}
335					return
336				}
337				if len(cr.Choices) == 0 {
338					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: empty response", e.cfg.Name)}
339					return
340				}
341				full.WriteString(cr.Choices[0].Message.Content)
342				complete = cr.Choices[0].FinishReason != "length" // "length" => hit the cap
343			}
344			res := e.finalize(full.String(), model, req)
345			if !complete {
346				markTruncated(res)
347			}
348			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
349		}()
350		return ch, nil
351	}
352	
353	// translateAnthropic uses the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages).
354	func (e *LLMEngine) translateAnthropic(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
355		system, user := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
356		body := anthropicRequest{
357			Model:     model,
358			MaxTokens: anthropicMaxTokens,
359			System:    system,
360			Messages:  []anthropicMessage{{Role: "user", Content: user}},
361			Stream:    req.Stream,
362		}
363		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
364		if err != nil {
365			return nil, err
366		}
367		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/messages"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
368		if err != nil {
369			return nil, err
370		}
371		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
372		httpReq.Header.Set("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
373		if req.Stream {
374			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
375		}
376		e.anthropicAuth(httpReq)
377	
378		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32)
379		go func() {
380			defer close(ch)
381			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
382			if err != nil {
383				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
384				return
385			}
386			defer resp.Body.Close()
387			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
388				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
389				return
390			}
391	
392			var full strings.Builder
393			complete := true
394			if req.Stream {
395				var err error
396				complete, err = e.readAnthropicSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
397				if err != nil {
398					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
399					return
400				}
401			} else {
402				var ar anthropicResponse
403				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&ar); err != nil {
404					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
405					return
406				}
407				if ar.Error != nil {
408					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ar.Error.Message)}
409					return
410				}
411				for _, c := range ar.Content {
412					if c.Type == "text" {
413						full.WriteString(c.Text)
414					}
415				}
416				complete = ar.StopReason != "max_tokens" // "max_tokens" => hit the cap
417			}
418			res := e.finalize(full.String(), model, req)
419			if !complete {
420				markTruncated(res)
421			}
422			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
423		}()
424		return ch, nil
425	}
426	
427	// anthropicAuth sets the Anthropic auth header (x-api-key, not Bearer). The
428	// copilot-proxy needs none, so an empty key yields no header.
429	func (e *LLMEngine) anthropicAuth(r *http.Request) {
430		if e.key != "" {
431			r.Header.Set("x-api-key", e.key)
432		}
433	}
434	
435	// readSSE parses an OpenAI-style event stream, emitting a ChunkToken per content
436	// delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports complete=true only
437	// when the stream ended on a terminal marker ([DONE] or finish_reason=="stop");
438	// a stream that closes with no marker, or on finish_reason=="length", is treated
439	// as truncated so the caller can flag it rather than silently accept partial text.
440	func (e *LLMEngine) readSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
441		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
442		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20) // tolerate long SSE lines
443		sawDone := false
444		finish := ""
445		for sc.Scan() {
446			line := sc.Text()
447			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
448				continue
449			}
450			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
451			if payload == "[DONE]" {
452				sawDone = true
453				break
454			}
455			var d streamDelta
456			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil || len(d.Choices) == 0 {
457				continue
458			}
459			if fr := d.Choices[0].FinishReason; fr != nil && *fr != "" {
460				finish = *fr
461			}
462			tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content
463			if tok == "" {
464				continue
465			}
466			full.WriteString(tok)
467			select {
468			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:
469			case <-ctx.Done():
470				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
471			}
472		}
473		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
474			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
475		}
476		complete := finish != "length" && (sawDone || finish == "stop")
477		return complete, nil
478	}
479	
480	// readAnthropicSSE parses an Anthropic Messages event stream, emitting a
481	// ChunkToken per text_delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports
482	// complete=true only when a terminal marker arrived (a message_stop event, or a
483	// message_delta with a natural stop_reason); a stream that closes with neither,
484	// or on stop_reason=="max_tokens", is treated as truncated.
485	func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
486		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
487		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
488		sawStop := false
489		stopReason := ""
490		for sc.Scan() {
491			line := sc.Text()
492			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
493				continue // ignore "event:" and blank lines
494			}
495			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
496			if payload == "" {
497				continue
498			}
499			var ev anthropicStreamEvent
500			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &ev) != nil {
501				continue
502			}
503			if ev.Error != nil {
504				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ev.Error.Message)
505			}
506			switch ev.Type {
507			case "message_stop":
508				sawStop = true
509				continue
510			case "message_delta":
511				if ev.Delta.StopReason != "" {
512					stopReason = ev.Delta.StopReason
513				}
514				continue
515			}
516			if ev.Type != "content_block_delta" || ev.Delta.Type != "text_delta" || ev.Delta.Text == "" {
517				continue
518			}
519			full.WriteString(ev.Delta.Text)
520			select {
521			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: ev.Delta.Text}:
522			case <-ctx.Done():
523				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
524			}
525		}
526		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
527			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
528		}
529		complete := stopReason != "max_tokens" &&
530			(sawStop || stopReason == "end_turn" || stopReason == "stop_sequence")
531		return complete, nil
532	}
533	
534	// httpError reads an error body (best effort) into a descriptive error.
535	func (e *LLMEngine) httpError(resp *http.Response) error {
536		b, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 2<<10))
537		msg := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
538		if msg == "" {
539			msg = resp.Status
540		}
541		return fmt.Errorf("%s: http %d: %s", e.cfg.Name, resp.StatusCode, msg)
542	}
543
```
</details></tool-use>

Config file lives at `~/.config/translate/config.toml` (`internal/xdgpath/paths.go:40`, overridable via `$TRANSLATE_CONFIG`, `internal/config/config.go:207-212`).

## 1. Config system

Schema is defined in **`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/config.go`**. Top-level struct `Config` (`config.go:17-27`):

```go
type Config struct {
	General   General    `toml:"general"`
	CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
	TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
	Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
	Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
	Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
	Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
	SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
	History   History    `toml:"history"`
}
```

### `[general]` (`config.go:48-63`) with defaults from `Default()` (`config.go:135-204`):

| Field | TOML key | Default |
|---|---|---|
| DefaultTarget | `default_target` | `"en"` |
| DefaultSource | `default_source` | `"auto"` |
| Pair | `pair` | `false` |
| PairWith | `pair_with` | `"en"` |
| RememberLastPair | `remember_last_pair` | `true` |
| LiveTranslate | `live_translate` | `false` |
| DebounceMs | `debounce_ms` | `700` |
| Engine | `engine` | `"auto"` (auto\|llm\|google\|dict) |
| Tier | `tier` | `"fast"` (default\|fast\|max) |
| Preset | `preset` | `"contextual"` (concise\|contextual\|dictionary) |
| Instructions | `instructions` | `""` |
| AlternativesCount | `alternatives_count` | `3` |
| Stream | `stream` | `true` |
| Color | `color` | `"auto"` |

### `[cli]` / `[tui]` overlays — the b183aa1 feature (`config.go:29-45`)

`Overlay` is a **partial `[general]` with every field a pointer** (nil = inherit). Both `Config.CLI` and `Config.TUI` are `*Overlay` with `omitempty`, so they are **nil / absent unless the user hand-writes the table**. Overlay fields: `engine, tier, preset, instructions, model, default_target, default_source, pair, pair_with, stream, live_translate, debounce_ms`. Note `model` exists on the overlay but not on `General` (read directly in `resolve.go:159-163`).

Precedence (comment `config.go:31` and `resolve.go:54`): **flag > env (`TRANSLATE_*`) > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > built-in default**. Merge logic: `resolve.go:60-168` — `overlayFor()` picks CLI vs TUI by `Mode`, `applyOverlay()` copies non-nil fields onto a `General` value-copy, then `pick()` layers flag/env on top.

### Other sections (all with `Default()` values):

- **`[chain]`** (`config.go:66-68`): `order = ["copilot", "ollama", "google", "dict"]` (`config.go:152`).
- **`[[provider]]`** array (`config.go:71-79`, defaults `config.go:153-175`): `copilot` (openai, `localhost:4141/v1`, models `claude-sonnet-5`/`claude-haiku-4-5`/`claude-opus-4-8`), `ollama` (`localhost:11434/v1`, `llama3.2:3b`), `openrouter` (off the default chain, needs `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`). Tier→model constants at `config.go:128-132`.
- **`[google]`** (`config.go:82-88`, defaults `config.go:176-182`): `enabled=true`, translate_a/single endpoint, `extra_dt=["bd","at"]`, `timeout_ms=4000`.
- **`[dict]`** (`config.go:91-105`, defaults `config.go:183-193`): `enabled=true`, `source="local"` (CC-CEDICT zh→en + ECDICT en→zh), `lang="en"`, `fuzzy=true`, cedict/ecdict URLs, `auto_download=false`, `api_fallback=true`.
- **`[smartdict]`** (`config.go:116-123`, defaults `config.go:194-198`): `close_distance=1`, `preset="dictionary"`, `define_default=true`.
- **`[history]`** (`config.go:108-112`, defaults `config.go:199-202`): `enabled=true`, `backend="jsonl"`.

`Load()` (`config.go:216-234`) starts from `Default()` then unmarshals the file over it, so omitted keys keep default values. On first run it writes `Default()` and returns `created=true`. `translate config show` (`cmd/config.go:27-42`) marshals the effective config.

(Note: `.specstory/cli/config.toml` is unrelated — it is SpecStory's own tool config, not translate's.)

---

## 2. `translate init` command

**`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/init.go`**. It is an interactive `huh` wizard, not a static scaffold. Flow: `config.Load()` → probe copilot/ollama reachability (`init.go:34-36`) → present a form → write back with `config.Save()`.

The form prompts for **only these fields** (`init.go:52-106`), all mapped onto `[general]`:
- Default **engine** (auto / copilot / ollama / google) — `init.go:54-63`
- Default **target language** — `init.go:65-71`
- **Translation style / preset** (contextual / concise / dictionary) — `init.go:73-81`
- **Custom instructions** — `init.go:83-86`
- **Live translate** (bool) — `init.go:88-91`
- **Bidirectional pair mode** (bool) — `init.go:93-96`
- **pair-with** language — `init.go:98-104`

Then it sets `cfg.General.{Engine,DefaultTarget,LiveTranslate,Preset,Instructions,Pair,PairWith}` (`init.go:111-117`), refreshes the copilot model ids to current recommendations (`init.go:120-122`), and `config.Save(cfg)` (`init.go:123`).

**What `init` writes vs omits:**
- Because `Save()` marshals the entire `Config` (defaults merged in `Load`), the saved file contains **all** sections (`[general]`, `[chain]`, `[[provider]]`, `[google]`, `[dict]`, `[smartdict]`, `[history]`) — but only the seven fields above reflect user choices; the rest are the built-in defaults.
- **It does NOT prompt for or scaffold**: the per-front-end `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlays (they stay nil → absent from the file), tier, `[dict]` mode/source, `[smartdict]` options (close_distance / preset / define_default), stream, color, alternatives_count, history, or provider details.
- **Regarding your three specific asks:** `init` covers **translation mode partially** (pair on/off + pair_with, and preset which includes the "dictionary" style), but has **no** dictionary-engine-mode selection and **no** smart-dict-LLM-fallback option. Per-front-end `[cli]`/`[tui]` defaults must be hand-edited (README documents this manually at `README.md:43-63`).

---

## 3. Translation modes

There are **two orthogonal axes**: the engine `Mode` (translate vs dict), and the "pair" bidirectional flag.

### Engine `Mode` enum — `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/engine.go:12-19`
```go
const (
	ModeTranslate Mode = iota // register-aware, faithful translation (the default)
	ModeDict                  // a dictionary/definition lookup
)
```

### Preset styles — `internal/engine/prompt.go:13-15`
`PresetConcise="concise"`, `PresetContextual="contextual"`, `PresetDictionary="dictionary"`. These are LLM prompt styles within `ModeTranslate`, independent of the dict engine.

### The selectable engines
- **LLM** (`internal/engine/llm.go`), **Google** (`internal/engine/google.go`) — `ModeTranslate`.
- **Dictionary** plain — `internal/engine/localdict.go` / `dict.go`, `ModeDict`.
- **smart-dict** — `internal/engine/smartdict.go`, `Supports` only `ModeDict` (`smartdict.go:46`).
- **Chain** ("auto" router) — `internal/engine/chain.go`.

### "Pair" (bidirectional) mode
Not an engine mode — a routing flag on `[general].pair` / `--pair` / `TRANSLATE_PAIR`. When on: home-language input → `pair_with`, else → `default_target`. Implemented at `cmd/root.go:180-186` (`effectiveTarget` → `lang.PairTarget`); resolved at `resolve.go:138`. Default **off** (`config.go:140`).

### Default mode per front-end
There is **no separate default engine-mode for CLI vs TUI** — both default to `Engine="auto"` + `ModeTranslate`.
- CLI: `runRoot` always issues `engine.ModeTranslate` requests (`cmd/root.go:335`, `oneShot` at `root.go:327-339`).
- TUI: starts on the primary translate engine; `buildEngineSet` (`cmd/build.go:120-137`) makes `{primary(ModeTranslate), google, dictionary(ModeDict), smart-dict(ModeDict)}` cycle-able via `^e`, but the initial mode is `ModeTranslate`.
The only place CLI/TUI defaults legitimately diverge is via the `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlays (e.g. README example: TUI `tier="max"`, `live_translate=true`).

---

## 4. Smart-dict LLM fallback (b183aa1)

Defined in **`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/smartdict.go`**. `SmartDictEngine` (`smartdict.go:31-40`) composes a dict engine + an LLM engine. Config struct `SmartDict` in `config.go:116-123`; built by `smartDictFromConfig` (`cmd/build.go:108-114`).

**When it falls back to LLM** — `Translate` drains the dict result, then `dictAnswered()` decides (`smartdict.go:86-99`):
- dict transport error or nil result (hard miss / `ErrNoDictEntry`) → **fall back**;
- exact entry (`res.Dictionary != nil`) → keep;
- fuzzy suggestion with `SuggestDistance != 0 && <= CloseDistance` (default 1) → keep as "did you mean";
- suggestions too far, unknown distance, or "not installed" notes → **fall back**.

The fallback (`smartdict.go:103-126`) reruns the request in `ModeTranslate` with preset `[smartdict].preset` (default `"dictionary"`), retargets Chinese↔English via `smartTarget` (`smartdict.go:131-140`), and **always stamps a Warning** (`smartdict.go:121-123`) — `"no dictionary entry for %q — defined via %s (LLM)"` — so the downgrade is never silent.

**How it is configured** (`config.go:116-123`, default `config.go:194-198`):
```toml
[smartdict]
close_distance = 1            # en edit-distance ≤ this stays "did you mean"; beyond → LLM
preset         = "dictionary"
define_default = true         # `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
```

**Is dict+LLM-fallback the default?** Only for the `translate define` subcommand, and only when a provider is reachable. `defineEngine` (`cmd/define.go:69-78`): `--plain` or no provider → plain dict; else if `--smart` **or `cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault` (default true)** → smart-dict; else plain. So `translate define <word>` defaults to smart-dict when an LLM provider is up. It is **not** part of the main `translate <word>` path.

---

## 5. Trace: `translate <word>` with a fresh/default config

Path: `runRoot` (`cmd/root.go:114-178`).

1. `config.Load()` → fresh config = `Default()`, so `Engine="auto"`, `Pair=false`, `Tier="fast"`, `Preset="contextual"`, `DefaultTarget="en"`, `DefaultSource="auto"`.
2. `invocationMode(args)` = `ModeCLI` because there is an arg (`root.go:104-112`); no `[cli]` overlay exists, so `Resolve` returns the `[general]` values unchanged.
3. `res.Pair` is false → `effectiveTarget` returns the plain home target (`root.go:181-186`); **no pair redirection**.
4. `buildEngine(res)` with `Engine="auto"` (`cmd/build.go:16-50`) walks `chain.order = ["copilot","ollama","google","dict"]`: adds `copilot` LLM (fast tier → `claude-haiku-4-5`), `ollama` LLM, `google`. **`"dict"` is skipped — there is no case for it** (`build.go:41` comment "wired in a later slice"). So the auto chain is copilot → ollama → google, wrapped in `engine.NewChain` (`build.go:49`).
5. The request is `Mode: engine.ModeTranslate`, `Preset:"contextual"`, streaming to a TTY (`root.go:327-339`). The Chain tries copilot first (`chain.go:79-148`).

**So a bare `translate <word>` on a default config runs: `ModeTranslate` via the auto chain (copilot-proxy LLM, `claude-haiku-4-5`, contextual preset), NOT pair mode, NOT the dictionary, and NOT smart-dict.** Dictionary and smart-dict are reached only via `translate define` or the TUI `^e` engine cycle; pair mode only when `[general].pair`/`--pair` is set; smart-dict's LLM fallback is exclusive to the `define` (dict) path.

## 5. Trace: `translate &lt;word&gt;` with a fresh/default config

1. `config.Load()` → fresh config = `Default()`, so `Engine="auto"`, `Pair=false`, `Tier="fast"`, `Preset="contextual"`, `DefaultTarget="en"`, `DefaultSource="auto"`.
2. `invocationMode(args)` = `ModeCLI` because there is an arg (`root.go:104-112`); no `[cli]` overlay exists, so `Resolve` returns the `[general]` values unchanged.
3. `res.Pair` is false → `effectiveTarget` returns the plain home target (`root.go:181-186`); **no pair redirection**.
4. `buildEngine(res)` with `Engine="auto"` (`cmd/build.go:16-50`) walks `chain.order = ["copilot","ollama","google","dict"]`: adds `copilot` LLM (fast tier → `claude-haiku-4-5`), `ollama` LLM, `google`. **`"dict"` is skipped — there is no case for it** (`build.go:41` comment "wired in a later slice"). So the auto chain is copilot → ollama → google, wrapped in `engine.NewChain` (`build.go:49`).
5. The request is `Mode: engine.ModeTranslate`, `Preset:"contextual"`, streaming to a TTY (`root.go:327-339`). The Chain tries copilot first (`chain.go:79-148`).

**So a bare `translate &lt;word&gt;` on a default config runs: `ModeTranslate` via the auto chain (copilot-proxy LLM, `claude-haiku-4-5`, contextual preset), NOT pair mode, NOT the dictionary, and NOT smart-dict.** Dictionary and smart-dict are reached only via `translate define` or the TUI `^e` engine cycle; pair mode only when `[general].pair`/`--pair` is set; smart-dict's LLM fallback is exclusive to the `define` (dict) path.</result>

## TUI Front-End Report: translate CLI/TUI

**Language/framework:** Go, using **Bubble Tea v2** under the `charm.land/*` module namespace (a fork/alias of charmbracelet). Confirmed in `go.mod:5-9` (`charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `charm.land/bubbles/v2`, `charm.land/lipgloss/v2`, `charm.land/huh/v2`). All TUI code lives in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/`.

Files: `model.go` (state + constructor), `update.go` (Update/handleKey/launch/layout), `view.go` (rendering), `keys.go` (keymap), `styles.go`, `msgs.go` (commands/messages), `pickers.go` (overlays), `history.go`, `cache.go`. Launched from `cmd/root.go:280-303` via `tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), ...).Run()`.

---

### 1. Overall TUI architecture

The main model is `Model` in `model.go:56-121` — a value-receiver Bubble Tea model (v2 convention: methods return the modified copy). Constructed by `New()` at `model.go:132-201`; `Init()` at `model.go:208-210`.

Three stacked panes rendered in `View()` (`view.go:16-36`), top to bottom via `lg.JoinVertical`:
- **input box** — a bordered box wrapping `m.ta.View()` (`view.go:24-28`)
- **result box** — fixed-height bordered box wrapping `m.vp.View()` (`view.go:32`)
- **footer** — status/help line, `m.statusLine()` (`view.go:33`)

Key sub-components held on the model (`model.go:60-67`): `ta textarea.Model` (input), `vp viewport.Model` (output), plus five `list.Model` overlays (`hist`, `langList`, `modelList`, `suggestList`, `presetList`) and a `spinner.Model`. Overlays are full-screen pickers gated by `m.overlay overlayKind` (`pickers.go:14-24`); when non-`overlayNone`, `View()` renders only the picker + its footer (`view.go:20-22`).

---

### 2. Input handling

- **Component:** `charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea` — a **multi-line** text input (it internally embeds a `viewport` and thus scrolls its own content when text exceeds its visible height; `defaultMaxHeight = 99`).
- **Construction:** `model.go:133-139`:
  ```go
  ta := textarea.New()
  ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
  ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
  ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
  ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter")) // Enter is reserved for translate
  ta.Focus()
  ```
  Note the newline key is remapped to **Alt+Enter** because plain Enter triggers translation.
- **Sizing (`relayout()`, `update.go:545-573`):** input is fixed at `const inputH = 4` rows; width `compW = m.width - 6` (the box frame is border 2 + padding 2 = 4, and the box is rendered at `m.width-2`). Set at `update.go:559-560` (`m.ta.SetWidth`/`SetHeight`). The box wrapper is applied in `view.go:28` at `.Width(m.width-2)`.
- **Scrolling when long:** Yes — because the textarea has a fixed height of 4 and content can exceed it, the textarea's internal viewport scrolls to follow the cursor (LineNext/LinePrevious bound to up/down/ctrl+n/ctrl+p in the bubbles textarea default keymap). There is no app-level scroll control of the input; it just follows the cursor.
- **Input feeds the pipeline:** ordinary keystrokes fall through `handleKey` to `m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)` (`update.go:257-258`), then arm the debounce in live mode (`update.go:271-276`). Empty input cancels in-flight work and clears the result (`update.go:260-269`).

---

### 3. Output handling

- **Component:** `charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport` (`m.vp`), constructed at `model.go:175-176` with `vp.SoftWrap = true` (long unwrapped lines soft-wrap instead of being clipped — see `pitfalls/tui-viewport-clips-long-translation-no-softwrap.md`).
- **Sizing:** fixed height `resultH = m.height - footerH - (inputH+2) - 2` (min 3), width `compW = m.width-6` (`update.go:555-563`). Height stored in `m.resultH` and the box is rendered at a **fixed** `.Height(m.resultH)` (`view.go:32`) so the layout never jumps between the "translating…" placeholder and streamed output.
- **Content is pushed, not pulled:** the viewport content is set imperatively via `m.vp.SetContent(...)` in several places — streaming tokens (`update.go:87` then `m.vp.GotoBottom()` at `:88`), completed result (`update.go:125` then `m.vp.GotoTop()` at `:126`), placeholder (`update.go:59`, `340`), errors (`update.go:142`), and clear (`update.go:241`, `267`, `389`).
- **How output scrolling works currently — this is the key finding:** the output viewport receives input **only from the mouse wheel**. `handleMouseWheel` (`update.go:493-501`) forwards wheel events to `m.vp.Update(msg)`. **No keyboard keys ever reach `m.vp`** — in `handleKey`, the terminal fallthrough sends every unmatched key to the *textarea* (`update.go:258`), never to the viewport. So arrow keys / PageUp / PageDown / ctrl-u / ctrl-d do **not** scroll the output; they either move the input cursor (arrows) or hit an app binding (ctrl-u = Clear). 
- **Reusable scroll infrastructure:** the viewport already has full pager methods and a default keymap that are currently dormant (never fed key events). From `charm.land/bubbles/v2@v2.1.1/viewport`: `PageDown/PageUp/HalfPageUp/HalfPageDown` methods (`viewport.go:485-513`) and `DefaultKeyMap()` binding pgdown/space/f, pgup/b, `u`/`ctrl+u` (half-page up), `d`/`ctrl+d` (half-page down), up/k, down/j. To enable keyboard output-scroll you'd either route selected keys into `m.vp.Update`, or call the `m.vp.HalfPageUp()`/etc. methods directly from `handleKey`.

---

### 4. Keybindings

Defined in `keys.go` — `keyMap` struct (`keys.go:7-20`) and `defaultKeys()` (`keys.go:22-37`). Handled in `handleKey` (`update.go:148-278`). Full mapping (footer left→right):

| Footer | Binding (keys.go) | Handler |
|---|---|---|
| `↵ translate` | `Translate` = `enter`, `ctrl+enter` (`keys.go:24`) | `update.go:244-253` → `m.launch(true)` (second Enter on a dict miss opens suggestions, `:247-252`) |
| `^y copy` | `Copy` = `ctrl+y` (`keys.go:32`) | `update.go:204-214` → `clipboard.WriteAll(m.copyText())`, flash |
| `^l live` | `ToggleLive` = `ctrl+l` (`keys.go:26`) | `update.go:215-217` → toggles `m.live` |
| `^e engine` | `CycleEngine` = `ctrl+e` (`keys.go:27`) | `update.go:219-231` → cycles `m.engIdx` |
| `^t lang` | `PickLang` = `ctrl+t` (`keys.go:28`) | `update.go:174-176` → `overlayLang` |
| `^p model` | `PickModel` = `ctrl+p` (`keys.go:29`) | `update.go:178-182` → `openModelPicker()` (suppressed in dict mode) |
| `^o style` | `PickPreset` = `ctrl+o` (`keys.go:30`) | `update.go:184-190` → `overlayPreset` (suppressed in dict mode) |
| `^g pair` | `TogglePair` = `ctrl+g` (`keys.go:31`) | `update.go:192-202` → toggles `m.pair` |
| `^r history` | `History` = `ctrl+r` (`keys.go:33`) | `update.go:170-172` → `overlayHistory` + `loadHistoryCmd` |
| `^c quit` | `Quit` = `ctrl+c`, `esc` (`keys.go:35`) | `update.go:166-168` → `tea.Quit` |
| (not in footer) | `Newline` = `alt+enter` (`keys.go:25`) | handled inside textarea (remapped at `model.go:138`) |

**Existing "clear input" binding — YES, already present but undocumented.** `Clear` = **`ctrl+u`** (`keys.go:34`, help `"^u","clear"`), handled at `update.go:233-242`:
```go
case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
    m.cancelInflight()
    m.seq++
    m.ta.Reset()
    m.result = nil
    m.err = nil
    m.streamBuf = ""
    m.status = statusIdle
    m.vp.SetContent("")
    return m, nil
```
It resets the textarea and clears the result pane. Notably it is **not** listed in either footer help string (`view.go:128` dict-mode, `view.go:156` normal-mode) — the feature exists but is not surfaced. (Also relevant: this task's directory carries a `.specstory/history/2026-07-13_09-39-53Z-1-tui-clear-input.md` session and the plan filename `1-tui-clear-input`.)

Overlay-mode keys are handled separately at the top of `handleKey` (`update.go:149-163`): while any picker is open, `ctrl+c` quits, `esc` closes the overlay, `enter` selects (`overlaySelect()`, `update.go:437-490`), everything else forwards to the active list (`overlayListUpdate()`, `update.go:419-434`).

**Focus-switching between input and output panes: NONE.** There is no Tab binding and no key that changes which pane receives input.

---

### 5. Focus model

- The only "focus" concept is the textarea's own focus state, used purely for **border styling**: `New()` calls `ta.Focus()` once (`model.go:139`); `View()` checks `m.ta.Focused()` to pick the highlighted border style `inputHi` vs `input` (`view.go:24-28`, styles at `styles.go:34-35`). The textarea is always focused; `Blur()` is never called.
- The output `viewport` has **no focus concept** at all (bubbles viewports are stateless w.r.t. focus; they just respond to `Update`). There is no "focused pane" abstraction, no `focus` field on `Model`.
- **No Tab handling anywhere.** A grep for `Tab`/`Focus`/`Blur`/`focus` across `internal/tui/` returns only `ta.Focus()` (`model.go:139`) and `m.ta.Focused()` (`view.go:25`).

**Implications for adding focus-switching / output-scroll:** there is no existing focus infrastructure to reuse — you'd add a focus field to `Model` (e.g. an enum input/output pane), gate the fallthrough at `update.go:257-258` so keys route to `m.vp` when the output pane is focused, add a Tab binding to `keyMap`, and toggle `m.ta.Focus()`/`m.ta.Blur()` accordingly (the input border style already keys off `Focused()`, so it will visually reflect focus for free). The viewport's dormant `HalfPageUp/HalfPageDown/PageUp/PageDown` methods and default keymap are ready to reuse. Note `ctrl+u` and `ctrl+e` collisions to watch: the app already binds `ctrl+u`=Clear and `ctrl+e`=CycleEngine, which shadow the viewport's default half-page-up and the textarea's line-end respectively.

---

**Most relevant files for the planned change:** `internal/tui/keys.go` (add/rename bindings), `internal/tui/update.go` (`handleKey` `:148-278`, `relayout` `:545-573`), `internal/tui/view.go` (footer help strings `:128`,`:156`; pane rendering `:24-36`), `internal/tui/model.go` (add focus state; component construction `:132-201`).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The real problem emerges when remember_last_pair is enabled: if home=zh-TW and away=en, after the first run with Chinese input, the state saves the away language (en). On the next run, applyLastPair restores that saved target, effectively flipping home to en, which collapses the pair mode since both home and away become en. The fix addresses this by saving the stable home value instead, but the user is still reporting issues. Good news: the current working tree does have the fix with the recordAndRemember function and rememberTarget parameter. So HEAD is solid there.

But the unreliability persists, and I'm seeing the core problem now—the detection logic is purely local and heuristic-based. The LLM never actually does the detecting or routing; instead, PairTarget makes that decision entirely through whatlanggo's language detection and a Han character check, then passes a fixed target to the LLM. The prompt just tells it to translate into that target language. So the whole reliability of pair mode hinges on whatlanggo.Detect working correctly with its 0.5 confidence threshold, which is fragile.

The asymmetry is brutal too—PairTarget only tests whether the input matches the home language, never the away language. Anything that doesn't confidently match home gets routed to home, which means source equals target and the LLM returns it unchanged. That's why "test" or "ok" in English stays English when home is English, but works fine when home is Chinese because the Han check is much more robust. The whole system's reliability flips based on which language is designated as home.

And whatlanggo itself struggles with short text—it returns empty when confidence drops below 0.5, which happens constantly on brief inputs.

Then there's the default configuration problem: both DefaultTarget and PairWith default to "en", so if someone enables pair mode without changing the languages, home and away are identical and the function just returns early. The TUI also defaults pairWith to "en" when toggling pair mode, creating the same degenerate case.

Issue 5 is trickier — the fix for remember_last_pair only stabilizes the home value at the CLI level, but saveLastPair also persists the source language. If a user runs a non-pair command with an explicit --to flag, that stale target gets cached in state, and on the next bare pair invocation it overrides the home with that wrong value. Plus any state file from before the fix could still contain a drifted target, which breaks pair mode until the state is refreshed.

The core issue is that auto-detection in pair mode fails because it depends on whatlanggo's confidence scoring for the non-Chinese language, and only validates against the home language. Short or ambiguous input that whatlanggo can't confidently identify as the home language silently defaults to no translation, which breaks the intended direction. 45		{"nl", "dutch", []string{"nld", "dut", "nederlands"}},
46		{"pl", "polish", []string{"pol", "polski"}},
47		{"tr", "turkish", []string{"tur", "türkçe"}},

49		{"th", "thai", []string{"tha", "ไทย"}},

51		{"uk", "ukrainian", []string{"ukr", "українська"}},
52		{"sv", "swedish", []string{"swe", "svenska"}},
53		{"cs", "czech", []string{"ces", "cze", "čeština"}},
54		{"el", "greek", []string{"ell", "gre", "ελληνικά"}},
55		{"he", "hebrew", []string{"heb", "עברית", "iw"}},
56		{"ro", "romanian", []string{"ron", "rum", "română"}},
57		{"hu", "hungarian", []string{"hun", "magyar"}},
58		{"fi", "finnish", []string{"fin", "suomi"}},
59		{"da", "danish", []string{"dan", "dansk"}},
60		{"no", "norwegian", []string{"nor", "norsk", "nb", "nn"}},
61		{"fa", "persian", []string{"fas", "per", "farsi", "فارسی"}},
62		{"bn", "bengali", []string{"ben", "বাংলা"}},
63		{"ta", "tamil", []string{"tam", "தமிழ்"}},
64		{"ms", "malay", []string{"msa", "may", "bahasa melayu"}},
65		{"ca", "catalan", []string{"cat", "català"}},
66	}

68	// candidate is one searchable token (code, name, or alias) pointing at a Lang.
69	type candidate struct {
70		token string
71		lang  *Lang
72		// weight scales the fuzzy score: exact code/name matches are preferred.
73		primary bool
74	}
75	
76	var candidates = buildCandidates()
77	
78	func buildCandidates() []candidate {
79		var cs []candidate
80		for i := range table {
81			l := &table[i]
82			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Code), lang: l, primary: true})
83			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Name), lang: l, primary: true})
84			for _, a := range l.Aliases {
85				cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(a), lang: l})
86			}
87		}
88		return cs
89	}
90	
91	// Resolve maps a free-form query to a language, tolerating typos. It returns the
92	// best match plus ranked alternatives (for TUI disambiguation). "auto" and ""
93	// pass through as an auto-detect sentinel (Code "auto", Exact true).
94	func Resolve(q string) (Match, []Match) {
95		norm := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(q))
96		if norm == "" || norm == "auto" {
97			return Match{Code: "auto", Name: "auto-detect", Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
98		}
99	
100		// Exact match on any code / name / alias.
101		for _, c := range candidates {
102			if c.token == norm {
103				return Match{Code: c.lang.Code, Name: c.lang.Name, Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
104			}
105		}
106	
107		// Fuzzy: rank candidates by normalized Levenshtein similarity.
108		type scored struct {
109			lang  *Lang
110			score float64
111		}
112		best := map[string]scored{} // dedupe by language code, keep best score
113		for _, c := range candidates {
114			d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(norm, c.token)
115			maxLen := max(len(norm), len(c.token))
116			if maxLen == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			sim := 1 - float64(d)/float64(maxLen)
120			if !c.primary {
121				sim -= 0.05 // slight preference for code/name over alias
122			}
123			if cur, ok := best[c.lang.Code]; !ok || sim > cur.score {
124				best[c.lang.Code] = scored{lang: c.lang, score: sim}
125			}
126		}
127	
128		ranked := make([]Match, 0, len(best))
129		for _, s := range best {
130			ranked = append(ranked, Match{Code: s.lang.Code, Name: s.lang.Name, Score: s.score})
131		}
132		sort.Slice(ranked, func(i, j int) bool { return ranked[i].Score > ranked[j].Score })
133	
134		if len(ranked) == 0 || ranked[0].Score < 0.5 {
135			// No confident match; echo the query back as an opaque code so callers
136			// can still attempt it (e.g. a code we don't have in the table).
137			return Match{Code: norm, Name: norm, Score: 0}, ranked
138		}
139		top := 3
140		if len(ranked) < top {
141			top = len(ranked)
142		}
143		return ranked[0], ranked[1:top]
144	}
145	
146	// List returns all known languages (for the target-language picker).
147	func List() []Lang {
148		out := make([]Lang, len(table))
149		copy(out, table)
150		return out
151	}
152	
153	// Name returns the English name for a code, or the code itself if unknown.
154	func Name(code string) string {
155		if strings.EqualFold(code, "auto") || code == "" {
156			return "auto-detect"
157		}
158		for i := range table {
159			if strings.EqualFold(table[i].Code, code) {
160				return table[i].Name
161			}
162		}
163		return code
164	}
165

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-13 09:46:13Z)**_

I now have the complete picture, including confirmation of the library behavior. Here is my report.

---

# Pair-mode language detection & target selection — findings

**Language/structure:** Go (module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, go 1.26.4). Cobra CLI + Bubble Tea TUI over a shared `engine` layer. Relevant packages: `cmd/`, `internal/config/`, `internal/lang/`, `internal/engine/`, `internal/tui/`, `internal/state/`. Language detection uses the `github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo` library.

**One-line answer to "why is it unreliable":** Pair-mode direction is decided entirely by a *local* heuristic (`lang.PairTarget`) that only tests whether the input is the **home** language, using `whatlanggo` trigram detection with a 0.5 confidence gate for non-CJK. The LLM is never asked to detect+route — it's handed a pre-decided fixed target. So routing is robust only in the Han-script direction (Chinese), and short Latin inputs like "test" fall below the confidence gate and silently route to home.

---

## 1. Pair mode definition — "home" vs "away"

The two languages are called **home** and **away** in the routing code. `home` is the resolved `Target` (from `default_target`/`--to`); `away` is `PairWith` (from `pair_with`/`--pair-with`).

- Config schema: `internal/config/config.go:51-52`
  ```go
  Pair     bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
  PairWith string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
  ```
- Per-front-end overlay copies: `internal/config/config.go:40-41`
- **Defaults (a real footgun):** `internal/config/config.go:139,141` — `DefaultTarget: "en"` AND `PairWith: "en"`. Out of the box home == away == `en`.
- Flag overrides: `internal/config/resolve.go:18-19` (`Overrides.Pair/PairWith`); resolved/merged at `internal/config/resolve.go:138-139`.
- CLI flags: `cmd/root.go:70-71` (`--pair`, `--pair-with`).
- CLI resolves `pairWith` code: `cmd/root.go:128-132`.
- TUI carries it: `internal/tui/model.go:36` (`PairWith` in Params), stored at `internal/tui/model.go:79,193-194`. The runtime toggle `^g` defaults an empty pairWith to `"en"`: `internal/tui/update.go:192-196`.

The "home"/"away" naming is defined in the signature `PairTarget(home, away, text string)` at `internal/lang/detect.go:36`.

---

## 2. Language detection — a local heuristic, NOT the LLM

All pair detection lives in `internal/lang/detect.go`. There is **no LLM-based detection** for routing (the LLM engine's `Detect` is a no-op: `internal/engine/llm.go:88-90`).

- `internal/lang/detect.go:13-19` — offline detect via whatlanggo, gated at confidence 0.5:
  ```go
  func Detect(text string) string {
      info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
      if info.Confidence < 0.5 {
          return ""
      }
      return info.Lang.Iso6391()
  }
  ```
- `internal/lang/detect.go:23-30` — `IsChinese` = any Han rune.
- `internal/lang/detect.go:48-59` — `inLang`: **Chinese uses the reliable Han-script check; every other language falls back to whatlanggo `Detect`:**
  ```go
  func inLang(text, code string) bool {
      base := baseCode(code)
      switch base {
      case "", "auto":
          return false
      case "zh":
          return IsChinese(text)
      default:
          d := Detect(text)
          return d != "" && d == base
      }
  }
  ```

**Why whatlanggo is flaky for the non-Chinese side** (confirmed in the library source, `whatlanggo@v1.0.1/detect.go:24-70`): detection is script-based. Han/Hangul/Greek/etc. return `Confidence: 1` immediately (`case unicode.Han: return Cmn, 1`). But **Latin-script** languages (en, es, fr, de…) go through `detectLangInProfiles` (trigram frequency profiles), which needs enough text to be confident. Short inputs — exactly the single-word/short-phrase case this tool is built for — produce low confidence, so `Detect` returns `""` and `inLang` is false.

---

## 3. Target selection — the asymmetric core bug

`internal/lang/detect.go:32-44`:
```go
// home=zh-TW, away=en: Chinese → en, everything else → zh-TW.
func PairTarget(home, away, text string) string {
    if away == "" || away == home {
        return home
    }
    if inLang(text, home) {
        return away
    }
    return home
}
```

**The routing only ever tests the home language.** The `else` branch sends *everything not confidently detected as home* to `home`. Consequences:

- **home = zh-TW, away = en (the ideal config): reliable.** English "test" → not Han → `home` (zh-TW) → Chinese ✓. Chinese → Han → `away` (en) ✓. This direction works because Chinese detection is the Han check.
- **home = en, away = zh-TW (reversed, or after drift): fragile.** English "test" → `inLang("test","en")` → `Detect("test")` → low-confidence → `""` → false → returns `home` = en. Result: **English "test" stays English — no translation.** Only if whatlanggo happens to peg the short input as English (it often won't) does it route to away.
- **home == away (e.g. default `en`/`en`, or `^g` toggle when target is already en): degenerate.** `detect.go:37` early-returns `home` for *every* input — pair mode is a silent no-op.

### Callers of the routing
- CLI: `cmd/root.go:180-186` (`effectiveTarget`), invoked for args at `cmd/root.go:150` and stdin at `cmd/root.go:167`.
- TUI: `internal/tui/update.go:344-347`:
  ```go
  target := m.target
  if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate {
      target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
  }
  ```

### remember_last_pair and the 8a243b9 fix (HEAD is a6d9f19, a rebased duplicate with identical message)
- Persist/restore state: `internal/state/state.go` (Source/Target/SourceMode).
- `cmd/root.go:190-204` `applyLastPair` — on a bare pair run, **overrides resolved `res.Source`/`res.Target` (i.e. the home) from the saved state** when no flags/env set them.
- `cmd/root.go:224-236` `recordAndRemember` + `cmd/root.go:256-268` `saveLastPair`.

The fix (`git show 8a243b9`, and HEAD `a6d9f19`) changed the CLI so it persists the **stable home** (`tgt`) instead of the pair-resolved effective target (`effTgt`):
```go
// cmd/root.go:155,172
recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)  // was: (…, effTgt)
// cmd/root.go:229-234
func recordAndRemember(…, target, rememberTarget string) {
    if st != nil { st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target)) }
    if cfg.General.RememberLastPair { saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine) }
}
```
Before the fix: translating away-language input (Chinese → en) saved `Target=en`, so the next bare run seeded `home=en`, collapsing pair mode (home became the away language, per case-2 above). The fix stops that CLI drift and matches the TUI (`cmd/root.go:299-304`, which already saved `fm.Pair()` = the home target, `internal/tui/model.go:205`).

**Caveats the fix does NOT address (still contributing to "not working ideally"):**
1. The **asymmetric/whatlanggo-confidence problem in `PairTarget` is untouched** — it's purely about *which* target is persisted, not about detection quality.
2. `saveLastPair` still also persists `source`; and `applyLastPair` still overrides the home target from state. A **stale state file written before the fix** (holding a drifted `Target=en`) will keep collapsing pair mode until it's overwritten by a home-valued run.
3. A one-off non-pair run with `--to X` writes `Target=X` to state, which then seeds the home on the next bare pair run.

---

## 4. The prompt — it does NOT auto-detect/route

Prompts are in `internal/engine/prompt.go`. All three presets take a **fixed target** and translate into it; none instructs the model to detect the input language and pick the opposite pair member.

- Concise (default hot path), `internal/engine/prompt.go:21-31` — note the last rule:
  > `Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.` … `- If the source language equals the target language, return the text unchanged (lightly corrected if it was misspelled).`
- Contextual: `internal/engine/prompt.go:34-45`. Dictionary: `internal/engine/prompt.go:48-61`.
- User message template, `internal/engine/prompt.go:76-80`:
  ```
  Source language: %s
  Target language: %s

  Text:
  %s
  ```
- Assembly, `internal/engine/prompt.go:85-98`: `Source` is passed as `"auto"` when unset (so the model *can* detect the source), but **`Target` is the already-decided `effTgt`** from `PairTarget`. Language codes are expanded to names (`zh-TW` → "chinese (traditional)") via `lang.Name` (`internal/lang/lang.go:153-164`, table at `:30-66`).

So when `PairTarget` mis-routes (e.g. English "test" → target en), the prompt tells the model "translate into English", and the concise rule "source == target → return unchanged" makes the model echo the input. The model has no signal that it was supposed to flip direction.

## 5. Engine layer trace (pair mode, end to end)

Interface/types: `internal/engine/engine.go` — `Engine` (`:127-133`), `Request` (`:23-36`, note `Source "auto"` = detect), `TranslateResult` (`:40-74`), `Drain` (`:151-165`).

**CLI path** (`cmd/root.go`):
1. `runRoot` → `cfg.Resolve` (`:125`) → `applyLastPair` (`:126`) → `resolvePair` (`:127`) → resolve `pairWith` (`:128-132`).

**TUI path**: `internal/tui/update.go:295-359` `launch` computes `target` via `PairTarget` (`:344-347`), builds the `Request`, calls `ne.Engine.Translate`.

**LLM execution** (`internal/engine/llm.go`):
- `Translate` (`:180-194`) routes Claude ids to `translateAnthropic` (`:353-425`, `/v1/messages`), else `translateOpenAI` (`:280-351`, `/chat/completions`); both call `buildTranslatePrompt(req)` (`:281`/`:355`).
- `finalize` (`:256-269`) sets `Translation`/`Target: req.Target` and, only for metadata, fills `DetectedSource` from `lang.Detect(req.Text)` when source was auto (`:264-267`) — **this detected value is display/history only; it does not influence the target.**

The auto-chain wrapper (`internal/engine/chain.go`) and `oneShot` warnings (`cmd/root.go:359-364`) surface fallbacks but don't affect pair routing.

## Summary: exact reasons pair-mode auto-detection is unreliable

1. **Detection ≠ LLM.** Direction is chosen by `lang.PairTarget` (`internal/lang/detect.go:36-44`) using `whatlanggo`; the LLM only translates into a pre-fixed target and is never asked to detect-and-flip. Prompt: `internal/engine/prompt.go:21-98`.
2. **Asymmetric test.** `PairTarget` only checks `inLang(text, home)`; anything not confidently home falls through to home. Robust only for the Chinese (Han) direction and only when Chinese is `home`.
3. **whatlanggo confidence gate (0.5) on short Latin text.** `internal/lang/detect.go:13-19` returns `""` for short/ambiguous non-CJK input (library uses trigram profiles for Latin, confidence 1 only for Han/Hangul/etc. — `whatlanggo@v1.0.1/detect.go:42-70`). So short English like "test" isn't recognized as `en`.
4. **Degenerate default.** `default_target="en"` + `pair_with="en"` (`internal/config/config.go:139,141`) and the `^g` fallback (`internal/tui/update.go:194-196`) make home == away, which `PairTarget` early-returns as a no-op (`internal/lang/detect.go:37`).
5. **State drift.** The 8a243b9/a6d9f19 fix (`cmd/root.go:155,172,229-234`) stops the CLI from persisting the away target as home, but `applyLastPair` (`cmd/root.go:190-204`) still lets a stale/foreign state target override the home, and the fix does nothing for the detection weaknesses in (2)-(4).

# Pair-mode language detection &amp; target selection — findings

**Language/structure:** Go (module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, go 1.26.4). Cobra CLI + Bubble Tea TUI over a shared `engine` layer. Relevant packages: `cmd/`, `internal/config/`, `internal/lang/`, `internal/engine/`, `internal/tui/`, `internal/state/`. Language detection uses the `github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo` library.

**One-line answer to "why is it unreliable":** Pair-mode direction is decided entirely by a *local* heuristic (`lang.PairTarget`) that only tests whether the input is the **home** language, using `whatlanggo` trigram detection with a 0.5 confidence gate for non-CJK. The LLM is never asked to detect+route — it's handed a pre-decided fixed target. So routing is robust only in the Han-script direction (Chinese), and short Latin inputs like "test" fall below the confidence gate and silently route to home.

---

## 1. Pair mode definition — "home" vs "away"

The two languages are called **home** and **away** in the routing code. `home` is the resolved `Target` (from `default_target`/`--to`); `away` is `PairWith` (from `pair_with`/`--pair-with`).

- Config schema: `internal/config/config.go:51-52`
  ```go
  Pair     bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
  PairWith string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
  ```
- Per-front-end overlay copies: `internal/config/config.go:40-41`
- **Defaults (a real footgun):** `internal/config/config.go:139,141` — `DefaultTarget: "en"` AND `PairWith: "en"`. Out of the box home == away == `en`.
- Flag overrides: `internal/config/resolve.go:18-19` (`Overrides.Pair/PairWith`); resolved/merged at `internal/config/resolve.go:138-139`.
- CLI flags: `cmd/root.go:70-71` (`--pair`, `--pair-with`).
- CLI resolves `pairWith` code: `cmd/root.go:128-132`.
- TUI carries it: `internal/tui/model.go:36` (`PairWith` in Params), stored at `internal/tui/model.go:79,193-194`. The runtime toggle `^g` defaults an empty pairWith to `"en"`: `internal/tui/update.go:192-196`.

The "home"/"away" naming is defined in the signature `PairTarget(home, away, text string)` at `internal/lang/detect.go:36`.

## 2. Language detection — a local heuristic, NOT the LLM

All pair detection lives in `internal/lang/detect.go`. There is **no LLM-based detection** for routing (the LLM engine's `Detect` is a no-op: `internal/engine/llm.go:88-90`).

- `internal/lang/detect.go:13-19` — offline detect via whatlanggo, gated at confidence 0.5:
  ```go
  func Detect(text string) string {
      info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
      if info.Confidence &lt; 0.5 {
          return ""
      }
      return info.Lang.Iso6391()
  }
  ```
- `internal/lang/detect.go:23-30` — `IsChinese` = any Han rune.
- `internal/lang/detect.go:48-59` — `inLang`: **Chinese uses the reliable Han-script check; every other language falls back to whatlanggo `Detect`:**
  ```go
  func inLang(text, code string) bool {
      base := baseCode(code)
      switch base {
      case "", "auto":
          return false
      case "zh":
          return IsChinese(text)
      default:
          d := Detect(text)
          return d != "" &amp;&amp; d == base
      }
  }
  ```

**Why whatlanggo is flaky for the non-Chinese side** (confirmed in the library source, `whatlanggo@v1.0.1/detect.go:24-70`): detection is script-based.
