Short inputs — exactly the single-word/short-phrase case this tool is built for — produce low confidence, so `Detect` returns `""` and `inLang` is false.

1	package lang
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"unicode"
6	
7		"github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo"
8	)
9	
10	// Detect returns a best-effort ISO-639-1 source-language code for text, or "" if
11	// the guess is not confident. Used to fill DetectedSource on the LLM path (where
12	// the model returns only the translation) and as a fallback when APIs are down.
13	func Detect(text string) string {
14		info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
15		if info.Confidence < 0.5 {
16			return ""
17		}
18		return info.Lang.Iso6391()
19	}
20	
21	// IsChinese reports whether text contains any Han characters — used to route
22	// dictionary lookups (Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT).
23	func IsChinese(text string) bool {
24		for _, r := range text {
25			if unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) {
26				return true
27			}
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	// PairTarget implements bidirectional "pair" mode: if the input is written in
33	// the home language, translate it to away; otherwise translate it to home. This
34	// makes "foreign → my language, my language → foreign" work from one input box
35	// (e.g. home=zh-TW, away=en: Chinese → en, everything else → zh-TW).
36	func PairTarget(home, away, text string) string {
37		if away == "" || away == home {
38			return home
39		}
40		if inLang(text, home) {
41			return away
42		}
43		return home
44	}
45	
46	// inLang reports whether text is (best-effort) in the language of code. Chinese
47	// uses a reliable Han-script check; others fall back to offline detection.
48	func inLang(text, code string) bool {
49		base := baseCode(code)
50		switch base {
51		case "", "auto":
52			return false
53		case "zh":
54			return IsChinese(text)
55		default:
56			d := Detect(text)
57			return d != "" && d == base
58		}
59	}
60	
61	// baseCode strips a region suffix: "zh-TW" → "zh".
62	func baseCode(code string) string {
63		code = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(code))
64		if i := strings.IndexByte(code, '-'); i > 0 {
65			return code[:i]
66		}
67		return code
68	}
69
```

1	package engine

3	import (
4		"fmt"

8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- If the source language equals the target language, return the text unchanged (lightly
31	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
32	
33	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
34	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
35	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
36	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
37	
38	Rules:
39	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
40	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
41	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
42	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
43	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
44	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
45	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
46	
47	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
48	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
49	that also gives brief usage examples.
50	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
51	
52	Rules:
53	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
54	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
55	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
56	- Format each example on two lines:
57	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
58	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
59	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
60	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
61	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
62	
63	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
64	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
65		switch preset {
66		case PresetContextual:
67			return translateSystemPromptContextual
68		case PresetDictionary:
69			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
70		default:
71			return translateSystemPromptConcise
72		}
73	}
74	
75	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
76	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
77	Target language: %s
78	
79	Text:
80	%s`
81	
82	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
83	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
84	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
85	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
86		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
87		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
88			src = "auto"
89		} else {
90			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
91		}
92		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
93		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
94		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
95			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
96		}
97		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
98	}
99

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
9	)
10	
11	// SmartDictConfig parameterizes the smart-dict engine.
12	type SmartDictConfig struct {
13		// CloseDistance is the English edit distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
14		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and returned as-is; a farther match (and
15		// any hard miss) triggers the LLM fallback. 0 disables the typo shortcut, so
16		// every non-exact lookup falls back.
17		CloseDistance int
18		// Preset is the LLM prompt style used for the fallback; "" => PresetDictionary
19		// (a gloss plus example sentences).
20		Preset string
21	}
22	
23	// SmartDictEngine composes a dictionary engine with an LLM: it answers from the
24	// dictionary when it can, and falls back to the LLM when the word is missing or the
25	// fuzzy match is too far off. It is a distinct, selectable engine — the plain
26	// dictionary engine is left untouched.
27	//
28	// This is the first engine to branch on a produced result's quality (the Chain
29	// only fails over on hard errors), so the fallback always stamps a Warning: the
30	// downgrade from an offline lookup to an LLM answer is never silent.
31	type SmartDictEngine struct {
32		dict Engine
33		llm  Engine
34		cfg  SmartDictConfig
35	}
36	
37	// NewSmartDict builds a smart-dict engine over a dictionary and an LLM backend.
38	func NewSmartDict(dict, llm Engine, cfg SmartDictConfig) *SmartDictEngine {
39		return &SmartDictEngine{dict: dict, llm: llm, cfg: cfg}
40	}
41	
42	// Name returns "smart-dict".
43	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Name() string { return "smart-dict" }
44	
45	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups.
46	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
47	
48	// Detect is not meaningful for a dictionary engine.
49	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
50		return "", nil
51	}
52	
53	// Available reports true when either backend can serve (a dictionary hit, or the
54	// LLM fallback).
55	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
56		return e.dict.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
57	}
58	
59	// Translate looks the word up in the dictionary and, on a miss or a too-weak fuzzy
60	// match, falls back to the LLM (streaming preserved).
61	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
62		dictCh, err := e.dict.Translate(ctx, req)
63		if err != nil {
64			// A setup failure (e.g. empty input) — nothing to fall back to.
65			return nil, err
66		}
67	
68		out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
69		go func() {
70			defer close(out)
71			// The dictionary engines are single-shot (no token stream), so drain fully
72			// before deciding whether the answer is good enough.
73			res, derr := Drain(dictCh, nil)
74			if e.dictAnswered(res, derr) {
75				out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
76				return
77			}
78			e.fallback(ctx, req, derr, out)
79		}()
80		return out, nil
81	}
82	
83	// dictAnswered reports whether the dictionary produced a result good enough to
84	// return without asking the LLM: an exact entry, or a fuzzy suggestion close enough
85	// to be a likely typo (within CloseDistance).
86	func (e *SmartDictEngine) dictAnswered(res *TranslateResult, derr error) bool {
87		if derr != nil || res == nil {
88			return false // hard miss (ErrNoDictEntry) or transport error → fall back
89		}
90		if res.Dictionary != nil {
91			return true // exact hit
92		}
93		// A close typo (distance known and within threshold) stays as "did you mean".
94		// Unknown distance (0, e.g. Chinese prefix matches) counts as too far → fall back.
95		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 && res.SuggestDistance != 0 && res.SuggestDistance <= e.cfg.CloseDistance {
96			return true
97		}
98		return false // suggestions too far, "not installed" notes, or empty → fall back
99	}
100	
101	// fallback runs the LLM in translate mode and pipes its chunks to out, stamping a
102	// warning on the terminal result so the downgrade is visible.
103	func (e *SmartDictEngine) fallback(ctx context.Context, req Request, derr error, out chan<- Chunk) {
104		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
105	
106		r := req
107		r.Mode = ModeTranslate
108		r.Preset = e.cfg.Preset
109		if r.Preset == "" {
110			r.Preset = PresetDictionary
111		}
112		r.Target = smartTarget(req.Text, req.Target)
113	
114		llmCh, err := e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
115		if err != nil {
116			out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("smart-dict: no dictionary entry for %q and LLM fallback failed: %w", word, err)}
117			return
118		}
119		for ch := range llmCh {
120			if ch.Kind == ChunkDone && ch.Result != nil {
121				ch.Result.Warnings = append(ch.Result.Warnings,
122					fmt.Sprintf("no dictionary entry for %q — defined via %s (LLM)", word, ch.Result.Engine))
123			}
124			out <- ch
125		}
126	}
127	
128	// smartTarget picks the LLM fallback's target language, mirroring the bilingual
129	// dictionary (Chinese ↔ English): Chinese input → English; English/other input →
130	// the caller's target, or Chinese when that target is English/unset.
131	func smartTarget(text, target string) string {
132		if lang.IsChinese(text) {
133			return "en"
134		}
135		t := strings.TrimSpace(target)
136		if t == "" || t == "auto" || strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(t), "en") {
137			return "zh"
138		}
139		return t
140	}
141

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"github.com/atotto/clipboard"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
15	)
16	
17	// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
18	// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
19	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
20		switch msg := msg.(type) {
21		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
22			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
23			m.relayout()
24			m.ready = true
25			return m, nil
26	
27		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
28			return m.handleKey(msg)
29	
30		case debounceMsg:
31			if msg.seq != m.seq {
32				return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
33			}
34			return m.launch(false)
35	
36		case historyLoadedMsg:
37			m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))
38			return m, nil
39	
40		case modelsLoadedMsg:
41			m.modelsLoading = false
42			m.modelsErr = msg.err
43			m.cachedModels = msg.models
44			m.modelList.SetItems(modelItems(msg.models, m.currentModelID()))
45			return m, nil
46	
47		case tea.MouseWheelMsg:
48			return m.handleMouseWheel(msg)
49	
50		case tea.MouseClickMsg:
51			return m.handleMouseClick(msg)
52	
53		case spinner.TickMsg:
54			var cmd tea.Cmd
55			m.sp, cmd = m.sp.Update(msg)
56			// Animate the placeholder inside the (fixed-height) viewport so the
57			// layout never jumps while waiting for the first token.
58			if m.status == statusTranslating && m.streamBuf == "" {
59				m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder())
60			}
61			return m, cmd
62	
63		case flashClearMsg:
64			m.flash = ""
65			return m, nil
66	
67		case streamMsg:
68			return m.handleStream(msg)
69		}
70	
71		var cmd tea.Cmd
72		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
73		return m, cmd
74	}
75	
76	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
77	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
78	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
79	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
80		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
81			return m, nil
82		}
83		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
84		case engine.ChunkToken:
85			m.retrying = false // real tokens are flowing; drop the "retrying" label
86			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
87			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
88			m.vp.GotoBottom()
89			return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token
90	
91		case engine.ChunkDone:
92			m.inflight = 0
93			m.status = statusDone
94			m.err = nil
95			m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey // remember what was translated (skip re-runs)
96			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
97				r := *msg.chunk.Result
98				// A truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is re-fired once
99				// automatically — a fresh request almost always completes. Only after a
100				// second truncation do we settle on the partial text + ⚠.
101				if r.Truncated && m.autoRetryKey != m.pendingKey {
102					m.autoRetryKey = m.pendingKey
103					nm, cmd := m.launch(true) // force a fresh fetch of the same input
104					mm := nm.(Model)
105					mm.retrying = true
106					mm.vp.SetContent(mm.translatingPlaceholder())
107					return mm, cmd
108				}
109				m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{} // settled (complete, or gave up after the retry)
110				m.retrying = false
111				m.result = msg.chunk.Result
112				// A truncated result keeps its partial text on screen (with a ⚠), but
113				// must not be cached or persisted as if it were the real answer — so a
114				// manual retry (Enter) actually re-fetches instead of replaying it.
115				if !r.Truncated {
116					m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result // write-through (force overwrites)
117				}
118				if r.Engine != "" {
119					m.curEngine = r.Engine
120				}
121				if r.Model != "" {
122					m.curModel = r.Model
123				}
124				m.relayout() // engine/model segment width may have changed
125				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(r))
126				m.vp.GotoTop() // show a completed result from its start, not scrolled
127				// Don't persist a truncated result, or a suggestions-only result
128				// (empty translation, no entry).
129				if !r.Truncated && (r.Dictionary != nil || r.Translation != "") {
130					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
131				}
132				return m, nil
133			}
134			return m, nil
135	
136		case engine.ChunkError:
137			m.inflight = 0
138			m.retrying = false
139			m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{}
140			m.status = statusError
141			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
142			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
143			return m, nil
144		}
145		return m, nil
146	}
147	
148	func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
149		// An open overlay (history / language / model picker) captures keys.
150		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
151			switch msg.String() {
152			case "ctrl+c":
153				m.cancelInflight()
154				return m, tea.Quit
155			case "esc":
156				m.overlay = overlayNone
157				return m, nil
158			case "enter":
159				return m.overlaySelect()
160			}
161			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
162			return m, cmd
163		}
164	
165		switch {
166		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
167			m.cancelInflight()
168			return m, tea.Quit
169	
170		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):
171			m.overlay = overlayHistory
172			return m, loadHistoryCmd(m.p.Store)
173	
174		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickLang):
175			m.overlay = overlayLang
176			return m, nil
177	
178		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickModel):
179			if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
180				return m, nil // no model concept in dictionary mode
181			}
182			return m.openModelPicker()
183	
184		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickPreset):
185			if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
186				return m, nil // presets are LLM translate styles
187			}
188			m.presetList.SetItems(presetItems(m.preset))
189			m.overlay = overlayPreset
190			return m, nil
191	
192		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.TogglePair):
193			m.pair = !m.pair
194			if m.pair && m.pairWith == "" {
195				m.pairWith = "en"
196			}
197			m.relayout() // footer pair segment changed
198			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
199				return m.launch(false)
200			}
201			m.clearResult()
202			return m, nil
203	
204		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):
205			if txt := m.copyText(); txt != "" {
206				if err := clipboard.WriteAll(txt); err != nil {
207					m.flash = "copy failed"
208				} else {
209					m.flash = "copied ✓"
210				}
211				return m, flashCmd()
212			}
213			return m, nil
214	
215		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
216			m.live = !m.live
217			return m, nil
218	
219		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine):
220			if n := len(m.p.Engines); n > 1 {
221				m.engIdx = (m.engIdx + 1) % n
222				m.curEngine, m.curModel = "", ""
223				m.relayout() // footer content (engine/mode) changed
224				// Only re-translate automatically in live mode; otherwise switch the
225				// engine, clear the now-stale result, and wait for Enter.
226				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
227					return m.launch(true)
228				}
229				m.clearResult()
230			}
231			return m, nil
232	
233		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
234			m.cancelInflight()
235			m.seq++
236			m.ta.Reset()
237			m.result = nil
238			m.err = nil
239			m.streamBuf = ""
240			m.status = statusIdle
241			m.vp.SetContent("")
242			return m, nil
243	
244		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
245			// After a dictionary miss the input still equals the missed word, so a
246			// second Enter means "help me choose" — open the ranked suggestions.
247			if m.result != nil && len(m.result.Suggestions) > 0 &&
248				strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == m.lastInput {
249				m.suggestList.SetItems(suggestItems(m.result.Suggestions))
250				m.overlay = overlaySuggest
251				return m, nil
252			}
253			return m.launch(true)
254		}
255	
256		// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
257		var cmd tea.Cmd
258		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
259	
260		if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
261			// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
262			m.cancelInflight()
263			m.seq++
264			m.result = nil
265			m.streamBuf = ""
266			m.status = statusIdle
267			m.vp.SetContent("")
268			return m, cmd
269		}
270	
271		if m.live {
272			m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
273			m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
274			m.status = statusTyping
275			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
276		}
277		return m, cmd
278	}
279	
280	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
281	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
282	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
283	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
284		label := "translating…"
285		if m.retrying {
286			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
287		}
288		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
289	}
290	
291	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
292	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
293	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
294	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
295	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
296		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
297		if text == "" {
298			m.status = statusIdle
299			return m, nil
300		}
301		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
302		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
303			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
304		}
305		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
306		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
307		if !force {
308			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
309				m.cancelInflight()
310				m.status = statusDone
311				m.err = nil
312				m.result = cached
313				if cached.Engine != "" {
314					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
315				}
316				if cached.Model != "" {
317					m.curModel = cached.Model
318				}
319				m.streamBuf = ""
320				m.lastInput = text
321				m.lastDoneKey = key
322				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
323				return m, nil
324			}
325		}
326		m.seq++
327		if m.cancel != nil {
328			m.cancel()
329		}
330		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
331		m.cancel = cancel
332		m.inflight = m.seq
333		m.status = statusTranslating
334		m.streamBuf = ""
335		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
336		m.err = nil
337		m.result = nil
338		m.lastInput = text
339		m.pendingKey = key
340		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
341	
342		seq := m.seq
343		ne := m.active()
344		target := m.target
345		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate {
346			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
347		}
348		req := engine.Request{
349			Text:          text,
350			Source:        m.source,
351			Target:        target,
352			Mode:          ne.Mode,
353			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
354			Model:         m.modelOverride,
355			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
356			Preset:        m.preset,
357			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
358		}
359		ch, err := ne.Engine.Translate(ctx, req)
360		if err != nil {
361			e := err
362			return m, func() tea.Msg {
363				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
364			}
365		}
366		m.stream = ch
367		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
368	}
369	
370	// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
371	// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
372	func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
373		if m.cancel != nil {
374			m.cancel()
375			m.cancel = nil
376		}
377		m.inflight = 0
378	}
379	
380	// clearResult drops the shown result. Used when a setting (engine/lang/model/
381	// style/pair) changes without an immediate re-translate, so the pane never shows
382	// output that's stale relative to the (now different) footer.
383	func (m *Model) clearResult() {
384		m.cancelInflight()
385		m.result = nil
386		m.streamBuf = ""
387		m.err = nil
388		m.status = statusIdle
389		m.vp.SetContent("")
390	}
391	
392	// copyText returns the text to place on the clipboard for the current result.
393	func (m Model) copyText() string {
394		if m.result == nil {
395			return ""
396		}
397		return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
398	}
399	
400	// currentModelID is the model id currently in effect (for the ✓ marker).
401	func (m Model) currentModelID() string {
402		if m.modelOverride != "" {
403			return m.modelOverride
404		}
405		return m.curModel
406	}
407	
408	// openModelPicker opens the model overlay, fetching the model list once per session.
409	func (m Model) openModelPicker() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
410		m.overlay = overlayModel
411		if m.cachedModels == nil && !m.modelsLoading && m.modelsErr == nil {
412			m.modelsLoading = true
413			return m, fetchModelsCmd(m.p.ModelSource)
414		}
415		return m, nil
416	}
417	
418	// overlayListUpdate forwards a message to the active overlay's list.
419	func (m *Model) overlayListUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
420		var cmd tea.Cmd
421		switch m.overlay {
422		case overlayHistory:
423			m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)
424		case overlayLang:
425			m.langList, cmd = m.langList.Update(msg)
426		case overlayModel:
427			m.modelList, cmd = m.modelList.Update(msg)
428		case overlaySuggest:
429			m.suggestList, cmd = m.suggestList.Update(msg)
430		case overlayPreset:
431			m.presetList, cmd = m.presetList.Update(msg)
432		}
433		return cmd
434	}
435	
436	// overlaySelect applies the highlighted overlay item and closes the overlay.
437	func (m Model) overlaySelect() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
438		switch m.overlay {
439		case overlayHistory:
440			// Recall is an explicit "show me this" action: always translate.
441			if it, ok := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem); ok {
442				m.ta.SetValue(it.rec.Input)
443				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
444				m.overlay = overlayNone
445				return m.launch(true)
446			}
447		case overlayLang:
448			if it, ok := m.langList.SelectedItem().(langItem); ok {
449				m.target = it.code
450				m.overlay = overlayNone
451				m.relayout() // pair segment width changed
452				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
453					return m.launch(true)
454				}
455				m.clearResult()
456				return m, nil
457			}
458		case overlayModel:
459			if it, ok := m.modelList.SelectedItem().(modelItem); ok {
460				m.modelOverride = it.id
461				m.overlay = overlayNone
462				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
463					return m.launch(true)
464				}
465				m.clearResult()
466				return m, nil
467			}
468		case overlaySuggest:
469			// Picking a suggestion updates the input and looks it up.
470			if it, ok := m.suggestList.SelectedItem().(suggestItem); ok {
471				m.ta.SetValue(it.word)
472				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
473				m.overlay = overlayNone
474				return m.launch(true)
475			}
476		case overlayPreset:
477			if it, ok := m.presetList.SelectedItem().(presetItem); ok {
478				m.preset = it.id
479				m.overlay = overlayNone
480				m.relayout() // style segment width changed
481				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
482					return m.launch(false)
483				}
484				m.clearResult()
485				return m, nil
486			}
487		}
488		m.overlay = overlayNone
489		return m, nil
490	}
491	
492	// handleMouseWheel scrolls the active overlay list, or the result viewport.
493	func (m Model) handleMouseWheel(msg tea.MouseWheelMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
494		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
495			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
496			return m, cmd
497		}
498		var cmd tea.Cmd
499		m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
500		return m, cmd
501	}
502	
503	// handleMouseClick routes clicks. In an overlay it forwards to the list; in
504	// normal mode a click on the footer row opens the language picker (like clicking
505	// the language selector in a translator UI).
506	func (m Model) handleMouseClick(msg tea.MouseClickMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
507		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
508			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
509			return m, cmd
510		}
511		if m.height > 0 && msg.Y == m.height-1 {
512			m.overlay = overlayLang
513			return m, nil
514		}
515		return m, nil
516	}
517	
518	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
519	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
520		src := m.source
521		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
522			src = res.DetectedSource
523		}
524		target := m.target
525		if res.Target != "" {
526			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
527		}
528		return store.Record{
529			SourceLang:   src,
530			TargetLang:   target,
531			Engine:       res.Engine,
532			Model:        res.Model,
533			Input:        m.lastInput,
534			Output:       res.Translation,
535			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
536			Notes:        res.Notes,
537		}
538	}
539	
540	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. The input/result
541	// boxes are rendered at .Width(m.width-2); lipgloss treats that as the TOTAL
542	// width, so the inner content area is that minus the box frame (border 2 +
543	// padding 2 = 4) = m.width-6. Wrapping content to anything wider makes each line
544	// overflow the box and get re-wrapped, orphaning a character per line.
545	func (m *Model) relayout() {
546		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
547			return
548		}
549		const inputH = 4
550		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
551		compW := m.width - 6
552		if compW < 1 {
553			compW = 1
554		}
555		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
556		if resultH < 3 {
557			resultH = 3
558		}
559		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
560		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
561		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
562		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
563		m.resultH = resultH
564		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
565		if overlayH < 1 {
566			overlayH = 1
567		}
568		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
569		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
570		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
571		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
572		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
573	}
574

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
8		lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
12	)
13	
14	// View renders the two-box layout (input, result) plus a status/help footer.
15	// AltScreen + mouse are set declaratively (the v2 way).
16	func (m Model) View() tea.View {
17		if !m.ready {
18			return altView("initializing…")
19		}
20		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
21			return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, m.overlayBody(), m.overlayFooter()))
22		}
23	
24		inStyle := m.st.input
25		if m.ta.Focused() {
26			inStyle = m.st.inputHi
27		}
28		input := inStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.ta.View())
29	
30		// The result box is kept at a fixed height (viewport height) so the layout
31		// never jumps between the "translating…" placeholder and streamed output.
32		result := m.st.result.Width(m.width - 2).Height(m.resultH).Render(m.vp.View())
33		footer := m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.statusLine())
34	
35		return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, input, result, footer))
36	}
37	
38	// overlayBody renders the active picker's list (or its loading/empty state).
39	func (m Model) overlayBody() string {
40		switch m.overlay {
41		case overlayHistory:
42			return m.hist.View()
43		case overlayLang:
44			return m.langList.View()
45		case overlayModel:
46			switch {
47			case m.modelsLoading:
48				return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("loading models…")
49			case m.modelsErr != nil:
50				return m.st.warn.Render("⚠ " + m.modelsErr.Error())
51			case len(m.cachedModels) == 0:
52				return m.st.warn.Render("no models available (is the provider up?)")
53			default:
54				return m.modelList.View()
55			}
56		case overlaySuggest:
57			return m.suggestList.View()
58		case overlayPreset:
59			return m.presetList.View()
60		}
61		return ""
62	}
63	
64	func (m Model) overlayFooter() string {
65		hint := "↵ select  esc close  ^c quit"
66		switch m.overlay {
67		case overlayHistory:
68			hint = "↵ recall  esc close  ^c quit"
69		case overlaySuggest:
70			hint = "↵ look up  esc close  ^c quit"
71		}
72		return m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.st.dim.Render(hint))
73	}
74	
75	// altView wraps content in a full-screen (alt-screen) tea.View with mouse on.
76	func altView(content string) tea.View {
77		v := tea.NewView(content)
78		v.AltScreen = true
79		v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
80		return v
81	}
82	
83	// statusLine renders the footer with the current transient state.
84	func (m Model) statusLine() string { return m.footerContent(false) }
85	
86	// footerHeight measures how many rows the footer occupies at the current width.
87	// It reserves the widest transient state ("translating") so the layout never
88	// jumps when that segment appears mid-flight.
89	func (m Model) footerHeight() int {
90		if m.width <= 0 {
91			return 1
92		}
93		return lg.Height(m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.footerContent(true)))
94	}
95	
96	// footerContent builds the footer string. forceState reserves the transient
97	// "translating" segment (for height measurement); otherwise the actual state is
98	// shown. lipgloss word-wraps it to the width, so narrow terminals get 2+ rows.
99	func (m Model) footerContent(forceState bool) string {
100		live := m.st.liveOff.Render("live○")
101		if m.live {
102			live = m.st.liveOn.Render("live●")
103		}
104	
105		var state string
106		if forceState {
107			state = m.st.dim.Render("⠿ translating")
108		} else if m.flash != "" {
109			state = m.st.liveOn.Render(m.flash)
110		} else {
111			switch m.status {
112			case statusTranslating:
113				state = m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("translating")
114			case statusError:
115				state = m.st.errText.Render("error")
116			}
117		}
118	
119		// Dictionary mode: no source→target pair (English-only lookups per script).
120		if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
121			left := strings.Join([]string{
122				m.st.label.Render("dictionary (zh↔en)"),
123				live,
124			}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
125			if state != "" {
126				left += "  " + state
127			}
128			return left + "  " + m.st.dim.Render("↵ define  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^r history  ^c quit")
129		}
130	
131		pair := fmt.Sprintf("%s→%s", lang.Name(m.source), lang.Name(m.target))
132		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
133			pair = fmt.Sprintf("pair %s⇄%s", m.target, m.pairWith) // compact codes
134		}
135	
136		// Engine segment: the selected engine name, plus the model that actually
137		// served the last result (once known).
138		engineSeg := m.active().Name
139		if m.curEngine != "" && m.curEngine != m.active().Name {
140			engineSeg = m.curEngine // chain fell back to a specific engine
141		}
142		if m.curModel != "" {
143			engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", engineSeg, m.curModel)
144		}
145	
146		left := strings.Join([]string{
147			m.st.label.Render(pair),
148			m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg),
149			m.st.dim.Render("style:" + m.preset),
150			live,
151		}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
152		if state != "" {
153			left += "  " + state
154		}
155	
156		help := m.st.dim.Render("↵ translate  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^t lang  ^p model  ^o style  ^g pair  ^r history  ^c quit")
157		return left + "  " + help
158	}
159	
160	// renderResult formats a completed translation, a dictionary entry, or a ranked
161	// "did you mean" list when a dictionary lookup missed.
162	func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
163		if res.Dictionary != nil {
164			return m.renderDictionary(res)
165		}
166		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
167			return m.renderSuggestions(res)
168		}
169		var b strings.Builder
170		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))
171	
172		if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
173			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
174		}
175		for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
176			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
177		}
178		if res.Notes != "" {
179			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
180		}
181		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
182			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
183		}
184		// A truncation warning already tells the user to press Enter to retry; the
185		// engine-switch hint only fits chain-fallback warnings, so skip it there.
186		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && !res.Truncated {
187			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("(^e switch engine · check the model/provider)"))
188		}
189		return b.String()
190	}
191	
192	// renderDictionary formats a dictionary lookup for the result pane.
193	func (m Model) renderDictionary(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
194		d := res.Dictionary
195		var b strings.Builder
196		head := d.Word
197		if d.Phonetic != "" {
198			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
199		}
200		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(head))
201		for _, mn := range d.Meanings {
202			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(mn.PartOfSpeech))
203			for i, def := range mn.Definitions {
204				if i >= 3 {
205					break
206				}
207				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("• "+def.Text))
208			}
209		}
210		return b.String()
211	}
212	
213	// renderSuggestions renders the ranked "did you mean" list for a dictionary miss.
214	func (m Model) renderSuggestions(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
215		var b strings.Builder
216		b.WriteString(m.st.dim.Render("no exact match — did you mean:"))
217		for i, w := range res.Suggestions {
218			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d.", i+1)) + " " + m.st.alt.Render(w))
219		}
220		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("↵ choose"))
221		return b.String()
222	}
223
```
</details></tool-use>

19	func newInitCmd() *cobra.Command {
20		return &cobra.Command{
21			Use:   "init",
22			Short: "Interactive configuration wizard (probes available providers)",
23			RunE:  runInit,
24		}
25	}
26	
27	func runInit(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
28		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
29		if err != nil {
30			return err
31		}
32	
33		// Probe providers so the form can show what's reachable.
34		copilotUp := probeProvider(cfg, "copilot")
35		ollamaUp := probeProvider(cfg, "ollama")
36		openrouterKey := os.Getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY") != ""
37	
38		engineChoice := cfg.General.Engine
39		target := cfg.General.DefaultTarget
40		live := cfg.General.LiveTranslate
41		preset := cfg.General.Preset
42		if preset == "" {
43			preset = engine.PresetContextual
44		}
45		instructions := cfg.General.Instructions
46		pair := cfg.General.Pair
47		pairWith := cfg.General.PairWith
48		if pairWith == "" {
49			pairWith = "en"
50		}
51	
52		form := huh.NewForm(
53			huh.NewGroup(
54				huh.NewSelect[string]().
55					Title("Default engine").
56					Description("Auto tries each provider in order and falls back.").
57					Options(
58						huh.NewOption("Auto fallback chain (recommended)", "auto"),
59						huh.NewOption(probeLabel("copilot-proxy", copilotUp), "copilot"),
60						huh.NewOption(probeLabel("Ollama (offline)", ollamaUp), "ollama"),
61						huh.NewOption("Google (free, translate only)", "google"),
62					).
63					Value(&engineChoice),
64	
65				huh.NewSelect[string]().
66					Title("Default target language").
67					Description("Type to filter; auto-detect is always the source.").
68					Options(targetLangOptions()...).
69					Height(12).
70					Filtering(true).
71					Value(&target),
72	
73				huh.NewSelect[string]().
74					Title("Translation style").
75					Description("How the LLM formats translations.").
76					Options(
77						huh.NewOption("contextual — translations across common senses", engine.PresetContextual),
78						huh.NewOption("concise — terse direct translation", engine.PresetConcise),
79						huh.NewOption("dictionary — translation + example sentences", engine.PresetDictionary),
80					).
81					Value(&preset),
82	
83				huh.NewText().
84					Title("Custom instructions (optional)").
85					Description("Extra guidance for the LLM, e.g. domain focus: quantitative finance + computer science.").
86					Value(&instructions),
87	
88				huh.NewConfirm().
89					Title("Live translate?").
90					Description("Auto-translate as you type. Off by default to avoid spamming the API.").
91					Value(&live),
92	
93				huh.NewConfirm().
94					Title("Bidirectional pair mode?").
95					Description("When you write in your target language, translate it to another instead. e.g. others → zh-TW, but zh → en.").
96					Value(&pair),
97	
98				huh.NewSelect[string]().
99					Title("…pair with (used only if pair mode is on)").
100					Description("Where your target-language input goes.").
101					Options(targetLangOptions()...).
102					Height(10).
103					Filtering(true).
104					Value(&pairWith),
105			),
106		)
107		if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110	
111		cfg.General.Engine = engineChoice
112		cfg.General.DefaultTarget = target
113		cfg.General.LiveTranslate = live
114		cfg.General.Preset = preset
115		cfg.General.Instructions = strings.TrimSpace(instructions)
116		cfg.General.Pair = pair
117		cfg.General.PairWith = pairWith
118		// Refresh copilot model ids to the verified-working recommendations (repairs
119		// configs written before a model id changed / was un-served by the proxy).
120		if p := cfg.ProviderByName("copilot"); p != nil {
121			p.Model, p.ModelFast, p.ModelMax = config.ModelDefault, config.ModelFast, config.ModelMax
122		}
123		if err := config.Save(cfg); err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126	
127		fmt.Printf("Saved %s\n  engine=%s  target=%s  live=%v\n", config.Path(), engineChoice, target, live)
128		if engineChoice == "copilot" && !copilotUp {
129			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: copilot-proxy is not reachable — start it with `copilot-proxy start`.")
130		}
131		if engineChoice == "ollama" && !ollamaUp {
132			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: Ollama is not reachable — start it and `ollama pull llama3.2:3b`.")
133		}
134		if openrouterKey {
135			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: OPENROUTER_API_KEY detected — the openrouter provider is available.")
136		}
137		return nil
138	}
139	
140	// targetLangOptions builds the language dropdown options (name + code).
141	func targetLangOptions() []huh.Option[string] {
142		ls := lang.List()
143		opts := make([]huh.Option[string], 0, len(ls))
144		for _, l := range ls {
145			opts = append(opts, huh.NewOption(fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", l.Name, l.Code), l.Code))
146		}
147		return opts
148	}
149	
150	func probeLabel(name string, up bool) string {
151		if up {
152			return name + "  ● up"
153		}
154		return name + "  ○ down"
155	}
156	
157	func probeProvider(cfg *config.Config, name string) bool {
158		p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
159		if p == nil {
160			return false
161		}
162		return probeURL(strings.TrimRight(p.BaseURL, "/")+"/models", 1500*time.Millisecond)
163	}
164	
165	func probeURL(url string, timeout time.Duration) bool {
166		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
167		defer cancel()
168		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
169		if err != nil {
170			return false
171		}
172		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
173		if err != nil {
174			return false
175		}
176		defer resp.Body.Close()
177		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
178	}

 `./cmd/build.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
10	)
11	
12	// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
13	// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
14	// a named provider builds a single LLM engine. (google/dict engines join the
15	// chain in later slices.)
16	func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
17		cfg := res.Cfg
18	
19		if res.Engine != "auto" {
20			switch res.Engine {
21			case "google":
22				return googleFromConfig(cfg), nil
23			case "llm":
24				// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
25			}
26			if res.Provider != nil {
27				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
28			}
29			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
30		}
31	
32		var engines []engine.Engine
33		for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
34			switch {
35			case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
36				p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
37				engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
38			case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
39				engines = append(engines, googleFromConfig(cfg))
40			}
41			// "dict" is wired in a later slice.
42		}
43		if len(engines) == 0 {
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
45		}
46		if len(engines) == 1 {
47			return engines[0], nil
48		}
49		return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
50	}
51	
52	// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
53	func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
54		if model == "" {
55			model = p.Model
56		}
57		return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
58			Name:      p.Name,
59			BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
60			Model:     model,
61			APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
62		})
63	}
64	
65	// googleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
66	func googleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
67		return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
68			Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
69			ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
70			UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
71			Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
72		})
73	}
74	
75	// dictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
76	// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
77	func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
78		if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
79			return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
80				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
81				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
82				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
83				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
84			})
85		}
86		var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
87		if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
88			apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
89				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
90				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
91				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
92				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
93			})
94		}
95		return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
96			Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
97			CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
98			EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
99			AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
100			Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
101			APIFallback:  apiFb,
102		})
103	}
104	
105	// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
106	// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
107	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
108	func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
109		cfg := res.Cfg
110		return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
111			CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
112			Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
113		})
114	}
115	
116	// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
117	// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
118	// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
119	// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
120	func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
121		cfg := res.Cfg
122		primaryName := res.Engine
123		if primaryName == "" {
124			primaryName = "auto"
125		}
126		set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
127		if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
128			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: googleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
129		}
130		if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
131			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: dictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
132			if res.Provider != nil {
133				set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: smartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
134			}
135		}
136		return set
137	}
138
```

 `./internal/tui/model.go`

```text
1	// Package tui implements the interactive Bubble Tea (v2, charm.land/*) front-end.
2	// It imports the shared engine layer but the engine layer never imports it.
3	package tui
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"time"
8	
9		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
10		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/list"
11		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
12		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea"
13		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
14		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
18	)
19	
20	// NamedEngine is one selectable engine in the TUI's ^e cycle.
21	type NamedEngine struct {
22		Name   string // "auto", "google", "dictionary", …
23		Engine engine.Engine
24		Mode   engine.Mode
25	}
26	
27	// Params configures a TUI session.
28	type Params struct {
29		Engines       []NamedEngine      // selectable engines; index 0 is the default
30		ModelSource   engine.ModelLister // fetches the model list for the model picker (may be nil)
31		ModelProvider string             // provider name the model override applies to
32		Store         store.Store        // nil when history is disabled
33		Source        string
34		Target        string
35		Pair          bool   // bidirectional pair mode
36		PairWith      string // the "away" language (resolved code)
37		Model         string // display model id for the footer (initial)
38		Preset        string // LLM prompt style
39		Instructions  string // extra system-prompt guidance
40		Live          bool   // live-debounce default state
41		DebounceMs    int
42	}
43	
44	type status int
45	
46	const (
47		statusIdle status = iota
48		statusTyping
49		statusTranslating
50		statusDone
51		statusError
52	)
53	
54	// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
55	// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
56	type Model struct {
57		p    Params
58		base context.Context
59	
60		ta          textarea.Model
61		vp          viewport.Model
62		hist        list.Model
63		langList    list.Model
64		modelList   list.Model
65		suggestList list.Model
66		presetList  list.Model
67		sp          spinner.Model
68		keys        keyMap
69		st          styles
70	
71		width, height int
72		ready         bool
73		overlay       overlayKind
74		resultH       int // result viewport height (kept fixed to avoid layout jumps)
75	
76		source   string
77		target   string
78		pair     bool
79		pairWith string
80		preset   string
81		live     bool
82		engIdx   int // index into p.Engines (the active engine)
83	
84		// model picker state (session-cached)
85		cachedModels  []string
86		modelOverride string
87		modelsLoading bool
88		modelsErr     error
89	
90		// curEngine/curModel reflect the engine that actually served the last
91		// result (may differ from the selected engine when the auto chain falls back).
92		curEngine string
93		curModel  string
94	
95		status status
96		result *engine.TranslateResult
97		err    error
98		flash  string // transient footer notice (e.g. "copied ✓")
99	
100		// request lifecycle: one monotonic seq drives debounce-collapse, cancel, and
101		// stale-stream-drop. cancel/stream/streamBuf belong to the in-flight request.
102		// streamBuf is a plain string (NOT strings.Builder): the model is copied by
103		// value on every Update, and copying a used Builder panics.
104		seq         int
105		inflight    int
106		cancel      context.CancelFunc
107		stream      <-chan engine.Chunk
108		streamBuf   string
109		debounce    time.Duration
110		lastInput   string     // input text that produced the in-flight/last request
111		lastDoneKey cacheKey   // key of the last COMPLETED result (skip identical re-runs)
112		pendingKey  cacheKey   // cache key of the in-flight request
113		cache       cacheStore // session result cache (live cache-hit = instant, no API call)
114	
115		// Truncation auto-retry: a truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is
116		// re-fired once automatically, since a fresh request almost always completes.
117		// autoRetryKey is the pendingKey we've already retried once (so we retry at
118		// most once per input); retrying drives the "retrying" placeholder label.
119		autoRetryKey cacheKey
120		retrying     bool
121	}
122	
123	// active returns the currently selected engine.
124	func (m Model) active() NamedEngine {
125		if len(m.p.Engines) == 0 {
126			return NamedEngine{}
127		}
128		return m.p.Engines[m.engIdx%len(m.p.Engines)]
129	}
130	
131	// New builds a TUI model.
132	func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
133		ta := textarea.New()
134		ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
135		ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
136		ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
137		// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
138		ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
139		ta.Focus()
140	
141		debounce := time.Duration(p.DebounceMs) * time.Millisecond
142		if debounce <= 0 {
143			debounce = 400 * time.Millisecond
144		}
145	
146		hist := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
147		hist.Title = "History (↵ recall · esc close)"
148		hist.SetShowHelp(false)
149	
150		langList := list.New(langItems(), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
151		langList.Title = "Target language (↵ select · esc close)"
152		langList.SetShowHelp(false)
153	
154		modelList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
155		modelList.Title = "Model (↵ select · esc close)"
156		modelList.SetShowHelp(false)
157	
158		suggestList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
159		suggestList.Title = "Did you mean? (↵ look up · esc close)"
160		suggestList.SetShowHelp(false)
161	
162		preset := p.Preset
163		if preset == "" {
164			preset = engine.PresetConcise
165		}
166		presetList := list.New(presetItems(preset), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
167		presetList.Title = "Style (↵ select · esc close)"
168		presetList.SetShowHelp(false)
169	
170		sp := spinner.New()
171		sp.Spinner = spinner.MiniDot
172	
173		// Soft-wrap long paragraph lines to the viewport width; without this a single
174		// long (unwrapped) translation line is clipped and its tail is lost on screen.
175		vp := viewport.New()
176		vp.SoftWrap = true
177	
178		return Model{
179			p:           p,
180			base:        ctx,
181			ta:          ta,
182			vp:          vp,
183			hist:        hist,
184			langList:    langList,
185			modelList:   modelList,
186			suggestList: suggestList,
187			presetList:  presetList,
188			sp:          sp,
189			keys:        defaultKeys(),
190			st:          newStyles(),
191			source:      p.Source,
192			target:      p.Target,
193			pair:        p.Pair,
194			pairWith:    p.PairWith,
195			preset:      preset,
196			live:        p.Live,
197			status:      statusIdle,
198			debounce:    debounce,
199			cache:       cacheStore{},
200		}
201	}
202	
203	// Pair returns the current source and target language codes (read by the caller
204	// after the program exits to persist the last pair).
205	func (m Model) Pair() (source, target string) { return m.source, m.target }
206	
207	// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink + spinner.
208	func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
209		return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, m.sp.Tick, tea.RequestWindowSize)
210	}
211
```

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"strings"
13	
14		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15		"golang.org/x/term"
16	
17		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
18	
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
26	)
27	
28	var (
29		flagTo           string
30		flagFrom         string
31		flagModel        string
32		flagProvider     string
33		flagEngine       string
34		flagTier         string
35		flagPreset       string
36		flagInstructions string
37		flagPair         bool
38		flagPairWith     string
39		flagJSON         bool
40		flagNoHistory    bool
41	)
42	
43	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
44	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
45		root := &cobra.Command{
46			Use:   "translate [text...]",
47			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
48			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
49				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
50				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
51				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
52			Version:       buildVersion(),
53			SilenceUsage:  true,
54			SilenceErrors: true,
55			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
56			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
57			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
58			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
59			RunE: runRoot,
60		}
61		f := root.PersistentFlags()
62		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
63		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
64		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
65		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
66		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
67		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
68		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
69		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
70		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
72		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
74	
75		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
76		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
77		return root
78	}
79	
80	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
81	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
82		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
83	}
84	
85	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
86	func overrides() config.Overrides {
87		return config.Overrides{
88			Source:       flagFrom,
89			Target:       flagTo,
90			Engine:       flagEngine,
91			Provider:     flagProvider,
92			Model:        flagModel,
93			Tier:         flagTier,
94			Preset:       flagPreset,
95			Instructions: flagInstructions,
96			Pair:         flagPair,
97			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
98		}
99	}
100	
101	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
102	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
103	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
104	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
105		if len(args) > 0 {
106			return config.ModeCLI
107		}
108		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
109			return config.ModeCLI
110		}
111		return config.ModeTUI
112	}
113	
114	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
115		ctx := cmd.Context()
116	
117		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
118		if err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if created {
122			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
123		}
124	
125		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
126		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
127		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
128		pairWith := ""
129		if res.Pair {
130			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
131			pairWith = pwm.Code
132		}
133	
134		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
135			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
136		}
137		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
138		if err != nil {
139			return err
140		}
141	
142		st := openStore(cfg)
143		if st != nil {
144			defer st.Close()
145		}
146	
147		switch {
148		case len(args) > 0:
149			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
150			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
151			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions)
152			if err != nil {
153				return err
154			}
155			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
156			return nil
157	
158		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
159			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
160			if err != nil {
161				return err
162			}
163			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
164			if text == "" {
165				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
166			}
167			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
168			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions)
169			if err != nil {
170				return err
171			}
172			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
173			return nil
174	
175		default:
176			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
177		}
178	}
179	
180	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
181	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
182		if pair && away != "" {
183			return lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
184		}
185		return home
186	}
187	
188	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
189	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
190	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
191		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
192			return
193		}
194		stt, err := state.Load()
195		if err != nil || stt == nil {
196			return
197		}
198		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
199			res.Source = stt.Source
200		}
201		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
202			res.Target = stt.Target
203		}
204	}
205	
206	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
207	// suppressed with --no-history.
208	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
209		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
210			return nil
211		}
212		path := cfg.History.Path
213		if path == "" {
214			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
215		}
216		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
217		if err != nil {
218			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
219			return nil
220		}
221		return st
222	}
223	
224	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
225	//
226	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
227	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
228	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
229	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
230		if st != nil {
231			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
232		}
233		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
234			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
235		}
236	}
237	
238	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
239	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
240		src := source
241		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
242			src = res.DetectedSource
243		}
244		return store.Record{
245			SourceLang:   src,
246			TargetLang:   target,
247			Engine:       res.Engine,
248			Model:        res.Model,
249			Input:        input,
250			Output:       res.Translation,
251			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
252			Notes:        res.Notes,
253		}
254	}
255	
256	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
257	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
258		mode := "fixed"
259		if source == "auto" {
260			mode = "auto"
261		}
262		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
263			Source:     source,
264			Target:     target,
265			SourceMode: mode,
266			Engine:     engineName,
267		})
268	}
269	
270	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
271	// pair on exit.
272	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
273		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
274		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
275		modelProvider := ""
276		if res.Provider != nil {
277			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
278			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
279		}
280		p := tui.Params{
281			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
282			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
283			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
284			Store:         st,
285			Source:        source,
286			Target:        target,
287			Pair:          res.Pair,
288			PairWith:      pairWith,
289			Model:         res.Model,
290			Preset:        res.Preset,
291			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
292			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
293			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
294		}
295		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
296		if err != nil {
297			return err
298		}
299		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
300			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
301				s, t := fm.Pair()
302				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
303			}
304		}
305		return nil
306	}
307	
308	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
309	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
310	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
311		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
312		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
313		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
314			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
315		}
316		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
317			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
318		}
319		return sm.Code, tm.Code
320	}
321	
322	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
323	//
324	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
325	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
326	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
327	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
328		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
329		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
330	
331		req := engine.Request{
332			Text:   text,
333			Source: source,
334			Target: target,
335			Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
336			Stream: stream,
337			Preset: preset,
338			Extra:  instructions,
339		}
340		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
341		if err != nil {
342			return nil, err
343		}
344	
345		var onTok func(string)
346		if stream {
347			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
348		}
349		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
350		if err != nil {
351			if stream {
352				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
353			}
354			return nil, err
355		}
356	
357		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
358		// failed and why, and how to switch.
359		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
360			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
361		}
362		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
363			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
364		}
365	
366		switch {
367		case flagJSON:
368			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
369			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
370			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
371				return nil, err
372			}
373		case stream:
374			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
375		default:
376			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
377		}
378		return res, nil
379	}
380
```

 `./cmd/define.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		return nil
64	}
65	
66	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
67	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
68	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
69	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
70		cfg := res.Cfg
71		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
72			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
73		}
74		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
75			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
76		}
77		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
78	}
79	
80	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
81	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
82		d := res.Dictionary
83		if d == nil {
84			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
85				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
86			}
87			return res.Translation + "\n"
88		}
89		var b strings.Builder
90		head := d.Word
91		if d.Phonetic != "" {
92			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
93		}
94		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
95		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
96			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
97			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
98				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
99					break
100				}
101				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
102				if def.Example != "" {
103					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
104				}
105			}
106		}
107		return b.String()
108	}
