<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm reviewing my findings on all four points and need to synthesize the design decisions. I've discovered that the "Clear" shortcut already exists as Ctrl+U in the TUI, which resets the textarea and clears the result pane, but there's something incomplete about how it's currently working. I need to add a focus system to switch between input and output panes using Tab, then route keyboard navigation appropriately—when output is focused, forward keys like j/k/space/arrows to the viewport for scrolling, while being careful not to interfere with the app's Clear binding. I'll also add visual feedback by dimming the textarea border and highlighting the output box when it has focus. So when output is focused, I'll route keypresses to the viewport instead of the textarea, which means regular letters just get ignored since the viewport doesn't handle them anyway—that keeps the textarea from capturing input when it shouldn't. For the input scrolling, the textarea's built-in behavior already handles cursor movement and scrolling, and arrow keys plus page up/down will naturally move the cursor and scroll the view. The user's perception might be inverted since output only scrolls via mouse while input scrolls with cursor keys, but the real requirement is clear: both panes need keyboard scrolling with Tab to switch focus. For the input pane, arrow keys already handle scrolling through the textarea, though I could enhance PageUp/PageDown support if needed. For the output pane, I need to add keyboard scroll capability entirely—that's the missing piece. So the core deliverable is output keyboard scrolling plus focus switching.

When live mode is active and focus is on the output pane, typing gets blocked (which is correct). Switching back to the input pane resumes the live debounce as you edit. I could also make mouse clicks set focus—clicking the input box focuses input, clicking output focuses output—as a nice optional enhancement. For focus behavior, I'll keep it simple: Tab and optional mouse clicks change focus, but launching a translation doesn't auto-switch focus. That way if you're scrolling through a long result, you stay there, but you can easily return to input to keep typing.

Now looking at the default translation mode setup. The findings show that `translate <word>` uses the auto chain (LLM) by default, not pair or dictionary mode. The "dictionary with LLM fallback" is actually the smart-dict engine, which exists but is only wired into `translate define` and the TUI cycle. Pair is just a routing flag that's off by default. The `translate init` command configures engine, target, preset, live mode, and pair settings, but it doesn't handle smart-dict or dictionary-as-default options.

The user seems to be asking whether the default should be pair + dictionary + LLM fallback, and noting that init doesn't let them configure this. This is a real design question: should the default `translate <word>` behavior switch to smart-dict (dictionary-with-LLM-fallback) instead of plain LLM? That would be a significant behavior change—single words would hit the dictionary first, while sentences would fall back to LLM translation. For their zh-TW ↔ en use case with dictionary lookups, this might make sense, but it's worth clarifying whether that's the behavior they actually want.

The architecture challenge is that smart-dict only works with ModeDict, while the main path uses ModeTranslate. The cleanest solution would be to add "smartdict" as a selectable engine option in the config and engine resolution logic, so users can set it via `[general].engine = "smartdict"` or through `translate init`, making it available as a first-class engine choice alongside the others. Smart-dict's translate method would execute regardless of the mode declaration, so using it as a single engine would work functionally—it'd handle word-level dictionary lookups and fall back to the LLM for sentences. For the deliverables, I need to add "smartdict" as a configurable engine option, wire it into the initialization, and expose the pair + smart-dict combination. The key question is whether to let users opt into this setup during init or change the shipped defaults entirely.

The user's question seems to be asking two things: whether the CLI currently defaults to pair + dictionary-with-LLM-fallback (it doesn't), and whether init can configure this (it can't yet). There's also a real bug lurking here—when the default target is "en" and pair_with is also "en", the pair mode becomes a no-op since source and destination are identical. Init should validate that pair_with doesn't equal the target language to prevent this footgun.

For the user's specific case, they want target=zh-TW (their home language) and pair_with=en (the away language), so input in zh-TW translates to en, and anything else translates back to zh-TW. That's the configuration that works reliably.

The screenshot showed the bug because their actual target was probably the default en — with home=en and away=zh-TW, English text like "test" fails language detection and stays in en instead of switching. So fixing point 4 means both guiding users to the right target configuration and making the detection symmetric so it works regardless of which language they start with. The root issues are that detection is asymmetric (only tests the home language), short Latin text fails the confidence threshold, and there's the degenerate case where home equals away. I'm considering a deterministic heuristic fix that makes pair target detection symmetric for CJK languages.

For CJK-to-non-CJK pairs, I'd route based on script detection: if the input contains Han characters or other CJK scripts, send it to the non-CJK language; otherwise send it to the CJK language. This works reliably offline for zh⇄en and solves the "test" problem. For non-CJK pairs like en⇄es, fall back to whatlanggo or LLM. Alternatively, I could use an LLM-based approach where pair mode doesn't pre-decide the target — instead pass both languages to the LLM and ask it to detect which one the text is in, then translate to the other. This matches the user's expectation of natural language detection, though it means the target won't be known until after the LLM responds.

The script-based approach (Option A) cleanly solves the user's zh-TW⇄en case deterministically and offline while preserving the known-target architecture for history and other engines. The LLM approach (Option B) is more general but adds architectural complexity and doesn't help with google/dict pair modes. I think the right move is Option A as the primary solution with a guard against home==away and better whatlanggo handling for non-CJK pairs, optionally with an LLM fallback for ambiguous cases.

Now I'm detailing how Option A rewrites the PairTarget function to check if exactly one language is CJK, then route based on whether the text contains CJK characters—if home is CJK and text is CJK, return home; otherwise return away, and vice versa if away is CJK. For cases where neither or both are CJK, it falls back to detection logic.

I need to be careful here though—currently `IsChinese` only checks Han characters, which works fine for Chinese, but if I want to support Japanese and Korean too, I'd need to generalize to check for all CJK scripts. The user's request is specifically for Chinese though, so I should probably keep it focused on Han.

Actually, let me think about the routing logic more carefully. If one language is CJK and the other isn't, I can use that to determine which way to route. For the edge case where both are Chinese variants like zh-TW and zh-CN, they'd both be CJK, so it would fall back to text detection and default to home—which is fine to ignore for now.

The symmetric routing should work like this: if the text is CJK, send it to the CJK language; if it's not, send it to the non-CJK language. So with home=en and away=zh, the word "test" isn't CJK, so it returns home.

But wait, that's backwards from what I want. The semantics should be: if the input matches the home language, translate to away; if it's foreign, translate to home. So with home=en and input "test" (English), it should go to away=zh for translation. And with home=zh-TW and input "你好" (Chinese), it should go to away=en.

The key insight is that regardless of which language is home, a zh⇄en pair always produces the same result: Chinese input becomes English, and English input becomes Chinese. The routing just needs to detect whether the input is in the home language to decide which direction to translate.

 `./internal/state/state.go`

```text
1	// Package state persists small session state (the last language pair and source
2	// mode) so a bare `translate` resumes where the user left off.
3	package state
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
12	)
13	
14	// State is the persisted last-session snapshot.
15	type State struct {
16		Source     string    `json:"source"`                // "auto" or a code
17		Target     string    `json:"target"`                // code
18		SourceMode string    `json:"source_mode,omitempty"` // auto | fixed
19		Provider   string    `json:"provider,omitempty"`
20		Engine     string    `json:"engine,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt  time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	}
23	
24	// Load reads the state file, returning a zero State if it does not exist.
25	func Load() (*State, error) {
26		b, err := os.ReadFile(xdgpath.StateFile())
27		if err != nil {
28			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
29				return &State{}, nil
30			}
31			return nil, err
32		}
33		var s State
34		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err != nil {
35			return &State{}, nil // corrupt state is non-fatal
36		}
37		return &s, nil
38	}
39	
40	// Save writes the state atomically (temp file + rename).
41	func Save(s *State) error {
42		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
43			return err
44		}
45		s.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
46		b, err := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		p := xdgpath.StateFile()
51		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".state-*.json")
52		if err != nil {
53			return err
54		}
55		tmpName := tmp.Name()
56		defer os.Remove(tmpName)
57		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
58			tmp.Close()
59			return err
60		}
61		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
62			return err
63		}
64		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
65	}
66
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/xdgpath/paths.go`

```text
1	// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
2	//
3	// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
4	// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
5	// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
6	// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
7	// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
8	package xdgpath
9	
10	import (
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13	)
14	
15	// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
16	const app = "translate"
17	
18	// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
19	func base(env, def string) string {
20		if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
21			return v
22		}
23		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
24		if err != nil {
25			home = "."
26		}
27		return filepath.Join(home, def)
28	}
29	
30	// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
31	func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }
32	
33	// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
34	func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }
35	
36	// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
37	func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }
38	
39	// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
40	func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }
41	
42	// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
43	func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }
44	
45	// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
46	func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }
47	
48	// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
49	func EnsureDirs() error {
50		for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
51			if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
52				return err
53			}
54		}
55		return nil
56	}
57
```
</details></tool-use>

**History shows pair routing is already correct (English 'test' → target zh-TW), but the LLM echoed 'test' back instead of translating to '測試'. The concise prompt even has a 'return unchanged if source==target' escape hatch. How should I fix pair-mode translation?**

Options:
- **Pair-aware prompt + router fix** - In pair mode, tell the LLM both languages, have it detect the input and translate into the OTHER, and forbid echoing. Also make the deterministic router symmetric so the reversed (home=en) config works too. Most robust; matches 'natural detection'.
- **Anti-echo rule only** - Keep current deterministic target routing; just add a strong 'always translate, never return unchanged' rule to the prompt (and drop the source==target escape hatch). Smaller, more surgical change.

**Today a bare `translate <word>` uses LLM pair-translate. 'Dictionary with LLM fallback' (smart-dict) already exists but is only reachable via `translate define` or the TUI ^e cycle, and `translate init` doesn't expose it. What default do you want, and what should `init` configure?**

Options:
- **Keep LLM default; expose smart-dict in init** - `translate` stays LLM pair-translate (handles your multi-line paragraphs well). Add smart-dict as a selectable engine and add init options for it + clean pair setup. Least surprising.
- **Smart-auto combined default** - New default: single words route to dictionary (with LLM fallback), phrases route to LLM translate, all bidirectional. Matches your described ideal but is a bigger new-engine change.
- **Only improve the init wizard** - No engine/behavior change. Just make `translate init` cover missing options (smart-dict/define-default, validate pair_with≠target) and document that dict+LLM-fallback already works via `translate define`.


**Answer:** Pair-aware prompt + router fix, Smart-auto combined default

// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
// the engine to detect the source language.
type Request struct {
	Text    string
	Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
	Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
	Mode    Mode
	MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
	Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
	Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
	// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
	// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
	ModelProvider string
	Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
	Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
}

// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
type TranslateResult struct {

# Plan: TUI ergonomics + smart-auto default + pair-mode fix + debug mode

## Context

`translate` is a Go CLI/TUI (Cobra + Bubble Tea v2 under `charm.land/*`, TOML config,
shared `internal/engine` layer). Five issues, gathered from the user + a code/history audit:

1. **No discoverable "clear input" in the TUI.** A clear binding already exists (`ctrl+u`,
   `internal/tui/keys.go:34`, handled `internal/tui/update.go:233-242`) but is **absent from
   the footer help** (`internal/tui/view.go:128,156`), so it's effectively hidden.
2. **No focus switch / keyboard scroll.** The output `viewport` scrolls **only via mouse wheel**
   (`update.go:493-501`); no key ever reaches it (`handleKey` routes every unmatched key to the
   textarea, `update.go:257-258`). There is **no focus concept** between the input/output panes and
   no Tab handling. Input (a `textarea`) does scroll, but only by cursor movement in a 4-row box.
3. **Default mode isn't "pair + dictionary-with-LLM-fallback."** A bare `translate <word>` runs
   plain LLM translate via the auto chain. "Dictionary with LLM fallback" (`SmartDictEngine`,
   `internal/engine/smartdict.go`) already exists but is reachable only via `translate define` /
   the TUI `^e` cycle, and `translate init` never exposes it. **Decision: build a new "smart-auto"
   default** — single words → dictionary (with LLM fallback), phrases → LLM translate, bidirectional.
4. **Pair mode "not working ideally."** Root cause confirmed from the user's config + history
   (`~/.config/translate/config.toml`: `default_target='zh-TW' pair=true pair_with='en'`;
   `history.jsonl`): routing is **already correct** — English "test" was routed to `target_lang=zh-TW`
   — but the **LLM echoed** `test → test` instead of translating to `測試`. The concise prompt even
   hands the model a "return the text unchanged if source == target" escape hatch
   (`internal/engine/prompt.go:30-31`). A secondary latent bug: `lang.PairTarget`
   (`internal/lang/detect.go:36-44`) only tests the *home* language, so the reversed `home=en` config
   and short Latin input fall through incorrectly. **Decision: pair-aware LLM prompt (model detects &
   routes, never echoes) + make `PairTarget` script-symmetric.**
5. **No debug mode.** There's no way to see the intermediate decisions (resolved config, pair routing,
   word-vs-phrase classification, dict hit/miss, chain fallback) to diagnose issues. **Add one.**

Intended outcome: a TUI that's comfortable for long input (clear/focus/scroll), a `translate` default
that behaves like a smart bilingual dictionary+translator, reliable pair-mode translation that never
echoes, and a `--debug` trace to diagnose the rest.

---

## Changes

### A. TUI — surface the quick clear (point 1)  ·  `internal/tui/view.go`
- Keep `ctrl+u`'s existing behavior (resets textarea **and** result pane — `update.go:233-242`).
- Add `^u clear` to both footer help strings: normal mode `view.go:156` and dict mode `view.go:128`.

### B. TUI — focus switch + keyboard scroll for both panes (point 2)
- `internal/tui/model.go`: add a `focus` field (`focusInput`/`focusOutput`, iota; default input).
- `internal/tui/keys.go`: add `SwitchFocus` = `tab` (+ `shift+tab`), help `⇥ focus`.
- `internal/tui/update.go` `handleKey`:
  - On `SwitchFocus`: toggle `m.focus`; call `m.ta.Focus()` / `m.ta.Blur()` accordingly (the input
    border already keys off `ta.Focused()`, `view.go:24-27`).
  - Fallthrough (`update.go:257`): when `focusOutput`, forward the key to `m.vp.Update(msg)` instead of
    the textarea — this reuses the viewport's **dormant default keymap** (↑/↓, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, space, b,
    u/d half-page, g/G-ish). Exception: a printable rune while `focusOutput` **snaps focus back to input**
    and forwards the key to the textarea (so typing "just works"). When `focusInput`, keep today's path
    (arrows already scroll the textarea's internal viewport).
  - Global bindings (Enter/`^y`/`^e`/… matched earlier in the switch) keep working regardless of focus.
- `internal/tui/styles.go`: add `resultHi` (accent border, mirror `inputHi`).
  `internal/tui/view.go:32`: render the result box with `resultHi` when `focusOutput`.
- `view.go` footer: add `⇥ focus` to the help string.
- Optional nicety: `handleMouseClick` (`update.go:506`) sets focus to the clicked pane.
- Note: `tab` overrides the textarea's tab insertion — acceptable for a translate box.

### C. Smart-auto default engine (point 3)
- **New** `internal/engine/smartauto.go` — `SmartAutoEngine{ smart Engine; llm Engine; cfg }`,
  `Supports(ModeTranslate)=true`, streaming preserved.
  - `Translate`: classify the input with an `isLookup(text)` heuristic —
    *single word/term* → delegate to `SmartDictEngine` (dict → LLM fallback, `ModeDict`);
    *phrase/sentence* → delegate to the LLM (`ModeTranslate`) with the pair-aware prompt (D).
  - `isLookup`: trimmed, no internal whitespace; Latin → one alpha token (len ≤ ~32); CJK → contains
    Han and rune-length ≤ 4; anything with spaces/sentence punctuation → phrase. Emits a debug line (E).
  - Thread the pair target through so a single word still targets `zh-TW` (not bare `zh`) — reuse/relax
    `smartTarget` (`smartdict.go:131`) to honor an explicit request target.
  - Stamp a `Warning`/note indicating which path served (mirrors smart-dict's transparency rule).
- `internal/engine/engine.go`: reuse `TranslateResult.Dictionary` (already rendered by
  `view.go:renderResult`/`root.go`), so no rendering changes are needed for word results.
- `cmd/build.go`: `buildEngine` — add `case res.Engine == "smartauto"` → `smartAutoFromConfig(res)`
  (requires `res.Provider != nil`; otherwise fall back to the existing chain). Add
  `smartAutoFromConfig(res)` next to `smartDictFromConfig` (`build.go:108`).
  `buildEngineSet` (`build.go:120`): when the primary is `smartauto`, keep it at index 0 and retain
  google/dictionary/smart-dict in the `^e` cycle.
- `internal/config/config.go`: accept `engine = "smartauto"`; document it. (Leave built-in `Default()`
  engine as `auto`; `init` writes `smartauto` — existing configs are only changed on re-`init`.)
- `cmd/init.go`: add engine option **"Smart auto — dictionary for words, LLM for phrases,
  bidirectional (recommended)"** → writes `engine='smartauto'`; and **validate** `pair_with != target`
  when pair is enabled (see D's degenerate-default guard).

### D. Pair-aware prompt + symmetric router + anti-echo (point 4)
- `internal/engine/engine.go` `Request`: add `Pair bool`, `PairHome string`, `PairAway string`
  (the two pair languages; `Target` remains the routed best-guess).
- `internal/engine/prompt.go`:
  - New pair-aware system prompt used by `buildTranslatePrompt` when `req.Pair` and both langs set:
    *"You translate between {Home} and {Away}. Detect which of these two the text is in and translate it
    into the OTHER. Always translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, name, or
    loanword. Output only the translation."*
  - Harden the concise prompt (`prompt.go:30-31`): replace the blanket "return unchanged if
    source == target" with a rule that still forbids echoing a translatable word.
- Thread `Pair/PairHome/PairAway` into the `Request` from `cmd/root.go:oneShot` (`root.go:331`) and
  `internal/tui/update.go:launch` (`update.go:348`).
- `internal/lang/detect.go`: rewrite `PairTarget` to be **script-symmetric** — when exactly one pair
  member is CJK, route purely by "does the text contain CJK" (fixes the reversed `home=en` case and
  short Latin input); same-script pairs fall back to best-effort `inLang`. Add `isCJKLang(code)` +
  generalize `IsChinese`→`containsCJK` (Han + Hiragana/Katakana/Hangul). This drives the **display target
  + non-LLM (google/dict) engines**; the LLM now self-routes via the prompt.
- `internal/tui/update.go:194-196` (`^g` toggle): when enabling pair with an empty/target-colliding
  `pairWith`, pick a **distinct** away language (target≈en → `zh-TW`, else `en`).
- CLI + TUI: warn (via debug/stderr) when `pair` is on but `pair_with == target` (degenerate no-op).

### E. Debug mode (point 5)
- **New** `internal/debug/debug.go`: process-global gate + `Logf(format, args…)` writing to a
  destination `io.Writer`. `Enable(w)` sets it; no-op when disabled.
- Enable via: `--debug` persistent flag (`cmd/root.go`), `TRANSLATE_DEBUG=1` env, or config
  `[general].debug` (+ `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay in `internal/config`).
- Destination: **stderr** for the one-shot CLI; a **file** `~/.local/state/translate/debug.log`
  (`xdgpath.StateDir()`) for the TUI, since the alt-screen hides stderr.
- Emit trace lines at the decision points: config resolve + `applyLastPair` overrides
  (`root.go:126,190`); `PairTarget` (home/away/text→target + reason: script vs detect); `buildEngine`
  / `Chain` attempts + fallback reason (`internal/engine/chain.go`); smart-dict dict hit/miss/fuzzy
  (`smartdict.go:86`); smart-auto word/phrase classification (C); the LLM request (source/target/preset,
  full prompt only at higher verbosity); and the final serving engine/model.
- Stretch (optional): a TUI `^d` overlay showing the last request's decision trace inline.

---

## Files to touch (primary)
- TUI: `internal/tui/keys.go`, `internal/tui/update.go`, `internal/tui/view.go`,
  `internal/tui/model.go`, `internal/tui/styles.go`
- Engine: **new** `internal/engine/smartauto.go`, `internal/engine/prompt.go`,
  `internal/engine/engine.go`, `internal/engine/smartdict.go`, `internal/engine/chain.go`
- Routing/lang: `internal/lang/detect.go`
- CLI/config: `cmd/build.go`, `cmd/init.go`, `cmd/root.go`, `internal/config/config.go`
  (+ `resolve.go` if `debug` joins the overlay)
- Debug: **new** `internal/debug/debug.go`
- Reuse: `SmartDictEngine` (`smartdict.go`), `dictFromConfig`/`llmFromProvider` (`build.go`),
  `renderResult` dict path (`view.go:162`), `xdgpath.StateDir()` (`internal/xdgpath/paths.go:37`).

## Verification
1. `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` (add tests below).
2. **Pair / echo (point 4):** `TRANSLATE_DEBUG=1 translate "test"` → Chinese (`測試`), **not** "test";
   `translate "測試"` → English; a full sentence → LLM translate. Inspect the stderr trace.
3. **Smart-auto (point 3):** single word (`escalations`) → dictionary entry (ECDICT) with LLM fallback
   on a miss; multi-word phrase → LLM translate; both bidirectional.
4. **TUI:** run `translate`; type `test` → Chinese; `Tab` to focus the output box (accent border),
   scroll with `j/k`/`PgDn`; type a printable key → focus snaps back to input; `^u` clears input+result;
   footer shows `^u clear` and `⇥ focus`. Confirm `~/.local/state/translate/debug.log` fills when debug on.
5. **Unit tests:** `PairTarget` symmetric cases (zh-TW⇄en and en⇄zh-TW, short Latin, CJK);
   smart-auto `isLookup` classification (word vs phrase, CJK vs Latin); pair prompt contains both
   languages and the no-echo instruction (extend `internal/engine/*_test.go`, `internal/lang`).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-13 10:01:36Z)**_

I've written the plan. Here's the essence of what I found and propose:

**Key diagnosis (point 4):** Your pair-mode *routing* is actually correct — history shows English "test" was routed to `target=zh-TW`. The bug is that the **LLM echoed "test" back** instead of translating to "測試", encouraged by the concise prompt's "return unchanged if source==target" escape hatch. So the fix is prompt-side (pair-aware prompt that detects + routes + never echoes), plus making `PairTarget` symmetric so the reversed `home=en` config can't break either.

**The five pieces:**
1. **Clear input** — `^u` already clears; it's just missing from the footer. Surface it.
2. **Focus + scroll** — add `Tab` to switch input⇄output focus, route keyboard scroll to the output viewport (currently mouse-only), highlight the focused pane; typing snaps focus back to input.
3. **Smart-auto default** (your choice) — new engine: single words → dictionary+LLM-fallback, phrases → LLM translate, bidirectional; expose it in `translate init`.
4. **Pair fix** (your choice) — pair-aware prompt + symmetric router + anti-echo, threaded through CLI & TUI.
5. **Debug mode** (your add) — `--debug`/`TRANSLATE_DEBUG`/config gate, trace at every decision point (routing, word-vs-phrase, dict hit/miss, chain fallback), to stderr for CLI and a log file for the TUI.

Bonus: smart-auto also independently fixes the "test" echo for single words — "test" becomes a dictionary lookup rather than an LLM call.

1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	}
37	
38	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
39	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
40	type TranslateResult struct {
41		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
42		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
43		Target         string   `json:"target"`
44		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
45		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
46		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
47	
48		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
49		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
50		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
51	
52		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
53		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
54		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
55		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
56		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
57	
58		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
59		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
60		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
63		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
64		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
65		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
66		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
67		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
68	
69		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
70		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
71		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
72		// Transient: not serialized.
73		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
74	}
75	
76	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
77	type DictEntry struct {
78		Word      string    `json:"word"`
79		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
80		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
81		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
82	}
83	
84	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
85	type Meaning struct {
86		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
87		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
88		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
89		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
90	}
91	
92	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
93	type Definition struct {
94		Text    string `json:"definition"`
95		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
96	}
97	
98	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
99	type ChunkKind int
100	
101	const (
102		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
103		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
104		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
105		ChunkDone
106		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
107		ChunkError
108	)
109	
110	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
111	type Chunk struct {
112		Kind   ChunkKind
113		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
114		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
115		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
116	}
117	
118	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
119	//
120	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
121	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
122	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
123	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
124	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
125	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
126	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
127	type Engine interface {
128		Name() string
129		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
130		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
131		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
132		Supports(m Mode) bool
133	}
134	
135	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
136	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
137	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
138		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
139		if err != nil {
140			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
141		} else {
142			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
143		}
144		close(ch)
145		return ch
146	}
147	
148	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
149	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
150	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
151	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
152		for c := range ch {
153			switch c.Kind {
154			case ChunkToken:
155				if onToken != nil {
156					onToken(c.Text)
157				}
158			case ChunkDone:
159				return c.Result, nil
160			case ChunkError:
161				return nil, c.Err
162			}
163		}
164		return nil, ErrNoResult
165	}
166
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/engine/chain.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"sync"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	// Chain is the AUTO fallback router. It tries engines in configured order and
11	// fails over to the next one when the current engine is known-down or fails
12	// before producing any token. Once tokens have streamed, a mid-stream error is
13	// surfaced rather than restarting on another engine (which would garble output);
14	// the common "provider not running" case fails at connect time, pre-token, and
15	// switches cleanly.
16	type Chain struct {
17		engines []Engine
18		ttl     time.Duration
19	
20		mu    sync.Mutex
21		avail map[string]probeCache
22	}
23	
24	type probeCache struct {
25		ok bool
26		at time.Time
27	}
28	
29	// NewChain builds a chain from engines in priority order. ttl is how long a
30	// health verdict is cached (default 5s).
31	func NewChain(engines []Engine, ttl time.Duration) *Chain {
32		if ttl <= 0 {
33			ttl = 5 * time.Second
34		}
35		return &Chain{engines: engines, ttl: ttl, avail: map[string]probeCache{}}
36	}
37	
38	// Name reports the chain as the "auto" engine.
39	func (c *Chain) Name() string { return "auto" }
40	
41	// Supports reports true if any member supports the mode.
42	func (c *Chain) Supports(m Mode) bool {
43		for _, e := range c.engines {
44			if e.Supports(m) {
45				return true
46			}
47		}
48		return false
49	}
50	
51	// recentlyDown reports whether we have a fresh "down" verdict for e, so we can
52	// skip it without paying a probe on the hot path. Engines are otherwise tried
53	// optimistically (no pre-probe latency on the happy path).
54	func (c *Chain) recentlyDown(e Engine) bool {
55		c.mu.Lock()
56		defer c.mu.Unlock()
57		pc, ok := c.avail[e.Name()]
58		return ok && !pc.ok && time.Since(pc.at) < c.ttl
59	}
60	
61	func (c *Chain) mark(e Engine, ok bool) {
62		c.mu.Lock()
63		c.avail[e.Name()] = probeCache{ok: ok, at: time.Now()}
64		c.mu.Unlock()
65	}
66	
67	func (c *Chain) candidates(m Mode) []Engine {
68		var out []Engine
69		for _, e := range c.engines {
70			if e.Supports(m) {
71				out = append(out, e)
72			}
73		}
74		return out
75	}
76	
77	// Translate proxies the first working engine's stream. Failover happens only
78	// before the first token.
79	func (c *Chain) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
80		cands := c.candidates(req.Mode)
81		if len(cands) == 0 {
82			return nil, ErrNoEngineForMode
83		}
84		out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
85		go func() {
86			defer close(out)
87			var lastErr error
88			var warns []string // reasons earlier engines were skipped/failed
89			for _, e := range cands {
90				if ctx.Err() != nil { // request abandoned (e.g. user typed more)
91					out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
92					return
93				}
94				if c.recentlyDown(e) {
95					lastErr = fmt.Errorf("%s: recently unavailable", e.Name())
96					warns = append(warns, e.Name()+" skipped (recently unavailable)")
97					continue
98				}
99				sub, err := e.Translate(ctx, req)
100				if err != nil {
101					lastErr = err
102					warns = append(warns, err.Error()) // engine errors are already name-prefixed
103					c.mark(e, false)
104					continue
105				}
106	
107				streamed, failed := false, false
108				for ch := range sub {
109					switch ch.Kind {
110					case ChunkToken:
111						streamed = true
112						out <- ch
113					case ChunkDone:
114						c.mark(e, true)
115						if ch.Result != nil && len(warns) > 0 {
116							ch.Result.Warnings = append(ch.Result.Warnings, warns...)
117						}
118						out <- ch
119						return
120					case ChunkError:
121						lastErr = ch.Err
122						failed = true
123						// Marking deferred until after the loop: a cancelled request
124						// (live-mode typing) must NOT be treated as an engine failure.
125					}
126				}
127	
128				if failed {
129					if ctx.Err() != nil { // cancelled: don't mark down, don't fail over
130						out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
131						return
132					}
133					c.mark(e, false)
134					warns = append(warns, lastErr.Error()) // already name-prefixed
135					if streamed {                          // already showed partial output: surface, don't restart
136						out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: lastErr}
137						return
138					}
139					continue // clean pre-token failover
140				}
141			}
142			if lastErr == nil {
143				lastErr = ErrAllEnginesFailed
144			}
145			out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%w: %v", ErrAllEnginesFailed, lastErr)}
146		}()
147		return out, nil
148	}
149	
150	// Detect returns the first available engine's detection, else "".
151	func (c *Chain) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
152		for _, e := range c.engines {
153			if c.recentlyDown(e) {
154				continue
155			}
156			if code, err := e.Detect(ctx, text); err == nil && code != "" {
157				return code, nil
158			}
159		}
160		return "", nil
161	}
162	
163	// Available reports whether any member is currently reachable.
164	func (c *Chain) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
165		for _, e := range c.engines {
166			if e.Available(ctx) {
167				c.mark(e, true)
168				return true
169			}
170			c.mark(e, false)
171		}
172		return false
173	}
174
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
14	)
15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
23		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
24		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
25		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
26		History   History    `toml:"history"`
27	}
28	
29	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
30	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
31	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
32	type Overlay struct {
33		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
34		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
35		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
36		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
37		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
38		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
39		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
40		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
41		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
42		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
43		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
44		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// General holds behavior settings.
48	type General struct {
49		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
50		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
51		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
52		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
53		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
54		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
55		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
56		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | llm | google | dict
57		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
58		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
59		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
60		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
61		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
62		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
63	}
64	
65	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
66	type Chain struct {
67		Order []string `toml:"order"`
68	}
69	
70	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
71	type Provider struct {
72		Name      string `toml:"name"`
73		Type      string `toml:"type"` // openai | ollama | openrouter | litellm | generic
74		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
75		Model     string `toml:"model"`
76		ModelFast string `toml:"model_fast,omitempty"`
77		ModelMax  string `toml:"model_max,omitempty"`
78		APIKeyEnv string `toml:"api_key_env,omitempty"` // "" => no Authorization header
79	}
80	
81	// Google configures the free translate_a/single endpoint.
82	type Google struct {
83		Enabled   bool     `toml:"enabled"`
84		Endpoint  string   `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
85		ExtraDT   []string `toml:"extra_dt,omitempty"`
86		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
87		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
88	}
89	
90	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.
91	type Dict struct {
92		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`
93		Source   string `toml:"source"` // local (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT) | api (dictionaryapi.dev)
94		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
95		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
96		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`
97		Wordlist string `toml:"wordlist,omitempty"` // "" => /usr/share/dict/words if present
98	
99		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
100		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
101		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
102		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
103		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
104		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
105	}
106	
107	// History configures translation history storage.
108	type History struct {
109		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
110		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
111		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
112	}
113	
114	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
115	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
116	type SmartDict struct {
117		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
118		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
119		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
120		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
121		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
122		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
123	}
124	
125	// Recommended copilot-proxy model ids per tier. Claude models are served via the
126	// Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages); the LLM engine routes them there
127	// automatically (they 400 on /chat/completions).
128	const (
129		ModelDefault = "claude-sonnet-5"  // balanced
130		ModelFast    = "claude-haiku-4-5" // snappy — the out-of-box default tier
131		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
132	)
133	
134	// Default returns a config with sensible defaults, written on first run.
135	func Default() *Config {
136		return &Config{
137			General: General{
138				DefaultTarget:     "en",
139				DefaultSource:     "auto",
140				Pair:              false,
141				PairWith:          "en", // used when pair mode is enabled
142				RememberLastPair:  true,
143				LiveTranslate:     false, // off by default to avoid spamming LLM/API while typing
144				DebounceMs:        700,   // when live is on, wait longer before firing
145				Engine:            "auto",
146				Tier:              "fast", // haiku by default — snappy for short, quick translations

190				EcdictURL:    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv",
191				AutoDownload: false, // explicit `dict update` is the blessed path
192				APIFallback:  true,  // English lookups still work before ECDICT is installed
193			},
194			SmartDict: SmartDict{
195				CloseDistance: 1,            // distance-1 typos stay "did you mean"; farther → LLM
196				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences
197				DefineDefault: true,         // `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
198			},
199			History: History{
200				Enabled: true,
201				Backend: "jsonl",
202			},
203		}
204	}
205	
206	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
207	func Path() string {
208		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
209			return p
210		}
211		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
212	}
213	
214	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
215	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
216	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
217		p := Path()
218		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
219		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
220			cfg = Default()
221			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
222				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
223			}
224			return cfg, true, nil
225		}
226		if err != nil {
227			return nil, false, err
228		}
229		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
230		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
231			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
232		}
233		return cfg, false, nil
234	}
235	
236	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
237	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
238		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
239			return err
240		}
241		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
242		if err != nil {
243			return err
244		}
245		p := Path()
246		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
247		if err != nil {
248			return err
249		}
250		tmpName := tmp.Name()
251		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
252		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
253			tmp.Close()
254			return err
255		}
256		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
257			return err
258		}
259		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
260	}
261	
262	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
263	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
264		for i := range c.Providers {
265			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
266				return &c.Providers[i]
267			}
268		}
269		return nil
270	}
271	
272	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
273	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
274	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
275		switch tier {
276		case "fast":
277			if p.ModelFast != "" {
278				return p.ModelFast
279			}
280		case "max":
281			if p.ModelMax != "" {
282				return p.ModelMax
283			}
284		}
285		return p.Model
286	}
287
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/config/resolve.go`

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20	}
21	
22	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
23	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
24	type Mode int
25	
26	const (
27		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
28		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
29	)
30	
31	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
32	type Resolved struct {
33		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
34		Target       string
35		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
36		Provider     *Provider
37		Model        string
38		Tier         string
39		Preset       string
40		Instructions string
41		Pair         bool
42		PairWith     string
43		Stream       bool
44		Color        string
45	
46		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
47		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
48		LiveTranslate bool
49		DebounceMs    int
50	
51		Cfg *Config
52	}
53	
54	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
55	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
56	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
57	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
58	
59	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
60	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
61		switch mode {
62		case ModeCLI:
63			return c.CLI
64		case ModeTUI:
65			return c.TUI
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
71	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
72	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
73		if ov == nil {
74			return
75		}
76		if ov.Engine != nil {
77			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
78		}
79		if ov.Tier != nil {
80			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
81		}
82		if ov.Preset != nil {
83			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
84		}
85		if ov.Instructions != nil {
86			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
87		}
88		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
89			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
90		}
91		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
92			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
93		}
94		if ov.Pair != nil {
95			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
96		}
97		if ov.PairWith != nil {
98			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
99		}
100		if ov.Stream != nil {
101			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
102		}
103		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
104			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
105		}
106		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
107			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
112	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
113	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
114		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
115		// the on-disk config is untouched).
116		g := c.General
117		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
118		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
119	
120		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
121			if flag != "" {
122				return flag
123			}
124			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
125				return v
126			}
127			return cfgVal
128		}
129	
130		r := Resolved{
131			Cfg:           c,
132			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
133			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
134			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
135			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
136			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
137			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
138			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
139			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
140			Color:         g.Color,
141			Stream:        g.Stream,
142			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
143			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
144		}
145	
146		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
147		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
148		if provName == "" {
149			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
150				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
151			} else {
152				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
153			}
154		}
155		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
156	
157		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
158		// provider's tier model.
159		cfgModel := ""
160		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
161			cfgModel = *ov.Model
162		}
163		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
164		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
165			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
166		}
167		return r
168	}
169	
170	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
171	// else the first configured provider, else "".
172	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
173		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
174			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
175				return name
176			}
177		}
178		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
179			return c.Providers[0].Name
180		}
181		return ""
182	}
183
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
// keys that represent printable characters shifted or unshifted (like 'a',
// 'A', '1', '!', etc.).
type Key struct {
	// Text contains the actual characters received. This usually the same as
	// [Key.Code]. When [Key.Text] is non-empty, it indicates that the key
	// pressed represents printable character(s).
	Text string

	// Mod represents modifier keys, like [ModCtrl], [ModAlt], and so on.
	Mod KeyMod

	// Code represents the key pressed. This is usually a special key like
	// [KeyTab], [KeyEnter], [KeyF1], or a printable character like 'a'.
	Code rune

	// ShiftedCode is the actual, shifted key pressed by the user. For example,
	// if the user presses shift+a, or caps lock is on, [Key.ShiftedCode] will
	// be 'A' and [Key.Code] will be 'a'.
	//
	// In the case of non-latin keyboards, like Arabic, [Key.ShiftedCode] is the
	// unshifted key on the keyboard.
	//
	// This is only available with the Kitty Keyboard Protocol or the Windows
	// Console API.
	ShiftedCode rune

	// BaseCode is the key pressed according to the standard PC-101 key layout.
	// On international keyboards, this is the key that would be pressed if the
	// keyboard was set to US PC-101 layout.
	//
	// For example, if the user presses 'q' on a French AZERTY keyboard,
	// [Key.BaseCode] will be 'q'.
	//
	// This is only available with the Kitty Keyboard Protocol or the Windows
	// Console API.
	BaseCode rune

	// IsRepeat indicates whether the key is being held down and sending events
	// repeatedly.
	//
	// This is only available with the Kitty Keyboard Protocol or the Windows
	// Console API.
	IsRepeat bool
}

// String implements [fmt.Stringer] and is quite useful for matching key

```go
// Package debug provides an opt-in diagnostic log. When enabled it writes
// timestamped trace lines describing the intermediate decisions (config
// resolution, pair-mode routing, word-vs-phrase classification, dictionary
// hit/miss, chain fallback) so an unexpected translation can be diagnosed.
//
// It is a cheap no-op until Enable is called. The one-shot CLI enables it to
// stderr; the TUI enables it to a log file, because its alt-screen hides stderr.
package debug

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"sync"
	"time"
)

var (
	mu  sync.Mutex
	out io.Writer // nil => disabled
)

// Enable turns debug logging on, directing trace lines to w. Passing nil turns
// it back off. Safe to call once at startup.
func Enable(w io.Writer) {
	mu.Lock()
	defer mu.Unlock()
	out = w
}

// Enabled reports whether debug logging is currently on.
func Enabled() bool {
	mu.Lock()
	defer mu.Unlock()
	return out != nil
}

// Logf writes one trace line when enabled; otherwise it does nothing.
func Logf(format string, args ...any) {
	mu.Lock()
	defer mu.Unlock()
	if out == nil {
		return
	}
	ts := time.Now().Format("15:04:05.000")
	fmt.Fprintf(out, "%s translate[debug] "+format+"\n", append([]any{ts}, args...)...)
}

```

```go
package engine

import (
	"context"
	"strings"
	"unicode"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
)

// SmartAutoEngine is the "smart" default: it treats a single word/term as a
// dictionary lookup (delegating to the smart-dict engine, which itself falls back
// to the LLM on a miss) and a phrase or sentence as an LLM translation. It is
// bidirectional-friendly — the caller sets Pair/PairHome/PairAway and the routed
// Target, and both branches honor them.
//
// Because dictionary word lookups are direction-agnostic (script routes them to
// CC-CEDICT vs ECDICT) and phrase translation uses the pair-aware LLM prompt, a
// single word like "test" is answered from the dictionary instead of risking an
// LLM echo, and a full sentence is translated into the other pair language.
type SmartAutoEngine struct {
	smart Engine // dictionary + LLM fallback (serves ModeDict)
	llm   Engine // LLM translate, possibly a fallback chain (serves ModeTranslate)
}

// NewSmartAuto builds a smart-auto engine over a smart-dict engine (word lookups)
// and an LLM/translate engine (phrases).
func NewSmartAuto(smart, llm Engine) *SmartAutoEngine {
	return &SmartAutoEngine{smart: smart, llm: llm}
}

// Name returns "smart-auto".
func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Name() string { return "smart-auto" }

// Supports translate mode (the main-path request mode); it dispatches to a
// dictionary lookup internally when the input looks like a single word.
func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }

// Detect is not meaningful here (the LLM/dict sub-engines own detection).
func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
	return "", nil
}

// Available reports true when either branch can serve.
func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
	return e.smart.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
}

// Translate routes a single word/term to the dictionary (smart-dict) and a phrase
// to the LLM. Streaming is preserved by returning the chosen sub-engine's channel.
func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
	if isLookup(req.Text) {
		debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → dictionary lookup (smart-dict), target=%s", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target)
		r := req
		r.Mode = ModeDict
		return e.smart.Translate(ctx, r)
	}
	debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → LLM translate, target=%s pair=%v", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target, req.Pair)
	r := req
	r.Mode = ModeTranslate
	return e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
}

// isLookup reports whether text should be treated as a dictionary lookup (a
// single word or short term) rather than a phrase to translate. Latin/other: one
// token of letters (plus '-' / apostrophe), up to 32 runes. CJK: a short run of
// Han without spaces (a word or idiom, up to 4 characters). Anything with
// internal whitespace is a phrase.
func isLookup(text string) bool {
	t := strings.TrimSpace(text)
	if t == "" {
		return false
	}
	if strings.ContainsAny(t, " \t\n\r") {
		return false // multiple tokens → phrase
	}
	if lang.IsChinese(t) {
		return len([]rune(t)) <= 4
	}
	for _, r := range t {
		if !unicode.IsLetter(r) && r != '-' && r != '\'' {
			return false // digits/punctuation → not a plain word
		}
	}
	return len([]rune(t)) <= 32
}

// truncateText shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
func truncateText(s string) string {
	s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
	r := []rune(s)
	if len(r) > 40 {
		return string(r[:40]) + "…"
	}
	return s
}

```

```go
package lang

import "testing"

func TestPairTargetCJKNonCJK(t *testing.T) {
	cases := []struct {
		name             string
		home, away, text string
		want             string
	}{
		// home=zh-TW, away=en (the ideal config): symmetric and reliable.
		{"zh-home/english-in", "zh-TW", "en", "test", "zh-TW"},
		{"zh-home/chinese-in", "zh-TW", "en", "你好", "en"},
		// home=en, away=zh-TW (reversed): must still work — this is the case the old
		// asymmetric router broke on short Latin input.
		{"en-home/english-in", "en", "zh-TW", "test", "zh-TW"},
		{"en-home/chinese-in", "en", "zh-TW", "測試", "en"},
		// A mixed sentence with any Han routes to the non-CJK side.
		{"mixed-han", "zh-TW", "en", "hello 世界", "en"},
		// Degenerate configs collapse to home (a no-op) rather than misbehaving.
		{"same-lang", "en", "en", "test", "en"},
		{"empty-away", "zh-TW", "", "test", "zh-TW"},
	}
	for _, c := range cases {
		t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			if got := PairTarget(c.home, c.away, c.text); got != c.want {
				t.Errorf("PairTarget(%q,%q,%q) = %q, want %q", c.home, c.away, c.text, got, c.want)
			}
		})
	}
}

func TestContainsCJK(t *testing.T) {
	yes := []string{"你好", "テスト", "가", "abc世界"}
	no := []string{"test", "hello world", "café", "123"}
	for _, s := range yes {
		if !containsCJK(s) {
			t.Errorf("containsCJK(%q) = false, want true", s)
		}
	}
	for _, s := range no {
		if containsCJK(s) {
			t.Errorf("containsCJK(%q) = true, want false", s)
		}
	}
}

```

package engine

import "testing"

func TestIsLookup(t *testing.T) {
	cases := []struct {
		text string
		want bool
	}{
		// Single words/terms → dictionary lookup.
		{"test", true},
		{"escalations", true},
		{"run-time", true},
		{"don't", true},
		{"你好", true},   // 2 Han runes
		{"  spaced  ", true}, // trims to one token
		// Phrases / non-words → LLM translate.
		{"hello world", false},
		{"This is a sentence.", false},
		{"測試一下下下", false}, // >4 Han runes → treat as a phrase
		{"test123", false},   // digits are not a plain word
		{"", false},
	}
	for _, c := range cases {
		if got := isLookup(c.text); got != c.want {
			t.Errorf("isLookup(%q) = %v, want %v", c.text, got, c.want)
		}
	}
}

```go
package engine

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
)

func TestBuildTranslatePromptPairDirective(t *testing.T) {
	req := Request{
		Text:     "test",
		Source:   "auto",
		Target:   "zh-TW",
		Preset:   PresetConcise,
		Pair:     true,
		PairHome: "zh-TW",
		PairAway: "en",
	}
	sys, _ := buildTranslatePrompt(req)

	// The pair directive must name both languages so the model can detect + route.
	for _, code := range []string{"zh-TW", "en"} {
		if name := lang.Name(code); !strings.Contains(sys, name) {
			t.Errorf("pair system prompt missing language %q (%s)", code, name)
		}
	}
	// And it must forbid echoing the input unchanged (the "test → test" bug).
	if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(sys), "never return the text unchanged") {
		t.Errorf("pair system prompt is missing the no-echo instruction:\n%s", sys)
	}
}

func TestBuildTranslatePromptNoDirectiveWhenNotPair(t *testing.T) {
	req := Request{Text: "test", Source: "auto", Target: "zh-TW", Preset: PresetConcise}
	sys, _ := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
	if strings.Contains(sys, "Bidirectional mode") {
		t.Errorf("non-pair prompt should not include the pair directive:\n%s", sys)
	}
}

func TestConcisePromptForbidsEcho(t *testing.T) {
	// Even outside pair mode, the concise prompt must not tell the model to echo a
	// word in a different language.
	if strings.Contains(translateSystemPromptConcise, "return the text unchanged") &&
		!strings.Contains(translateSystemPromptConcise, "ALREADY in the target language") {
		t.Errorf("concise prompt still has a bare echo escape hatch:\n%s", translateSystemPromptConcise)
	}
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
PTY smoke test of the TUI

```bash
export TRANSLATE_CONFIG=/tmp/tr-verify/config.toml
export XDG_STATE_HOME=/tmp/tr-verify/state
export XDG_DATA_HOME=$HOME/.local/share
export TRANSLATE_DEBUG=1
rm -f /tmp/tr-verify/state/translate/debug.log
python3 - <<'PY'
import os, pty, time, sys, select

def main():
    pid, fd = pty.fork()
    if pid == 0:
        # child: run the TUI
        os.environ["TERM"] = "xterm-256color"
        os.execv("/tmp/translate", ["/tmp/translate"])
    # parent
    os.write(3, b"")  # noop to ensure fd import ok
    def send(b, wait=0.6):
        os.write(fd, b)
        time.sleep(wait)
    def drain():
        try:
            while True:
                r,_,_ = select.select([fd],[],[],0.2)
                if not r: break
                if not os.read(fd, 65536): break
        except OSError:
            pass
    time.sleep(1.2)      # window size + first render
    drain()
    send(b"test\r", 2.5) # type + translate (hits LLM)
    drain()
    send(b"\t", 0.5)     # Tab → focus output
    send(b"j", 0.3)      # scroll down
    send(b"k", 0.3)      # scroll up
    send(b"g", 0.3)      # top
    send(b"\t", 0.4)     # Tab → back to input
    send(b"\x15", 0.4)   # ^u clear
    send(b"\x03", 0.6)   # ^c quit
    drain()
    _, status = os.waitpid(pid, 0)
    print("EXIT_STATUS", os.waitstatus_to_exitcode(status))

main()
PY
echo "===== TUI debug.log ====="
cat /tmp/tr-verify/state/translate/debug.log 2>/dev/null || echo "(no debug log)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-13 10:21:03Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
26	## Usage
27	
28	| Command | What it does |
29	|---|---|
30	| `translate <text>` / `… \| translate` | One-shot translation (streams to a TTY; plain text when piped) |
31	| `translate` (no args, TTY) | Interactive TUI |
32	| `translate --to <lang> --from <lang>` | Language override; both are fuzzy (`chinees` → `zh`) |
33	| `translate --json` | Emit the full structured result |
34	| `translate define <word>` | Dictionary lookup (bilingual: zh↔en local, or English API) |
35	| `translate history` / `history search <q>` | Recent history / fuzzy search (`--tsv`, `--json`) |
36	| `translate init` | Interactive config wizard (probes providers) |
37	| `translate config path\|show` · `lang resolve <q>` | Introspection helpers |
38	
39	Flags: `--engine auto|<provider>|google`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--tier default|fast|max`, `--preset concise|contextual|dictionary`, `--instructions`, `--pair`/`--pair-with`, `--no-history`.
40	Env overrides: `TRANSLATE_TARGET`, `TRANSLATE_SOURCE`, `TRANSLATE_ENGINE`, `TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, `TRANSLATE_MODEL`, `TRANSLATE_CONFIG`.
41	Precedence: **flag > env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > default**.
42	
43	### Per-front-end defaults (`[cli]` / `[tui]`)
44	
45	The CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI share `[general]`, but
46	either can override any subset of it via an optional `[cli]` or `[tui]` table — e.g.
47	a snappy concise CLI and a richer, higher-tier TUI:
48	
49	```toml
50	[general]
51	preset = "contextual"
52	tier   = "fast"
53	
54	[cli]                 # one-shot / piped translation
55	preset = "concise"
56	
57	[tui]                 # interactive front-end
58	tier          = "max"
59	live_translate = true
60	```
61	
62	Only keys you list are overridden; the rest inherit `[general]`. Flags and env vars
63	still win over both.
64	
65	## TUI keys
66	
67	`↵` translate · live-debounce auto-translates ~700ms after you stop typing (off by default) ·
68	`^y` copy · `^l` toggle live · `^e` cycle engine · `^t` target language · `^p` model ·
69	`^o` style · `^g` toggle pair mode · `^r` history · `^u` clear · `⌥↵` newline · `^c`/`esc` quit.
70	
71	## Engines & fallback
72	
73	The `auto` engine tries `chain.order` in turn, failing over **before the first
74	token** (so a down provider switches cleanly; a mid-stream error surfaces rather
75	than restarting):
76	
77	- **copilot-proxy** — OpenAI-compatible at `http://localhost:4141/v1`, no API key.
78	  Default tier is **fast** (`claude-haiku-4-5`); `--tier default` → `claude-sonnet-5`,
79	  `--tier max` → `claude-opus-4-8`. Claude models are served only via the proxy's
80	  Anthropic Messages endpoint (`/v1/messages`) — the engine routes `claude-*` ids
81	  there automatically (they 400 on `/chat/completions`).
82	- **Ollama** — `http://localhost:11434/v1` (offline; `llama3.2:3b`).
83	- **Google** — free, keyless; also reports the detected source language.
84	- **openrouter** — configured but off the default chain; needs `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.
85	
86	> ⚠️ Using a GitHub Copilot subscription to back non-GitHub tools (copilot-proxy)
87	> may violate Copilot's Terms of Service. Reorder `chain.order` (drop `copilot`,
88	> lead with `ollama`/`google`) in the config if that matters to you.
89	
90	Config lives at `~/.config/translate/config.toml`, history at
91	`~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl`, last-pair state at
92	`~/.local/state/translate/state.json` (XDG-honored on macOS and Linux).
93	
94	## Dictionary mode
95	
96	`^e` cycles to the dictionary engine (or use `translate define <word>`). It is an
97	**offline bilingual** dictionary that routes by script — Chinese → CC-CEDICT
98	(zh→en), English → ECDICT (en→zh):
99	
100	```sh
101	translate dict update all      # one-time ~67 MB download/build into ~/.local/share/translate/dict
102	```
103	
104	Until then, English lookups fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (`[dict] api_fallback`),
105	and Chinese lookups prompt you to run the update. Misses show a ranked "did you
106	mean" list. Set `[dict] source = "api"` to use only dictionaryapi.dev.
107	
108	### Smart dictionary (`smart-dict`)
109	
110	A distinct engine that keeps the offline dictionary fast and deterministic, but on
111	a miss — no entry, or a fuzzy match too far off — falls back to the LLM for a gloss
112	plus example sentences (never silently: the result carries a `⚠ … defined via <provider> (LLM)`
113	warning). Close typos (edit distance ≤ `[smartdict] close_distance`, default 1) still
114	show "did you mean" without calling the LLM.
115	
116	```sh
117	translate define serendipity        # exact ECDICT entry
118	translate define zzzznotaword       # miss → LLM definition (⚠ warning on stderr)
119	translate define helllo             # distance-1 typo → "did you mean: hello, …"
120	translate define --plain <word>     # force the offline dictionary, no LLM fallback
121	```
122	
123	`translate define` uses smart-dict whenever an LLM provider is reachable
124	(`[smartdict] define_default`, `--smart`/`--plain` to force); in the TUI, `^e`
125	cycles to a `smart-dict` engine alongside the plain `dictionary`.
126	
127	```toml
128	[smartdict]
129	close_distance = 1            # en edit-distance ≤ this stays "did you mean"; beyond → LLM
130	preset         = "dictionary" # LLM output style for the fallback
131	define_default = true         # `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
132	```
133	
134	Data: **CC-CEDICT** (CC BY-SA 4.0, © Paul Andrew Denisowski / MDBG) and
135	**ECDICT** (MIT, © skywind3000).
136	
137	## Developer note — Charm v2 (`charm.land/*`)
138	
139	This project pins the **v2** Charm stack on the `charm.land/*` vanity module paths
140	(`charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `bubbles/v2`, `lipgloss/v2`, `huh/v2`) — **not**
141	`github.com/charmbracelet/*`. v1 code will not compile here. When editing the TUI:
142	`View()` returns `tea.View` (wrap strings with `tea.NewView`); keys arrive as
143	`tea.KeyPressMsg`; alt-screen is the declarative `View.AltScreen` field; window
144	size is requested via `tea.RequestWindowSize`.
145	
146	## Architecture
147	
148	The `internal/engine` and `internal/store` layers are pure Go (no TUI imports);
149	the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical engine values and
150	diverge only at presentation. A single monotonic `seq` counter in the TUI drives
151	debounce-collapse, in-flight cancellation, and stale-result dropping.
152	
153	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:readme-roadmap -->
154	<!-- Snippet for project's README.md, placed near other meta sections like
155	     "Customization" or "Contributing". -->
156	
157	## Roadmap & lessons learned
158	
159	Forward-looking work — long-term ideas, deferred items, things needing
160	evaluation — lives in [`TODO.md`](TODO.md), prioritised P1 → P3 with effort
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-13 10:22:24Z)**_

All five requests are implemented, verified end-to-end, and the tree is green (build + `go vet` + tests + `gofmt` all clean). Here's what changed and the evidence.

## 1 · Quick clear input (TUI)
`^u` already cleared the input — it just wasn't shown. It's now in the footer help (`^u clear`), still resets input **and** result, and now also returns focus to the input.

## 2 · Focus switch + keyboard scroll (TUI)
- **`⇥` (Tab)** toggles focus between the input and result boxes; the focused box gets an accent border.
- With the **result focused**, `↑/↓ j/k PgUp/PgDn g/G space` scroll it (previously mouse-wheel only). Typing any character **snaps focus back to input** and types there. A mouse click focuses whichever box it lands in.

## 3 · Smart-auto default (your choice: combined mode)
New `smart-auto` engine — **single word → dictionary** (with LLM fallback), **phrase → LLM translate**, bidirectional. `translate init` now offers it as the recommended engine. Verified:
- `test` → dict lookup → `n. 测试, 试验, …`
- `蘋果` → dict lookup → `Apple`
- `how are you doing today` → LLM → `今天過得怎麼樣？`
- `zzzqqxx` (dict miss) → LLM fallback with a `⚠ … defined via copilot (LLM)` warning

## 4 · Pair-mode fix (your choice: pair-aware prompt + router)
Root cause was an **LLM echo**, not bad routing. Fixed via a pair-aware prompt (detect-and-route, *never* echo) threaded through CLI + TUI, a hardened concise prompt, and a **script-symmetric `PairTarget`** (works even in the reversed `home=en` config). Verified against your real config:
- `test` → **測試** (was echoing "test") ✅
- `測試` → **Test** ✅
- Degenerate `pair_with == target` now warns; `^g` / `init` pick a distinct language.

## 5 · Debug mode (your mid-turn add)
`--debug` / `TRANSLATE_DEBUG=1` / `[general] debug = true` traces every decision (resolved settings, pair routing, word-vs-phrase, dict hit/miss, chain fallback). CLI → **stderr**; TUI → **`~/.local/state/translate/debug.log`** (alt-screen hides stderr). The traces above are real output.

TUI PTY smoke test: launches, translates, focus/scroll/clear/quit all work, exits cleanly, logs to file.

---

**New:** `internal/debug/`, `internal/engine/smartauto.go`, plus tests for `PairTarget`, `isLookup`, and the pair prompt. **Touched:** engine (`prompt`, `engine`, `smartdict`, `chain`), `lang/detect`, `config`, `cmd/{root,build,init}`, TUI (`model/keys/update/view/styles`), README.

One note: your live config still has `engine = 'auto'`, so the pair/echo fix and debug are active now, but to get the smart-auto default you'll want to run `translate init` (pick **Smart auto**) or set `engine = "smartauto"`. Want me to make that config edit for you?

---

Commit the changes

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
feat: smart-auto default, pair-mode echo fix, TUI focus/scroll, debug mode

Address five issues in the translate CLI/TUI:

1. TUI clear: surface the existing ^u binding in the footer; reset focus to
   input on clear.
2. TUI focus/scroll: add ⇥ (Tab) to switch focus between the input and result
   boxes (accent border on the focused one); route ↑/↓ j/k PgUp/PgDn g/G space
   to the result viewport when it has focus; typing snaps focus back to input;
   click focuses the box under the cursor.
3. Smart-auto engine (new default option): single words → dictionary (with LLM
   fallback), phrases → LLM translate, bidirectional. Wired into buildEngine and
   exposed in `translate init`.
4. Pair-mode fix: the bug was an LLM echo ("test" → "test"), not routing. Add a
   pair-aware translate prompt (detect-and-route, never echo), harden the concise
   prompt's echo escape hatch, and make lang.PairTarget script-symmetric so the
   reversed home=en config also routes short input correctly. Warn on a
   degenerate pair_with == target config; ^g / init pick a distinct language.
5. Debug mode: --debug / TRANSLATE_DEBUG / [general].debug traces resolved
   settings, pair routing, word-vs-phrase classification, dict hit/miss, and
   chain fallback. CLI → stderr; TUI → ~/.local/state/translate/debug.log.

Tests: PairTarget symmetry, isLookup classification, pair-prompt content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "=== result ==="; git --no-pager log --oneline -1; git status --short | head
```
