```text
126		cfg.General.DefaultTarget = target
127		cfg.General.LiveTranslate = live
128		cfg.General.Preset = preset
129		cfg.General.Instructions = strings.TrimSpace(instructions)
130		cfg.General.Pair = pair
131		cfg.General.PairWith = pairWith
132		// Refresh copilot model ids to the verified-working recommendations (repairs
133		// configs written before a model id changed / was un-served by the proxy).
134		if p := cfg.ProviderByName("copilot"); p != nil {
135			p.Model, p.ModelFast, p.ModelMax = config.ModelDefault, config.ModelFast, config.ModelMax
136		}
137		if err := config.Save(cfg); err != nil {
138			return err
139		}
140	
141		fmt.Printf("Saved %s\n  engine=%s  target=%s  live=%v\n", config.Path(), engineChoice, target, live)
142		if engineChoice == "copilot" && !copilotUp {
143			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: copilot-proxy is not reachable — start it with `copilot-proxy start`.")
144		}
145		if engineChoice == "ollama" && !ollamaUp {
146			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: Ollama is not reachable — start it and `ollama pull llama3.2:3b`.")
147		}
148		if openrouterKey {
149			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: OPENROUTER_API_KEY detected — the openrouter provider is available.")
150		}
151		return nil
152	}
153	
154	// targetLangOptions builds the language dropdown options (name + code).
155	func targetLangOptions() []huh.Option[string] {
156		ls := lang.List()
157		opts := make([]huh.Option[string], 0, len(ls))
158		for _, l := range ls {
159			opts = append(opts, huh.NewOption(fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", l.Name, l.Code), l.Code))
160		}
161		return opts
162	}
163	
164	func probeLabel(name string, up bool) string {
165		if up {
166			return name + "  ● up"
167		}
168		return name + "  ○ down"
169	}
170	
171	func probeProvider(cfg *config.Config, name string) bool {
172		p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
173		if p == nil {
174			return false
175		}
176		return probeURL(strings.TrimRight(p.BaseURL, "/")+"/models", 1500*time.Millisecond)
177	}
178	
179	func probeURL(url string, timeout time.Duration) bool {
180		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
181		defer cancel()
182		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
183		if err != nil {
184			return false
185		}
186		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
187		if err != nil {
188			return false
189		}
190		defer resp.Body.Close()
191		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
192	}
193
```

```text
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd

33	var (
34		flagTo            string
35		flagFrom          string
36		flagModel         string
37		flagProvider      string
38		flagEngine        string
39		flagTier          string
40		flagPreset        string
41		flagInstructions  string
42		flagPair          bool
43		flagPairWith      string
44		flagLearn         bool
45		flagBilingual     bool
46		flagBilingualMode string

48		flagNoHistory     bool
49		flagDebug         bool
50		flagSpeak         bool
51		flagSpeakLang     string
52	)
53	
54	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
55	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
56		root := &cobra.Command{
57			Use:   "translate [text...]",
58			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
59			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
60				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
61				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
62				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
63			Version:       buildVersion(),
64			SilenceUsage:  true,
65			SilenceErrors: true,
66			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
67			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
68			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
69			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
70			RunE: runRoot,
71		}
72		f := root.PersistentFlags()
73		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
74		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
75		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
76		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
77		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
78		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
79		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
80		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
81		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
82		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
83		f.BoolVar(&flagLearn, "learn", false, "learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)")
84		f.BoolVarP(&flagBilingual, "bilingual", "2", false, "bilingual pipe mode: keep original (with color) + translation beneath (stdin only)")
85		f.StringVar(&flagBilingualMode, "bilingual-mode", "doc", "bilingual strategy: doc (context-aware, one LLM call) | blocks (per-block)")
86		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
87		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
88		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
89		f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud (free TTS)")
90		f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
91	
92		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
93		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
94		return root
95	}
96	
97	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
98	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
99		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
100	}
101	
102	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
103	func overrides() config.Overrides {
104		return config.Overrides{
105			Source:       flagFrom,
106			Target:       flagTo,
107			Engine:       flagEngine,
108			Provider:     flagProvider,
109			Model:        flagModel,
110			Tier:         flagTier,
111			Preset:       flagPreset,
112			Instructions: flagInstructions,
113			Pair:         flagPair,
114			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
115			Learn:        flagLearn,
116			Debug:        flagDebug,
117		}
118	}
119	
120	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
121	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
122	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
123	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
124		if len(args) > 0 {
125			return config.ModeCLI
126		}
127		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
128			return config.ModeCLI
129		}
130		return config.ModeTUI
131	}
132	
133	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
134		ctx := cmd.Context()
135	
136		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
137		if err != nil {
138			return err
139		}
140		if created {
141			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
142		}
143	
144		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
145		if res.Debug {
146			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
147			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
148			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
149				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
150			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
151				debug.Enable(w)
152			}
153		}
154		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
155		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
156		pairWith := ""
157		if res.Pair {
158			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
159			pairWith = pwm.Code
160		}
161		// Learn mode is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language so
162		// direction routing has two sides to choose between.
163		if res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
164			pairWith = defaultAway(tgt)
165		}
166		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s learn=%v",
167			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith, res.Learn)
168		if res.Pair && !res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
169			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
170		}
171	
172		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
173			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
174		}
175		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
176		if err != nil {
177			return err
178		}
179	
180		// The one-shot CLI path routes a learn request through a bare LLM engine
181		// (bypassing smart-auto/dictionary); the TUI keeps its normal primary engine and
182		// toggles learn at runtime against Params.LearnEngine.
183		learnCLI := res.Learn && invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI
184		if learnCLI && res.Provider == nil {
185			return fmt.Errorf("learn mode requires an LLM provider; check %s", config.Path())
186		}
187		oneShotEng := eng
188		if learnCLI {
189			oneShotEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
190		}
191	
192		st := openStore(cfg)
193		if st != nil {
194			defer st.Close()
195		}
196	
197		switch {
198		case len(args) > 0:
199			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
200			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
201			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
202			if err != nil {
203				return err
204			}
205			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
206			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
207				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
208			}
209			return nil
210	
211		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
212			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
213			if err != nil {
214				return err
215			}
216			// Strip ANSI/SGR escapes so colored piped input (e.g. `tldr rg | translate`)
217			// never pollutes the prompt. The raw bytes are retained only for --bilingual,
218			// which needs the original styling for display.
219			text := strings.TrimSpace(bitext.Strip(string(b)))
220			if text == "" {
221				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
222			}
223			// Bilingual is a multi-block reading view; --json/--learn keep their own
224			// structured output and take precedence.
225			if flagBilingual && !flagJSON && !res.Learn {
226				// doc mode needs an LLM (structured JSON, empty Text confuses
227				// smartauto/chain routing) — build a bare LLM like learn mode; nil when
228				// no provider, in which case runBilingual falls back to per-block.
229				var docEng engine.Engine
230				if res.Provider != nil {
231					docEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
232				}
233				return runBilingual(ctx, oneShotEng, docEng, string(b), src, tgt, res.Instructions, flagBilingualMode)
234			}
235			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
236			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
237			if err != nil {
238				return err
239			}
240			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
241			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
242				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
243			}
244			return nil
245	
246		default:
247			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
248		}
249	}
250	
251	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
252	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
253		if pair && away != "" {
254			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
255			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
256			return t
257		}
258		return home
259	}
260	
261	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
262	func clip(s string, n int) string {
263		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
264		r := []rune(s)
265		if len(r) > n {
266			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
267		}
268		return s
269	}
270	
271	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
272	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
273	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
274		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
275			return nil
276		}
277		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
278			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
279		if err != nil {
280			return nil
281		}
282		return f
283	}
284	
285	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
286	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
287	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
288		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
289			return
290		}
291		stt, err := state.Load()
292		if err != nil || stt == nil {
293			return
294		}
295		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
296			res.Source = stt.Source
297			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
298		}
299		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
300			res.Target = stt.Target
301			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
302		}
303	}
304	
305	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
306	// suppressed with --no-history.
307	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
308		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
309			return nil
310		}
311		path := cfg.History.Path
312		if path == "" {
313			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
314		}
315		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
316		if err != nil {
317			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
318			return nil
319		}
320		return st
321	}
322	
323	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
324	//
325	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
326	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
327	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
328	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
329		if st != nil {
330			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
331		}
332		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
333			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
334		}
335	}
336	
337	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
338	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
339		src := source
340		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
341			src = res.DetectedSource
342		}
343		return store.Record{
344			SourceLang:   src,
345			TargetLang:   target,
346			Engine:       res.Engine,
347			Model:        res.Model,
348			Input:        input,
349			Output:       res.Translation,
350			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
351			Notes:        res.Notes,
352		}
353	}
354	
355	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
356	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
357		mode := "fixed"
358		if source == "auto" {
359			mode = "auto"
360		}
361		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
362			Source:     source,
363			Target:     target,
364			SourceMode: mode,
365			Engine:     engineName,
366		})
367	}
368	
369	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
370	// pair on exit.
371	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
372		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
373		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
374		modelProvider := ""
375		if res.Provider != nil {
376			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
377			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
378		}
379		// Learn mode (^n) runs against a bare LLM engine; nil when no provider is
380		// configured, in which case the toggle refuses.
381		var learnEng engine.Engine
382		if res.Provider != nil {
383			learnEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
384		}
385		p := tui.Params{
386			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
387			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
388			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
389			Store:         st,
390			Source:        source,
391			Target:        target,
392			Pair:          res.Pair,
393			PairWith:      pairWith,
394			Learn:         res.Learn,
395			LearnEngine:   learnEng,
396			Model:         res.Model,
397			Preset:        res.Preset,
398			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
399			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
400			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
401			Speaker:       tuiSpeaker(res.Cfg),
402			Foreign:       speakForeign(res.Cfg, pairWith),
403		}
404		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
405		if err != nil {
406			return err
407		}
408		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
409			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
410				s, t := fm.Pair()
411				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
412			}
413		}
414		return nil
415	}
416	
417	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
418	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
419	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
420		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
421		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
422		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
423			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
424		}
425		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
426			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
427		}
428		return sm.Code, tm.Code
429	}
430	
431	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
432	//
433	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
434	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
435	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
436	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
437		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
438		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn // learn output is structured (parsed at done)
439	
440		req := engine.Request{
441			Text:     text,
442			Source:   source,
443			Target:   target,
444			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
445			Stream:   stream,
446			Preset:   preset,
447			Extra:    instructions,
448			Pair:     pair,
449			PairHome: pairHome,
450			PairAway: pairAway,
451			Learn:    learn,
452		}
453		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
454		if err != nil {
455			return nil, err
456		}
457	
458		var onTok func(string)
459		if stream {
460			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
461		}
462		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
463		if err != nil {
464			if stream {
465				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
466			}
467			return nil, err
468		}
469	
470		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
471		// failed and why, and how to switch.
472		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
473			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
474		}
475		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
476			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
477		}
478	
479		switch {
480		case flagJSON:
481			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
482			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
483			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
484				return nil, err
485			}
486		case res.Learn != nil:
487			fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
488		case stream:
489			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
490		default:
491			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
492		}
493		return res, nil
494	}
495	
496	// runBilingual renders the "immersive" bilingual view for piped input: every
497	// original block is printed verbatim (ANSI/color intact) and a translation is shown
498	// beneath each prose block; indented command/code blocks are echoed untranslated.
499	// mode "doc" (default) translates the whole document in one context-aware call via
500	// docEng (a bare LLM); "blocks" (or a doc-mode fallback) translates each prose block
501	// in isolation via blockEng. Pair and learn routing are out of scope for this view.
502	func runBilingual(ctx context.Context, blockEng, docEng engine.Engine, raw, source, target, instructions, mode string) error {
503		blocks := bitext.Split(raw)
504		if len(blocks) == 0 {
505			return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
506		}
507	
508		var translations map[int]string
509		switch mode {
510		case "blocks":
511			translations = bilingualBlocks(ctx, blockEng, blocks, source, target, instructions)
512		default: // "doc"
513			if docEng != nil {
514				if t, ok := bilingualDoc(ctx, docEng, blocks, source, target, instructions); ok {
515					translations = t
516				}
517			}
518			if translations == nil { // no LLM provider, or the doc call failed/was unparseable
519				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "translate: bilingual: doc mode unavailable, using per-block")
520				translations = bilingualBlocks(ctx, blockEng, blocks, source, target, instructions)
521			}
522		}
523	
524		dim := dimFunc(term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())))
525		fmt.Print(bitext.Render(blocks, translations, dim))
526		return nil
527	}
528	
529	// bilingualBlocks translates each prose block in isolation (concurrent, bounded).
530	// The original strategy: works with any engine, but has no cross-block context.
531	func bilingualBlocks(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, blocks []bitext.Block, source, target, instructions string) map[int]string {
532		translations := make(map[int]string)
533		errs := make(map[int]error)
534		var mu sync.Mutex
535		var wg sync.WaitGroup
536		sem := make(chan struct{}, 5) // bound concurrent LLM calls
537	
538		for i, blk := range blocks {
539			if blk.Kind != bitext.Prose {
540				continue
541			}
542			wg.Add(1)
543			go func(i int, plain string) {
544				defer wg.Done()
545				sem <- struct{}{}
546				defer func() { <-sem }()
547	
548				// Force concise (Stream:false, Preset:"") so each block maps to exactly
549				// one translation — contextual/dictionary presets would reshape output.
550				r, err := translateOnce(ctx, eng, engine.Request{
551					Text:   plain,
552					Source: source,
553					Target: target,
554					Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
555					Extra:  instructions,
556				})
557				mu.Lock()
558				defer mu.Unlock()
559				if err != nil {
560					errs[i] = err
561					return
562				}
563				tr := strings.TrimSpace(r.Translation)
564				// Drop echoes (proper nouns / already-target text): nothing to add beneath.
565				if tr != "" && !strings.EqualFold(tr, strings.TrimSpace(plain)) {
566					translations[i] = tr
567				}
568			}(i, blk.Plain)
569		}
570		wg.Wait()
571	
572		// Never fail silently: report untranslated blocks (originals still printed).
573		if len(errs) > 0 {
574			var sample error
575			for _, e := range errs {
576				sample = e
577				break
578			}
579			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %d block(s) not translated (%v)\n", len(errs), sample)
580		}
581		return translations
582	}
583	
584	// bilingualDoc translates the whole document in ONE context-aware structured call:
585	// the model sees every block (prose + code as context) and returns a JSON map of
586	// prose-segment number → translation. Returns ok=false when structured output isn't
587	// available (no LLM, error, or unparseable/empty reply) so the caller falls back.
588	func bilingualDoc(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, blocks []bitext.Block, source, target, instructions string) (map[int]string, bool) {
589		var segs []engine.Segment
590		proseBlock := make(map[int]int) // prose-segment number (1-based) → block index
591		n := 0
592		for i, blk := range blocks {
593			switch blk.Kind {
594			case bitext.Prose:
595				n++
596				proseBlock[n] = i
597				segs = append(segs, engine.Segment{Text: blk.Plain})
598			case bitext.Code:
599				segs = append(segs, engine.Segment{Text: blk.Plain, Code: true})
600			}
601		}
602		if n == 0 {
603			return map[int]string{}, true // nothing to translate
604		}
605	
606		r, err := translateOnce(ctx, eng, engine.Request{
607			Bilingual: true,
608			Segments:  segs,
609			Source:    source,
610			Target:    target,
611			Mode:      engine.ModeTranslate,
612			Extra:     instructions,
613		})
614		if err != nil {
615			debug.Logf("bilingual doc: LLM call failed: %v", err)
616			return nil, false
617		}
618		if r == nil || len(r.Bilingual) == 0 {
619			warn := ""
620			if r != nil && len(r.Warnings) > 0 {
621				warn = r.Warnings[0]
622			}
623			debug.Logf("bilingual doc: empty/unparseable structured result (warnings: %q)", warn)
624			return nil, false
625		}
626	
627		translations := make(map[int]string)
628		for num, tr := range r.Bilingual {
629			bi, ok := proseBlock[num]
630			if !ok {
631				continue
632			}
633			t := strings.TrimSpace(tr)
634			if t != "" && !strings.EqualFold(t, strings.TrimSpace(blocks[bi].Plain)) {
635				translations[bi] = t
636			}
637		}
638		return translations, true
639	}
640	
641	// translateOnce runs a single non-streaming translation and returns the result,
642	// draining the engine channel with no token callback. It is the printing-free core
643	// shared by bilingual mode.
644	func translateOnce(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, req engine.Request) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
645		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
646		if err != nil {
647			return nil, err
648		}
649		return engine.Drain(ch, nil)
650	}
651	
652	// dimFunc returns the styler for bilingual translation lines. It dims (grey) only
653	// when stdout is a TTY and NO_COLOR is unset, so piping the output onward stays
654	// clean; otherwise it is the identity function. The original block's own ANSI is
655	// never touched — only our added translation lines are styled.
656	func dimFunc(stdoutTTY bool) func(string) string {
657		if !stdoutTTY || os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") != "" {
658			return func(s string) string { return s }
659		}
660		style := lg.NewStyle().Foreground(lg.Color("#6C6C6C"))
661		return func(s string) string { return style.Render(s) }
662	}
663	
664	// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
665	// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
666	// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
667	func defaultAway(home string) string {
668		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
669			return "zh-TW"
670		}
671		return "en"
672	}
673	
674	// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
675	func renderLearnCLI(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
676		l := res.Learn
677		if l == nil {
678			return res.Translation + "\n"
679		}
680		var b strings.Builder
681		if l.Direction == "correct" {
682			corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
683			if corrected == "" {
684				corrected = res.Translation
685			}
686			b.WriteString("✔ " + corrected + "\n")
687			if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
688				b.WriteString("  ↳ " + s + "\n")
689			}
690			for _, is := range l.Issues {
691				frag := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(is.Span+" → "+is.Fix, " → "), " → "))
692				expl := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation)
693				line := expl
694				switch {
695				case frag != "" && expl != "":
696					line = frag + ": " + expl
697				case frag != "":
698					line = frag
699				}
700				if line != "" {
701					b.WriteString("  ✎ " + line + "\n")
702				}
703			}
704		} else {
705			tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
706			if tr == "" {
707				tr = res.Translation
708			}
709			b.WriteString(tr + "\n")
710			for _, v := range l.Vocab {
711				head := "  • " + v.Term
712				if v.Pos != "" {
713					head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
714				}
715				if v.Phonetic != "" {
716					head += " " + v.Phonetic
717				}
718				if v.Meaning != "" {
719					head += " — " + v.Meaning
720				}
721				b.WriteString(head + "\n")
722			}
723			for _, ex := range l.Examples {
724				b.WriteString("  ✎ " + ex.Foreign + "\n")
725				if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
726					b.WriteString("    ↳ " + s + "\n")
727				}
728			}
729		}
730		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
731			b.WriteString("  ⓘ " + s + "\n")
732		}
733		return b.String()
734	}
735
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 14:14:59Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see the issue now—the config specifies `zh-TW` as the default target with `pair = true` and `pair_with = 'en'`, but the state file has `target: "en"` stored. When `applyLastPair` runs, it's overriding the target with the stored "en" value because there's no explicit flag or environment variable to prevent it.

This creates the problematic en⇄en pairing scenario that triggers the warning. The root cause is that `remember_last_pair` is persisting an old target value from a previous session (likely when the config was different or before the init command), and this persisted state is now winning over the configured default. The real design flaw is that in pair mode, the home language should be locked to the configured value and not overridable by the remembered state. The real problem is that `remember_last_pair` shouldn't override the configured home target when pair mode is active — the anchor language should come from config, not from saved state. Last-pair state makes sense for regular mode to remember what you were translating to, but in pair mode the target is determined by the anchor, not user preference, and the away language is computed dynamically based on input. So the safest approach is to skip the target override entirely when pair mode is active, since the home language is locked to the config default. The user's second question is asking whether their config file is actually updated to v4.0, and whether the config should record its generation version for validation purposes.

That's a solid feature idea — I should add a `version` field to the config schema, and on load, if the stored version is older than the current binary's schema version, prompt the user to run `translate init` to pick up new defaults and fields. Let me check the config package structure to see if this field already exists.

The third question asks about native-language mode, which refers to the **learn mode** (`--learn` flag and `^n` in the TUI) — the bidirectional language-learning feature. That mode already exists in the config as `learn = false`.

So I need to address three things: diagnose and fix the en⇄en bug (state clobbering the home target in pair mode), explain the existing learn mode, and implement the config version tracking feature.

Let me start by examining the config and state packages to understand how the version field should work and where the bug originates.
</details></think>

```text
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.

239	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
240	func Path() string {
241		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
242			return p
243		}
244		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
245	}
246	
247	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
248	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
249	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
250		p := Path()
251		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
252		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
253			cfg = Default()
254			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
255				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
256			}
257			return cfg, true, nil
258		}
259		if err != nil {
260			return nil, false, err
261		}
262		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
263		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
264			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
265		}
266		return cfg, false, nil
267	}
268	
269	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
270	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
271		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
272			return err
273		}
274		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
275		if err != nil {
276			return err
277		}
278		p := Path()
279		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
280		if err != nil {
281			return err
282		}
283		tmpName := tmp.Name()
284		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
285		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
286			tmp.Close()
287			return err
288		}
289		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
290			return err
291		}
292		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
293	}
294	
295	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
296	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
297		for i := range c.Providers {
298			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
299				return &c.Providers[i]
300			}
301		}
302		return nil
303	}
304	
305	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
306	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
307	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
308		switch tier {
309		case "fast":
310			if p.ModelFast != "" {
311				return p.ModelFast
312			}
313		case "max":
314			if p.ModelMax != "" {
315				return p.ModelMax
316			}
317		}
318		return p.Model
319	}
320
```

```text
28	const (

30		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end

38		Provider     *Provider

52		LiveTranslate bool

58	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
59	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
60	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
61	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
62

64	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
65		switch mode {
66		case ModeCLI:
67			return c.CLI
68		case ModeTUI:
69			return c.TUI
70		}
71		return nil
72	}

74	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
75	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
76	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
77		if ov == nil {
78			return
79		}
80		if ov.Engine != nil {
81			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
82		}
83		if ov.Tier != nil {
84			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
85		}
86		if ov.Preset != nil {
87			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
88		}
89		if ov.Instructions != nil {
90			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
91		}

93			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
94		}
95		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
96			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
97		}

99			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
100		}

102			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
103		}
104		if ov.Learn != nil {
105			g.Learn = *ov.Learn
106		}

108			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
109		}
110		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {

118		}

121	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
122	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
123	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
124		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
125		// the on-disk config is untouched).
126		g := c.General
127		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
128		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
129	
130		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
131			if flag != "" {
132				return flag
133			}
134			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
135				return v
136			}
137			return cfgVal
138		}
139	
140		r := Resolved{
141			Cfg:           c,
142			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
143			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
144			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
145			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
146			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
147			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
148			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
149			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
150			Learn:         o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "",
151			Color:         g.Color,
152			Stream:        g.Stream,
153			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
154			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
155			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
156		}
157	
158		// Learning mode is inherently bidirectional; it reuses pair's home/away
159		// direction routing, so enabling learn enables pair.
160		if r.Learn {
161			r.Pair = true
162		}
163	
164		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
165		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
166		if provName == "" {
167			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
168				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
169			} else {
170				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
171			}
172		}
173		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
174	
175		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
176		// provider's tier model.
177		cfgModel := ""
178		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
179			cfgModel = *ov.Model
180		}
181		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
182		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
183			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
184		}
185		return r
186	}
187	
188	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
189	// else the first configured provider, else "".
190	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
191		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
192			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
193				return name
194			}
195		}
196		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
197			return c.Providers[0].Name
198		}
199		return ""
200	}
```

```text
1	// Package state persists small session state (the last language pair and source
2	// mode) so a bare `translate` resumes where the user left off.
3	package state
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
12	)
13	
14	// State is the persisted last-session snapshot.
15	type State struct {
16		Source     string    `json:"source"`                // "auto" or a code
17		Target     string    `json:"target"`                // code
18		SourceMode string    `json:"source_mode,omitempty"` // auto | fixed
19		Provider   string    `json:"provider,omitempty"`
20		Engine     string    `json:"engine,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt  time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	}
23	
24	// Load reads the state file, returning a zero State if it does not exist.
25	func Load() (*State, error) {
26		b, err := os.ReadFile(xdgpath.StateFile())
27		if err != nil {
28			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
29				return &State{}, nil
30			}
31			return nil, err
32		}
33		var s State
34		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err != nil {
35			return &State{}, nil // corrupt state is non-fatal
36		}
37		return &s, nil
38	}
39	
40	// Save writes the state atomically (temp file + rename).
41	func Save(s *State) error {
42		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
43			return err
44		}
45		s.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
46		b, err := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		p := xdgpath.StateFile()
51		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".state-*.json")
52		if err != nil {
53			return err
54		}
55		tmpName := tmp.Name()
56		defer os.Remove(tmpName)
57		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
58			tmp.Close()
59			return err
60		}
61		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
62			return err
63		}
64		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
65	}
66
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/version.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"runtime/debug"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// version is injected at build time via
9	//
10	//	-ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.1"
11	//
12	// by packaged builds (the Homebrew formula, goreleaser, etc.). It overrides the
13	// build-info value below, which reports "(devel)" for a binary built from an
14	// extracted source tarball — no module version and no VCS metadata to read.
15	var version string
16	
17	// buildVersion reports a version string derived from the Go build info, so
18	// `translate --version` distinguishes a freshly-built dev binary from a stale
19	// installed one. For `go install <path>@<ver>` it shows the module version; for
20	// a local `go build`/`go install .` off a working tree it shows the VCS revision,
21	// a "+dirty" marker when the tree had uncommitted changes, and the build time.
22	// A build-time-injected version (above) takes precedence over both.
23	func buildVersion() string {
24		if version != "" {
25			return version
26		}
27		info, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()
28		if !ok {
29			return "(unknown; no build info)"
30		}
31		ver := info.Main.Version // e.g. "v0.1.0" or "(devel)"
32		var rev, when string
33		dirty := false
34		for _, s := range info.Settings {
35			switch s.Key {
36			case "vcs.revision":
37				rev = s.Value
38			case "vcs.time":
39				when = s.Value
40			case "vcs.modified":
41				dirty = s.Value == "true"
42			}
43		}
44		var b strings.Builder
45		b.WriteString(ver)
46		if rev != "" {
47			if len(rev) > 12 {
48				rev = rev[:12]
49			}
50			b.WriteString(" (" + rev)
51			if dirty {
52				b.WriteString("+dirty")
53			}
54			b.WriteString(")")
55		}
56		if when != "" {
57			b.WriteString(" commit " + when)
58		}
59		return b.String()
60	}
61
```
</details></tool-use>

But there's a deeper issue — if a user has an existing pre-versioning config with no schema key at all, seeding Default().Schema = SchemaVersion masks that absence, and we lose the ability to detect and warn them. The solution is to decouple the staleness check from the default-seeding: I'll unmarshal into a minimal probe struct that doesn't get default values, so an absent schema key stays zero. Root cause confirmed. Two distinct things are going on, and neither is what the warning suggests:

1. **The `en⇄en` bug is a state-vs-config conflict, not a stale config.** Your `config.toml` is actually correct (`default_target='zh-TW'`, `pair_with='en'`). But `~/.local/state/translate/state.json` has `"target": "en"` left over from an earlier session, and `remember_last_pair` makes that state **override** the config's home target. So at runtime home becomes `en`, away is `en` → pair collapses to a no-op. In pair mode the two languages are anchored by config; last-pair should never override them.

2. **Your idea about recording the config version is right** — there's currently no way to know a config is stale. I'll add a schema version + an "outdated, run `translate init`" reminder.

Let me set up tasks and implement.

 `./internal/xdgpath/paths.go`

```text
1	// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
2	//
3	// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
4	// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
5	// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
6	// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
7	// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
8	package xdgpath
9	
10	import (
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13	)
14	
15	// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
16	const app = "translate"
17	
18	// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
19	func base(env, def string) string {
20		if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
21			return v
22		}
23		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
24		if err != nil {
25			home = "."
26		}
27		return filepath.Join(home, def)
28	}
29	
30	// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
31	func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }
32	
33	// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
34	func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }
35	
36	// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
37	func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }
38	
39	// CacheDir returns the translate cache directory (e.g. ~/.cache/translate). Used
40	// for regenerable artifacts like TTS audio; it is intentionally NOT created by
41	// EnsureDirs (cache is optional and created lazily on first write).
42	func CacheDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CACHE_HOME", ".cache"), app) }
43	
44	// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
45	func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }
46	
47	// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
48	func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }
49	
50	// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
51	func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }
52	
53	// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
54	func EnsureDirs() error {
55		for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
56			if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
57				return err
58			}
59		}
60		return nil
61	}
62
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
package config

import "testing"

// TestResolveLearnImpliesPair: enabling learn (via flag) forces pair mode on, since
// learn reuses pair's home/away direction routing.
func TestResolveLearnImpliesPair(t *testing.T) {
	c := Default()
	c.General.Pair = false
	got := c.Resolve(Overrides{Learn: true}, ModeCLI)
	if !got.Learn {
		t.Error("Learn should resolve on when the flag is set")
	}
	if !got.Pair {
		t.Error("Learn should imply Pair")
	}
}

// TestResolveLearnOverlay: a per-front-end [tui] overlay can default learn on for the
// TUI while the CLI stays off, mirroring the other overlay knobs.
func TestResolveLearnOverlay(t *testing.T) {
	c := Default()
	c.TUI = &Overlay{Learn: boolptr(true)}
	if tui := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeTUI); !tui.Learn || !tui.Pair {
		t.Errorf("TUI overlay learn=true should resolve Learn && Pair, got learn=%v pair=%v", tui.Learn, tui.Pair)
	}
	if cli := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeCLI); cli.Learn {
		t.Error("CLI should not inherit the [tui] learn overlay")
	}
}
```

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package config

import (
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
)

// A config written before schema tracking has no `schema` key, so it reads as 0
// and is reported outdated; a config at the current schema is not.
func TestOutdated(t *testing.T) {
	// Pre-versioning config: no schema key. Load seeds Default() (schema 0) then
	// unmarshal leaves it 0, so the absent key correctly reads as outdated.
	old := Default()
	if err := toml.Unmarshal([]byte("[general]\ndefault_target = 'en'\n"), old); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if !old.Outdated() {
		t.Errorf("config with no schema key should be Outdated (schema=%d, want < %d)", old.Schema, SchemaVersion)
	}

	cur := Default()
	if err := toml.Unmarshal([]byte("schema = 1\n[general]\n"), cur); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if cur.Schema == SchemaVersion && cur.Outdated() {
		t.Errorf("config at current schema %d should not be Outdated", SchemaVersion)
	}
}

// Save stamps the current schema (and the app version when Generator is set), so a
// freshly saved config round-trips as up-to-date.
func TestSaveStampsVersion(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG", filepath.Join(dir, "config.toml"))
	t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", dir)
	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", dir)
	t.Setenv("XDG_STATE_HOME", dir)

	old := Generator
	Generator = "v9.9.9"
	defer func() { Generator = old }()

	cfg := Default() // Schema 0 in memory until Save stamps it
	if err := Save(cfg); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if cfg.Schema != SchemaVersion {
		t.Errorf("Save should stamp Schema=%d, got %d", SchemaVersion, cfg.Schema)
	}
	if cfg.Version != "v9.9.9" {
		t.Errorf("Save should stamp Version from Generator, got %q", cfg.Version)
	}

	loaded, created, err := Load()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if created {
		t.Error("Load should read the just-saved file, not report created")
	}
	if loaded.Outdated() {
		t.Errorf("a just-saved config should not be Outdated (schema=%d)", loaded.Schema)
	}
	if loaded.Version != "v9.9.9" {
		t.Errorf("Version should persist, got %q", loaded.Version)
	}
}

```

```text
1	
2	## Releasing & versioning
3	
4	`translate --version` is derived from Go build info (`cmd/version.go` →
5	`debug.ReadBuildInfo`), so **the version *is* the git tag** — there is no version
6	constant to edit. `go install …@latest` and the chezmoi/ansible pin both resolve
7	to a published **tag**, not to `main`; a commit on `main` does **not** reach an
8	installed binary until it is pushed **and** tagged.
9	
10	SemVer, pre-1.0 (`0.y.z`):
11	
12	- backward-compatible feature (new mode/flag, e.g. `--learn`) → bump **minor** (`0.1.0 → 0.2.0`)
13	- bug fix only → bump **patch** (`0.2.0 → 0.2.1`)
14	- breaking CLI/flag/config change → still a minor bump while `0.x`; call it out in the tag message
15	- cut **`v1.0.0`** only once the CLI/flags/config surface is considered stable
16	
17	To ship the current `main` to this machine via chezmoi:
18	
19	1. `go test ./...` green, working tree clean.
20	2. `git push origin main`
21	3. `git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m "<highlights>"` && `git push origin vX.Y.Z`
22	4. verify: `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@vX.Y.Z && translate --version` → `vX.Y.Z`
23	5. install on this machine (dotfiles repo): run `just upgrade-go` — it installs each go
24	   tool at `@latest` (= the new tag) into `~/.local/bin`. The `go_tools` pin in
25	   `dot_ansible/roles/go_tools/defaults/main.yml` is only the *fresh-install floor*
26	   (its header says **don't** bump it for upgrades); `chezmoi apply`/ansible is
27	   install-only and won't move an already-installed binary. Make sure no stale copy
28	   shadows `~/.local/bin` earlier on `PATH` (e.g. a hand-built one in `~/.dotfiles/bin`).
29	
30	There is no CHANGELOG; the annotated tag message is the release note. Keep commit
31	subjects in `feat(scope): …` / `fix(scope): …` form so `git log <prev-tag>..<tag>`
32	reads as a coherent changelog.
33	
34	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
35	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
36	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
37	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
38	
39	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
40	
41	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
42	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
43	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
44	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
45	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
```

## 1. 為什麼是 `en ⇄ en`?——不是 config 沒更新,是 **state 蓋掉了 config**

你的 `~/.config/translate/config.toml` 其實**完全正確**(`default_target='zh-TW'`、`pair=true`、`pair_with='en'`)。問題出在另一個檔案:

`~/.local/state/translate/state.json` 裡有一筆舊 session 留下的 `"target": "en"`。因為 `remember_last_pair = true`,`applyLastPair` 會拿這個 state 的 target **覆蓋掉 config 的 home**,於是 runtime 變成 home=`en`、away=`en` → pair 塌成 no-op,才跳「en equals target」警告。

**根因**:pair 模式的兩個語言是 config 錨定的(home=target、away=pair_with,方向逐句判斷),session state 不該覆蓋它。修法是在 `applyLastPair` 裡 pair/learn 模式直接跳過 override(`cmd/root.go`)。

用你「一模一樣的 config + 那筆 stale state」重現並驗證:

```
remember_last_pair: skipped (pair mode anchors languages from config)
resolved: ... target=zh-TW pair=true pair_with=en      ← home 正確回到 zh-TW
pair route: home=zh-TW away=en text="test"  → target=zh-TW   ✓ 英→中
pair route: home=zh-TW away=en text="測試"  → target=en      ✓ 中→英
```

不再有 en⇄en 警告。

## 2. config 記錄版本 → 有更新就提醒(你的提議,已實作)

- `config.toml` 頂端新增 `version`(寫入時的 app tag)+ `schema`(schema 世代)。

## 3. 母語模式——有,就是 learn/tutor 模式

之前實作的就是它:**bidirectional 語言學習模式**(母語→外語=教學給詞彙+例句;外語→母語=改文法+解釋,說明都用母語寫)。

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit and create annotated tag v0.5.0

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui
git commit -F - <<'EOF'
fix(pair): stop remembered last-pair from collapsing pair mode to a no-op

In pair (and learn) mode the home/target and away/pair_with languages are
anchored by config, with direction chosen per input. applyLastPair was
overriding the resolved target with the persisted session target, so if a
previous session ended on the away language the restored target made
home == away and pair mode silently became a no-op (the "en⇄en" warning,
despite a correct config). Skip the last-pair override entirely in pair mode.

Also add config schema versioning so a stale config is visible:
- config.toml records `version` (writing app tag) + `schema`
- Save stamps both; Config.Outdated() compares schema to SchemaVersion
- a bare `translate` run reminds the user to run `translate init` when outdated
- cmd.Execute sets config.Generator; docs note bumping SchemaVersion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "=== tag ==="
git tag -a v0.5.0 -m "v0.5.0 — fix pair-mode home anchoring (en⇄en no-op); config schema versioning + outdated reminder"
git --no-pager log --oneline -1; git tag --sort=-creatordate | head -3
```

The user is raising a good design question about the migration workflow — specifically whether we could auto-upgrade when init options haven't changed, or if we need to detect compatibility somehow, or if every binary upgrade requires re-running init. This gets at the core UX problem: currently each schema change forces a manual re-init, which is friction we might be able to reduce.

The key realization is that `Load()` already handles this elegantly — it seeds defaults then unmarshals over them, so new fields automatically get sensible values in memory. An old config file is missing the new keys and the schema stamp, but the user's actual choices are still there. The strategy is to distinguish additive schema bumps (new field with a default) from breaking changes (renamed or removed keys). For breaking changes, we'd need explicit handling, though since this is 0.x and changes have been additive so far, auto-migration should work fine. runRoot now re-saves the config (materializing new keys on disk, preserving
```bash
every existing value) and re-stamps the schema, printing a one-line upgrade
note — no `translate init` required. A breaking change would still need an
explicit migration before the re-save.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
# v0.5.0 was never pushed — move it to include auto-migration for one coherent release.
git tag -d v0.5.0
git tag -a v0.5.0 -m "v0.5.0 — fix pair-mode home anchoring (en⇄en no-op); config schema versioning with auto-migration"
echo "=== reinstall clean v0.5.0 ==="
go install -ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.5.0" .
~/.local/bin/translate --version
echo "=== commits ahead of origin/main (to be pushed) ==="
git --no-pager log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```

## Answer to your question
The formula was local-only until just now. I committed + pushed it to `daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap`, so **anyone can now install v0.5.0** via `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate` (or `brew upgrade translate`).

## Final state — all v0.5.0

| Artifact | Status |
|---|---|
| `translate` repo `main` + tag `v0.5.0` | ✅ pushed to GitHub (`ddcaa7e`) |
| Homebrew formula (tap repo) | ✅ v0.5.0 + new sha256, pushed (`46f77e9`) — brew-built & verified |
| Active `translate` on your PATH | `~/.local/bin/translate` → **v0.5.0** |
| Homebrew Cellar | **0.5.0** (shadowed by `~/.local/bin`, but same version) |

One harmless note: you have **two** v0.5.0 binaries — the `go install` one in `~/.local/bin` (wins on PATH) and the Homebrew one in the Cellar (shadowed). Both are v0.5.0 built from the same tag, so it doesn't matter which runs. If you'd ever rather let Homebrew's copy be the one invoked, just `rm ~/.local/bin/translate`.

## What shipped in v0.5.0
- **`fix(pair)`** — remembered last-pair no longer clobbers the config-anchored home, so pair mode stays `zh-TW⇄en` instead of collapsing to `en⇄en`.
- **`feat(config)`** — config now records `version` + `schema`; an outdated config is **auto-migrated** on next run (new fields get defaults, existing values preserved) instead of forcing a manual `translate init`.
